content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/adrien/.claude/skills/r-feature-batch

Implement a batch of **3-4 ROADMAP features** for a pqverse R package, **one feature at a
time** in the `dev` working tree, then finish with **one consolidation pass** that does every
global edit exactly once and **one Quarto demo** illustrating all the new features together.

This skill reproduces the workflow proven in MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000 (clean_pq NA-cleanup,
reshape_ggplot, phyloseq_to_MDT_excel, fastp, tool-presence checks).

## The discipline that keeps the diff clean

While implementing each feature, touch **only its own files** — `R/<slug>.R` and
`tests/testthat/test_<slug>.R`. Defer **every global file** to the single consolidation stage
at the end:

`NEWS.md`, `_pkgdown.yml`, `DESCRIPTION` (Suggests), `inst/WORDLIST`, `NAMESPACE`, `man/`.

Why batch them: NEWS bullets are alphabetised across the *whole* set at once; `document()`
and `air format` run once over the final tree (not N times); and a single `git diff
NAMESPACE man/` at the end cleanly reveals any carryover. Editing globals per-feature just
creates churn and re-sorting work.

## Arguments

`` may contain, in any order:

- A count (`3` or `4`) — how many features to implement. If absent, ask.
- `--filter "High/easy"` — priority/facility filter for candidate selection. Default:
  `High/easy` plus `Medium/easy`. Prefer items carrying a `(source: …)` prototype pointer.
- `--package <name>` — target package. If absent, read `Package:` from `DESCRIPTION` in the
  cwd. Resolves to `pqverse_pkg/<name>/` from the workspace root.

## Commits

**Leave everything uncommitted on `dev` for the developer.** The rule is "Don't commit, the
developer do." This skill never commits, branches, or merges.

---

## Stage 0 — Preconditions & context

1. Confirm cwd (or `--package` target) is an R package: `DESCRIPTION` present. Read `Package:`.
2. Locate the workspace root and `ROADMAP.md` (pqverse root, e.g.
   `/home/adrien/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/ROADMAP.md`).
3. Confirm the current branch is `dev` (or ask). Run `git status` so you know the starting
   diff and can keep the new work scoped.
4. Identify the demo target dir: `<workspace>/arround_MiscMetabar/`.

## Stage 1 — Select features

1. Read the target package's section of `ROADMAP.md`. Extract bullets matching the filter.
   **Strongly prefer items with a `(source: …)` pointer** — the prototype already exists, so
   they're lower-risk and faster. Open each pointer (often
   `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd:<lines>`) to confirm scope.
2. Present the candidates to the user with `AskUserQuestion` (`multiSelect: true`). Let them
   pick the 3-4 to build. Show the one-line description and facility tag per option.
3. For each chosen feature, record a small manifest:
   - `slug` — kebab-case, drives `R/<slug>.R` and `test_<slug>.R`.
   - target function name(s) (with `()`),
   - one-line description + source pointer,
   - the ROADMAP bullet text (to delete from ROADMAP on success — mirror the prior session).

## Stage 2 — Implement each feature (linear, one at a time)

For each selected feature, in turn:

1. **Read 2-3 existing functions** in `R/` to match conventions before writing anything.
2. Write `R/<slug>.R`:
   - Base pipe `|>` (never `%>%`); `function() {}` not `\()`; `{}` around every if/else body.
   - Full roxygen2 (`@param`, `@return`, `@export`, `@examples`, `@author`), matching the
     style of neighbouring functions.
   - Guard every Suggests-package call with `requireNamespace("<pkg>", quietly = TRUE)` +
     `cli::cli_abort(...)`. **Note** any new Suggests dependency for Stage 3 — do **not**
     edit `DESCRIPTION` yet.
   - CRAN constraints: primary example in `\donttest{}`, variants in `\dontrun{}`; cap any
     per-sample loop at 5 samples via
     `prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini)`
     (`data_fungi_mini` has 137 samples); external-tool examples in `\dontrun{}`.
3. Write `tests/testthat/test_<slug>.R`:
   - **Underscore** filename — repo convention. (r_development.md says `test-<name>.R` with a
     hyphen; that's wrong for this repo. Use underscore.)
   - `skip_on_cran()` as the **first line** if the whole file should skip on CRAN.
   - Minimal, few comments. Guard Suggests-dependent tests with
     `skip_if_not_installed("<pkg>")`.
4. **Validate this feature before moving on:**
   `NOT_CRAN=true Rscript -e "devtools::load_all('.'); testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test_<slug>.R')"`
   (never append `2>&1` to Rscript calls). Don't start the next feature with this one red.

Do **not** edit any global file in this stage — collect the per-feature notes (function
names, new Suggests deps, NEWS bullet text, `_pkgdown.yml` section, spelling terms) for
Stage 3.

## Stage 3 — Consolidate (ONE pass over the global files)

Once all features are implemented and individually green, do the global edits once, in order:

1. **NEWS.md** — add every bullet, then **re-sort the whole current-version list**
   alphabetically by function name (bullets without a function name go first). Present tense,
   function name in `backticks()` early in the bullet.
2. **_pkgdown.yml** — add each new topic to the section it belongs in.
3. **DESCRIPTION** — add all new Suggests deps in a single edit, keep the list sorted.
4. **inst/WORDLIST** — add all flagged terms; keep it `LC_ALL=C` sorted, en-US.
5. **`air format`** — run, then **scope the diff**: `air format .` reformats unrelated files.
   Revert incidental reformats so the diff stays feature-scoped
   (`git checkout -- <unrelated.R>`), or format only the changed files.
6. **`devtools::document()` ONCE** → regenerates `NAMESPACE` + `man/` with all new exports
   together.
7. **Carryover check** — `git diff NAMESPACE man/` and flag anything **not** attributable to
   the new features (e.g. a prior commit that never ran `document()`, like the
   `rarefy_even_depth_pq` carryover). Report it; don't silently bundle it.
8. **Spelling** — `Rscript -e "spelling::spell_check_package()"` once; fix real typos, add
   legitimate jargon to `inst/WORDLIST`.
9. **Full test suite** — once, `NOT_CRAN=true Rscript -e "devtools::test()"`. Swallow output;
   show `✓` if green, dump on failure.

## Stage 4 — One Quarto demo for all features

Write `<workspace>/arround_MiscMetabar/<name>_demo.qmd` covering **every** new feature in one
document:

- A setup chunk that `devtools::load_all()`s the dev package and loads
  `data(data_fungi_mini)`, capped to 5 samples for any per-sample work.
- One section per feature: a short prose explanation + a **runnable** chunk. Tool-dependent
  features (fastp etc.) use the dry-run / `cmd_is_run = FALSE` path or `is_*_installed()` so
  the chunk evaluates without the external binary.
- Close with `sessionInfo()`.
- **Verify the chunks actually evaluate** (render, or source each chunk) against the
  5-sample subset — a demo that only *looks* runnable is a regression waiting to happen.

## Stage 5 — ROADMAP cleanup & report

1. **ROADMAP.md** — delete the shipped bullets and any now-dead `(source:)` pointer rows. If a
   prototype in `en_cours_de_dev.qmd` is fully shipped, offer to remove that section too.
2. **Report** a table of features shipped (function · test · NEWS · pkgdown · man), the test
   result, spelling status, the demo path, and any `NAMESPACE`/`man/` **carryover** flagged
   in Stage 3.7. State plainly that the diff is left **uncommitted on `dev`** for the
   developer, and that **R CMD check was not run** — offer `/r-check`.

## Important rules

- **Never commit** — the developer commits.
- **No global-file edits while implementing a feature.** All of NEWS / _pkgdown / DESCRIPTION
  / WORDLIST / NAMESPACE / man happen once, in Stage 3.
- **When scope or design is ambiguous, ask the developer** — especially before writing a
  custom implementation of something a CRAN package (vegan, etc.) likely already provides.
- Tests are `test_<slug>.R` (underscore). Never append `2>&1` to Rscript Bash calls.
- Prefer items with an existing `(source:)` prototype; they're the low-risk path.

- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- r-audit: Run an external audit of a pqverse R package (MiscMetabar, tidypq, comparpq). Creates independent test data, runs all exported functions, and produces an R script and a TODO report of warnings, errors, and recommendations. Use this skill to stress-test a package with edge cases (NAs, empty samples, zero-count taxa, malformed trees).
- r-build: Build the pkgdown documentation website and the R package source tarball. Use this skill whenever the user wants to build their R package, rebuild the pkgdown site, regenerate documentation, build the README, lint the package.
- r-check: Style, document, and run R CMD check on the current R package, then propose fixes for any issues found. Use this skill whenever the user wants to check, validate, or verify their R package, run R CMD check, fix NOTEs/WARNINGs/ERRORs.
- r-feature-batch: Implement a batch of 3-4 ROADMAP features for a pqverse R package, one after another, then consolidate with full package hygiene (tests, NEWS bullets, _pkgdown.yml entries, regenerated man/, spelling, air format) and a single Quarto demo illustrating every new feature. Use this skill whenever the user wants to ship several roadmap features at once, batch-implement features, or do a feature sprint.
- r-pkg-bump-version: Bump an R package from a development version (X.Y.Z.9000) to a release version and finalize the NEWS.md heading. Use this skill whenever the user wants to bump the version of an R package for release, prepare DESCRIPTION + NEWS.md for CRAN, finalize the version before merging dev → master, or trigger Stage 8 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-merge-release: Merge an R package's dev branch into master, push, and bump dev to the next development cycle (X.Y.Z → X.Y.Z.9000). Use this skill after Stage 8 (version bump) when the user wants to finalize the release, merge dev into master, push to origin, start the next dev cycle, or trigger Stage 9 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-release: Full release pipeline for R packages: runs format, document, R CMD check (--as-cran), tests, optional coverage/audit/win-builder/rhub, CRAN extra checks, and builds the package. Supports --auto mode for autonomous runs that only stop for true ambiguity, with a resumable state file. Use this skill whenever the user wants to release a package, prepare for CRAN submission, run a pre-release check, do a full validation pipeline, or get a package ready for release. Also trigger when the user mentions 'release checklist', 'CRAN ready', 'prepare release'.
- r-stats: Perform a rigorous statistical analysis in R, guiding the user through test selection, assumption checking, analysis, effect sizes, and APA-compliant reporting. Use this skill whenever the user asks for a statistical test, wants to compare groups, check assumptions, compute effect sizes, run a t-test, ANOVA, chi-square, correlation, regression, or Bayesian analysis.
- r-test: Run the test suite and code coverage for the current R package, then propose new tests to improve coverage and fix failing tests. Use this skill whenever the user wants to run tests, check test coverage, fix failing tests, submit to Codecov.
- r-viz: Create publication-ready scientific figures with R and ggplot2. Use this skill whenever the user wants to create a plot, figure, chart, visualization, or graph for a manuscript, paper, or presentation. Covers multi-panel layouts, significance annotations, error bars, colorblind-safe palettes, and journal-specific formatting.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- posit-dev:critical-code-reviewer: Conduct rigorous, adversarial code reviews with zero tolerance for mediocrity. Use when users ask to "critically review" my code or a PR, "critique my code", "find issues in my code", or "what's wrong with this code". Identifies security holes, lazy patterns, edge case failures, and bad practices across Python, R, JavaScript/TypeScript, SQL, and front-end code. Scrutinizes error handling, type safety, performance, accessibility, and code quality. Provides structured feedback with severity tiers (Blocking, Required, Suggestions) and specific, actionable recommendations.
- posit-dev:describe-design: Research a codebase and create architectural documentation describing how features or systems work. Use when the user asks to: (1) Document how a feature works, (2) Create an architecture overview, (3) Explain code structure for onboarding or knowledge transfer, (4) Research and describe a system's design. Produces markdown documents with Mermaid diagrams and stable code references suitable for humans and AI agents.
- open-source:release-post: Create professional package release blog posts following Tidyverse or Shiny blog conventions. Use when the user needs to: (1) Write a release announcement blog post for an R or Python package for tidyverse.org or shiny.posit.co, (2) Transform NEWS/changelog content into blog format, (3) Generate acknowledgments sections with contributor lists, (4) Format posts following specific blog platform requirements. Supports both Tidyverse (hugodown) and Shiny (Quarto) blog formats with automated contributor fetching and comprehensive style guidance.
- open-source:create-release-checklist: Create a release checklist and GitHub issue for an R package. Use when the user asks to "create a release checklist" or "start a release" for an R package.
- r-lib:testing-r-packages: Best practices for writing R package tests using testthat version 3+. Use when writing, organizing, or improving tests for R packages. Covers test structure, expectations, fixtures, snapshots, mocking, and modern testthat 3 patterns including self-sufficient tests, proper cleanup with withr, and snapshot testing.
- r-lib:cli: Comprehensive R package for command-line interface styling, semantic messaging, and user communication. Use this skill when working with R code that needs to: (1) Format console output with inline markup and colors, (2) Display errors, warnings, or messages with cli_abort/cli_warn/cli_inform, (3) Show progress indicators for long-running operations, (4) Create semantic CLI elements (headers, lists, alerts, code blocks), (5) Apply themes and customize output styling, (6) Handle pluralization in user-facing text, (7) Work with ANSI strings, hyperlinks, or custom containers. Also use when migrating from base R message/warning/stop, debugging cli code, or improving existing cli usage.
- r-lib:cran-extrachecks: Prepare R packages for CRAN submission by checking for common ad-hoc requirements not caught by devtools::check(). Use when: (1) Preparing a package for first CRAN release, (2) Preparing a package update for CRAN resubmission, (3) Reviewing a package to ensure CRAN compliance, (4) Responding to CRAN reviewer feedback. Covers documentation requirements, DESCRIPTION field standards, URL validation, examples, and administrative requirements.
- r-lib:lifecycle: Guidance for managing R package lifecycle according to tidyverse principles using the lifecycle package. Use when: (1) Setting up lifecycle infrastructure in a package, (2) Deprecating functions or arguments, (3) Renaming functions or arguments, (4) Superseding functions, (5) Marking functions as experimental, (6) Understanding lifecycle stages (stable, experimental, deprecated, superseded), or (7) Writing deprecation helpers for complex scenarios.
- r-lib:r-package-development: R package development with devtools, testthat, and roxygen2. Use when the user is working on an R package, running tests, writing documentation, or building package infrastructure.
- r-lib:r-cli-app: Build command-line apps in R using the Rapp package. Use when creating a CLI tool in R, adding argument parsing to an R script, turning an R script into a command-line app, shipping CLIs in an R package, or using Rapp (the alternative Rscript front-end). Also use for shebang scripts, exec/ directory in R packages, or subcommand-based R tools.
- r-lib:mirai: Help users write correct R code for async, parallel, and distributed computing using mirai. Use when users need to: run R code asynchronously or in parallel, write mirai code with correct dependency passing, set up local or remote parallel workers, convert code from future or parallel, use parallel map operations, integrate async tasks with Shiny or promises, or configure cluster/HPC computing.
- r-lib:alt-text: Generate and improve accessible alt text for data visualizations and images in R packages and Quarto documents. Use when the user wants to add, improve, or audit alt text for figures in a pkgdown site or .qmd files. Activate for requests that mention fig-alt, fig.alt, figure descriptions, or alt text in the context of an R package or Quarto document.
- quarto:brand-yml: Create and use brand.yml files for consistent branding across Shiny apps and Quarto documents. Covers: (1) Creating new _brand.yml files, (2) Applying to Shiny (R and Python), (3) Using in Quarto, (4) Modifying existing files, and (5) Troubleshooting. Includes complete specifications and integration guides.
- quarto:quarto-authoring: Use when the user is explicitly working with Quarto, .qmd files, _quarto.yml, Quarto projects, or Quarto features such as callouts, cross-references, citations, Mermaid diagrams, extensions, websites, books, presentations, and reports. Also use for explicit migration from or comparison with R Markdown, bookdown, blogdown, xaringan, distill, or Jupyter notebooks to Quarto. Do not use for general R Markdown or related-format questions unless Quarto or migration to Quarto is explicitly mentioned.
- quarto:alt-text: Generate and improve accessible alt text for data visualizations and images in R packages and Quarto documents. Use when the user wants to add, improve, or audit alt text for figures in a pkgdown site or .qmd files. Activate for requests that mention fig-alt, fig.alt, figure descriptions, or alt text in the context of an R package or Quarto document.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# pqverse ROADMAP
2	
3	## How to read this file
4	
5	Each item is tagged with a **priority** and a **facility** level:
6	
7	| Priority | Meaning |
8	|---|---|
9	| **Critical** | Release-blocking; breaks documented workflows |
10	| **High** | Next-release target; prototype or clear design already exists |
11	| **Medium** | Planned; requires design work or new dependency |
12	| **Low** | Nice-to-have, deferred, or long-term vision |
13	
14	| Facility | Meaning |
15	|---|---|
16	| **easy** | Self-contained, < ~150 LOC, no new dependency |
17	| **moderate** | A few functions + tests, new CRAN dependency, or dataset bundling |
18	| **hard** | New S4/S7 class, cross-package change, upstream permission needed, or non-CRAN dependency |
19	
20	Format: `**[Priority / facility]**` — description. `(source: path[:lines])`
21	
22	> **Open decision** (see bottom of file): Windows support posture.
23	
24	---
25	
26	# MiscMetabar
27	
28	## Architecture
29	
30	- **[moderate / hard]** New `extended_phyloseq` S4 class with a `tax_data` data.frame slot supporting numeric and list columns for per-taxon traits (solves the two limitations of `tax_table`: no numeric columns, no list columns). Or just a tiny function that transform a tax_table into a data.frame with numeric and list columns. 
31	
32	## Data I/O
33	
34	- **[Medium / easy]** `vs_cluster_subseq()` — expose `--query_cov` and `--target_cov` as direct arguments (currently only settable via `vsearch_options`). may be we can add this to MiscMetabar::postcluster_pq as well.
35	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:52–62)
36	
37	- **[Medium / easy]** Align `write.csv` output to the GBIF MDT CSV template format (distinct from the Excel exporter already planned).
38	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/#fitting-the-data-into-a-template)
39	
40	## Analysis / Statistics
41	
42	- **[High / hard]** : Continue to implement bootstrapping (functions with rarperm in their name) by looking at https://f1000research.com/articles/14-87/v1 . May be we should create a new package for this in the pqverse. Is it possible to use one master function for all bootstrapping functions? See https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/msphere.00354-23 for a theoretical overview of bootstrapping. Note that I will prefer use bootstrapping word in place of rarefaction because it is often confused with rarefy. rarefy_even_depth_pq is not rarefaction (=bootstrapping) but rarefying (https://forum.qiime2.org/t/rarefaction-vs-rarefying-and-when-to-use-them-in-your-analysis-pipeline/33251). Note that bootstrapping (rarefaction) use rarefying methodology.
43	
44	- **[High / moderate]** Incorporate ecodive R package (https://github.com/cmmr/ecodive) to compute beta diversity metrics, in particular robust Aitchison distances (https://cmmr.github.io/ecodive/reference/robust_aitchison.html). It must be far more quick than vegan implementations. First, create a short benchmark to compare the two implementations. Second, if the benchmark is satisfying list the functions that used betadiversity metrics and third- implement the ecodive functions in the `diversity_pq()` wrapper.
45	
46	
47	- **[High / moderate]** TINA and PINA computation via `tina_pq()` and `pina_pq()`. We must use two repos (https://github.com/defleury/Schmidt_et_al_2016_community_similarity/blob/8ef50591278925a2384324d4a56b0b633dd91104/functions.community_similarity.R for function community.similarity.corr.par and https://github.com/trtcrd/Bench_sedaDNA/blob/53962761cf890a8e3f817f8371b27e2977410c72/Collect_stats_synthetic_datasets_clusters.R especially lines 583 to 601) and two papers (https://doi.org/10.1038/ismej.2016.139, 10.1093/ismeco/ycaf230).
48	
49	- **[High / moderate]** `aldex_pq()` wrapper with three test modes (kw, t, corr) — full prototype exists; inspired by EasyMicrobiomeR. Add as standalone function and vignette.
50	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:83–203)
51	
52	- **[Medium / moderate]** `check_distance()` adapted from Betadiag — checks distance matrix validity before PCoA / PERMANOVA / MiRKAT. Add `check_dist = TRUE` default to all distance-computing functions with `cli::warn()` feedback.

416	- **bioinfo.starter** — `targets`-based metabarcoding pipeline template. Located at `arround_MiscMetabar/bioinfo.starter/`. Remaining work: fill `_targets.R` primer/refseq placeholders, complete `02_alpha_diversity.qmd` / `03_beta_diversity.qmd` factor-of-interest params, populate `NEWS.md`, ship Docker image.
417	
418	- **docker_starter** and **starter_folder** rest on the pqverse package
419	
420	---
421	
422	# Cross-cutting concerns
423	
424	- **Hill diversity convention** — all new Hill-based functions must use `divent::div_hill()` with `estimator = "UnveilJ"` as default (migration completed in MiscMetabar 0.15.1 and comparpq 0.2.1; deprecate `scales`/`hill_scales` params in favour of `q`).
425	
426	- **Windows platform** — 13+ MiscMetabar functions are documented as non-working on Windows (`build_phytree_pq`, `cutadapt_remove_primers`, `krona`, `venn_pq`, etc.). Consider improving Docker workflow documentation as the primary mitigation.
427	
428	- **Pending external contacts:**
429	  - **Mahendra Mariadassou** — permission to port ternary/diamond plot implementation into MiscMetabar.
430	  - **Éric Marcon** — discuss `divent`-based Hill accumulation and singleton handling approach.
431	
432	- **Dataset licensing** — before bundling FungalTraits, TAXREFv18, or BDC_18 into any `inst/extdata`, verify that the respective licenses permit redistribution inside an R package.
433	
434	---
435	
436	# CRAN Submission Roadmap
437	
438	> Goal: submit every pqverse package to CRAN, in dependency-first order.
439	
440	## Submission order
441	
442	The order is dictated by the dependency graph. Each package must be on CRAN before downstream dependents can submit.
443	
444	| # | Package | Version | Bioconptron in Depends/Imports? | Non-CRAN Suggests? | Blockers | Readiness |
445	|---|---------|---------|------------------------------|-------------------|----------|-----------|
446	| 1 | greenAlgoR | 0.1.2 | No | No | Minor | High |
447	| 2 | tidypq | 0.1.1 | phyloseq (Depends) | Biostrings, dada2 | phyloseq risk | Medium-High |
448	| 3 | taxinfo | 0.1.2 | phyloseq (Imports) | gbif.range | LazyData, gbif.range guard | Medium |
449	| 4 | comparpq | 0.1.3 | phyloseq (Depends), Biostrings (Imports) | Multiple Bioc | Quarto vignette, Biostrings in Imports | Medium-Low |
450	| 5 | dbpq | 0.0.0.9000 | Biostrings (Imports) | dada2 | Version bump, Biostrings → Suggests | Low |
451	| 6 | MiscMetabar | 0.16.1.9000 | phyloseq, dada2 (Depends) | None critical | Already on CRAN; .html vignette cleanup | On CRAN |
452	| 7 | pqverse | 0.1.0 | None direct | None | ALL Imports not on CRAN yet | Very Low |
453	
454	> MiscMetabar is already on CRAN and is listed last because it needs only maintenance fixes. pqverse must wait for every other package to clear CRAN first.
455	
456	Use the skill /R_Package_Release_Pipeline on each package in parallel.  TODO: improve the use of task 
457	
458	Task(subagent_type="general", description="Check greenAlgoR", prompt="Run the /R Package Release Pipeline skill on pqverse_pkg/greenAlgoR") 
459	Task(subagent_type="general", description="Check tidypq", prompt="Run the /R Package Release Pipeline skill on pqverse_pkg/tidypq") 
460	Task(subagent_type="general", description="Check taxinfo", prompt="Run the /R Package Release Pipeline skill on pqverse_pkg/taxinfo") 
461	Task(subagent_type="general", description="Check comparpq", prompt="Run the /R Package Release Pipeline skill on pqverse_pkg/comparpq")
462	Task(subagent_type="general", description="Check dbpq", prompt="Run the /R Package Release Pipeline skill on pqverse_pkg/dbpq") 
463	Task(subagent_type="general", description="Check MiscMetabar", prompt="Run the /R Package Release Pipeline skill on pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
464	Task(subagent_type="general", description="Check pqverse", prompt="Run the /R Package Release Pipeline skill on pqverse_pkg/pqverse") 
465	
466	---
467	
468	
469	# Sources consolidated into this ROADMAP
470	
471	The items above were extracted and merged from the following files (in priority order for traceability):
472	
473	| File | Role |
474	|---|---|
475	| `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd` | Primary de-facto roadmap (2610 lines); section banners by target package |
476	| `arround_MiscMetabar/extend_phyloseq.qmd` | S4 `extended_phyloseq` class prototype |

# pqverse ROADMAP

## How to read this file

Each item is tagged with a **priority** and a **facility** level:

| Priority | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Critical** | Release-blocking; breaks documented workflows |
| **High** | Next-release target; prototype or clear design already exists |
| **Medium** | Planned; requires design work or new dependency |
| **Low** | Nice-to-have, deferred, or long-term vision |

| Facility | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **easy** | Self-contained, < ~150 LOC, no new dependency |
| **moderate** | A few functions + tests, new CRAN dependency, or dataset bundling |
| **hard** | New S4/S7 class, cross-package change, upstream permission needed, or non-CRAN dependency |

Format: `**[Priority / facility]**` — description. `(source: path[:lines])`

> **Open decision** (see bottom of file): Windows support posture.

---

# MiscMetabar

## Architecture

- **[moderate / hard]** New `extended_phyloseq` S4 class with a `tax_data` data.frame slot supporting numeric and list columns for per-taxon traits (solves the two limitations of `tax_table`: no numeric columns, no list columns). Or just a tiny function that transform a tax_table into a data.frame with numeric and list columns. 

## Data I/O

- **[Medium / easy]** `vs_cluster_subseq()` — expose `--query_cov` and `--target_cov` as direct arguments (currently only settable via `vsearch_options`). may be we can add this to MiscMetabar::postcluster_pq as well.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:52–62)

- **[Medium / easy]** Align `write.csv` output to the GBIF MDT CSV template format (distinct from the Excel exporter already planned).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/#fitting-the-data-into-a-template)

## Analysis / Statistics

- **[High / hard]** : Continue to implement bootstrapping (functions with rarperm in their name) by looking at https://f1000research.com/articles/14-87/v1 . May be we should create a new package for this in the pqverse. Is it possible to use one master function for all bootstrapping functions? See https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/msphere.00354-23 for a theoretical overview of bootstrapping. Note that I will prefer use bootstrapping word in place of rarefaction because it is often confused with rarefy. rarefy_even_depth_pq is not rarefaction (=bootstrapping) but rarefying (https://forum.qiime2.org/t/rarefaction-vs-rarefying-and-when-to-use-them-in-your-analysis-pipeline/33251). Note that bootstrapping (rarefaction) use rarefying methodology.

- **[High / moderate]** Incorporate ecodive R package (https://github.com/cmmr/ecodive) to compute beta diversity metrics, in particular robust Aitchison distances (https://cmmr.github.io/ecodive/reference/robust_aitchison.html). It must be far more quick than vegan implementations. First, create a short benchmark to compare the two implementations. Second, if the benchmark is satisfying list the functions that used betadiversity metrics and third- implement the ecodive functions in the `diversity_pq()` wrapper.


- **[High / moderate]** TINA and PINA computation via `tina_pq()` and `pina_pq()`. We must use two repos (https://github.com/defleury/Schmidt_et_al_2016_community_similarity/blob/8ef50591278925a2384324d4a56b0b633dd91104/functions.community_similarity.R for function community.similarity.corr.par and https://github.com/trtcrd/Bench_sedaDNA/blob/53962761cf890a8e3f817f8371b27e2977410c72/Collect_stats_synthetic_datasets_clusters.R especially lines 583 to 601) and two papers (https://doi.org/10.1038/ismej.2016.139, 10.1093/ismeco/ycaf230).

- **[High / moderate]** `aldex_pq()` wrapper with three test modes (kw, t, corr) — full prototype exists; inspired by EasyMicrobiomeR. Add as standalone function and vignette.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:83–203)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `check_distance()` adapted from Betadiag — checks distance matrix validity before PCoA / PERMANOVA / MiRKAT. Add `check_dist = TRUE` default to all distance-computing functions with `cli::warn()` feedback.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:52–54; https://github.com/bioscinema/Betadiag)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary plot for biomarker taxa using `lefser` + `ggtern`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1106–1179)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary + diamond plots for 3–4 group compositions — port Mahendra Mariadassou's implementation, simplify with `psmelt`. **Requires permission from M. Mariadassou before use.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1182–1525)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Correlation iconography — wrappers around `HelpersMG::IC_threshold_matrix()` and `IC_correlation_simplify()`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:955–1005)

- **[Medium / moderate]** GDM (Generalized Dissimilarity Modelling) wrapper using `gdm::formatsitepair()` + `gdm.varImp()`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1938–2004)

- **[Medium / moderate]** TITAN2 threshold indicator analysis wrapper.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2031–2051)

- **[Medium / hard]** PLS-DA / sPLS-DA via `mixOmics`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1011–1089)

- **[Low / moderate]** Hill accumulation with `divent` (replaces iNEXT approach) + proper singleton handling. **Action required: discuss with É. Marcon.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2134–2143)

- **[High / moderate]** Harmonize parameter names across all functions: `fact`, `bifactor`, `by.fact`, `merge_sample_by`, `modality`, `variable`, `var_to_test` — in progress.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / easy]** `assign_sintax()` gains a vote-based consensus method alongside / instead of the `lcaout` output (e.g. majority-vote at configurable threshold).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Post-clustering via ABGD / ASAP (when available on CRAN) — see `arround_MiscMetabar/delimtools_trying.R` for a prototype.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Integrate `Rsearch` bioinfo functions: `plot_read_quality()`, `plot_ee_rate_dist()`, `vs_fastx_uniques()`, `vs_uchime_ref()`, `vs_uchime_denovo()`.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme; https://cassandrahjo.github.io/Rsearch/)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `resolve_vector_ranks()` gains a bitscore threshold parameter (top 2% of best hit) and a majority-vote threshold (e.g. 85%) inspired by MEGAN6 LCA filtering.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — taxonomy)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Robust Aitchison PCA (rCLR + matrix completion, deicode-style) — partial implementation via `rpca::rpca()`; see also upcoming `vegan` PR.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** ALDEx2 scale-uncertainty sensitivity analysis: `aldex.senAnalysis()` + `plotGamma()` for gamma-range exploration.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices/diff abundance)

- **[Medium / moderate]** LDA Effect Size (LefSe) wrapper via `lefser` package.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `adiv::dbMANOVAspecies()` indicator-species method (Pavoine 2020).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Zeta-diversity wrappers via `zetadiv` package (Hui & McGeoch 2014).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `DivNet` + `breakaway` for bias-corrected alpha-diversity estimation.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** aPCoA (adjusted PCoA, confounder-corrected) via MicrobiomeStat implementation.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)

- **[Medium / moderate]** dbMEM (distance-based Moran's Eigenvector Maps) for spatial variation partitioning.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)

- **[Low / easy]** `indicspecies` additional association/indicator functions (see indval, phi, A, B).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** NST + iCAMP for null-model community-assembly analysis (stochastic vs. deterministic partitioning).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** Proportionality analysis (`MicEco::proportionality()`) as a CoDA-friendly co-occurrence metric.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** `coda4microbiome` and `philr` integration for isometric log-ratio–based analyses.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** Welch MANOVA for robust multivariate group comparison.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** SparseMCMM compositional mediation analysis.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** Spike-in analysis for absolute abundance quantification correction.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / hard]** `TreeSummarizedExperiment` integration as an alternative data container.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** rANOMALY + psadd inspiration for normalisation/transformation functions — review their approaches for additional transform options.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — normalisation)

- **[Low / moderate]** tidyomics — assess integration/compatibility with the tidyomics ecosystem.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / moderate]** Simulate communities utility using `miaSim` (Hubbell/LV models) for test data generation + function examples for `dist_pos_control`, `subset_taxa_tax_control`.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; vignettes)

- **[Low / easy]** More vignette examples using `microbiomeDataSets` + `miaSim` + Grinder / SparseDOSSA2 for realistic simulated data.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, vignettes)

- **[Low / easy]** Vignette on microbiome data structure characteristics: count-based, compositional, zero-inflated, high-dimensional, tree-structured (Peterson et al. 2024 Ann. Rev. Statistics).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, vignettes)

- **[Low / easy]** Vignette on zero-inflated distributions in microbiome data with recommended modelling approaches.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, vignettes)

## Visualisation

- **[Medium / moderate]** `wheat_plot_gg()` — wheat / violin hybrid plot for compositional data. Prototype available.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2285–2391)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Comet plot for 2-variable and 3-variable comparisons (inspired by zanarmstrong). Two prototypes available.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2146–2281)

- **[Low / moderate]** Color palette derived from geographic location via `earthtones`; also option to pull color from taxon photo URL (`wt_photos_gallery_pq`). Fix upstream latitude-invariance bug before integrating.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2289–2338)

- **[Medium / easy]** Volcano plot for differential abundance results (DESeq2 / ANCOMBC2 output): log₂FC vs −log₁₀(padj), directional coloring (Up / Down / Not DA).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary plot for diversity decomposition per Ricotta & Pavoine 2024 using `adiv` package (distinct from the compositional 3/4-group ternary plots already planned).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — plot; https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolind.2024.111...)

- **[Medium / easy]** `envfit()` integration from vegan inside ordination functions (`biplot_pq()`, `pcoa_pq()`) to overlay environmental vectors.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)

- **[Medium / easy]** `plot_overview_pq()` — single-call function producing basic alpha + beta diversity figures given a phyloseq object and a grouping factor.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

## Bioinfo wrappers

- **[Medium / moderate]** `mergePairs_keeping_fw()` — custom DADA2 `mergePairs` that retains the forward read when pair merging fails. Full prototype available.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1594–1656)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `fastq_to_fasta()` converter — multi-file support + flexible extension handling. Prototype adapted from baseq; needs loop over `list_fastq_files` and extension-aware logic.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1790–1840)

- **[Medium / moderate]** vsearch `--fastq_mergepairs` wrapper. Prototype has placeholder `XXXX` values; needs completion.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1661–1690)

- **[Medium / moderate]** DADA2 + FROGS hybrid pipeline — handle merged vs. concatenated read pairs with FROGS-format output conversion.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1538–1588)

- **[Medium / hard]** `vs_cluster_unoise()` — Unoise3 zOTU clustering. TODO: multi-file loop + merge `.biom` outputs per sample.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1695–1786)

- **[Low / moderate]** Phylogenetic placement via epa-ng / BoSSA / gappa.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2008–2028)

- **[Low / hard]** Sequence similarity networks / NSC reclustering per Forster et al. 2019.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2576–2610)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Function to build a `phylo` tree from a taxonomy table — inspired by `TreeSummarizedExperiment::tree_toTree()` and `Rsearch::taxonomy_tree()`.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Swarm + vsearch clustering directly from dereplicated sequences (no DADA2 required) — complements existing `asv2otu()` workflow.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** MMseqs2 integration for taxonomic assignment (`easy-taxonomy` mode) — high performance for marine / short-read metabarcoding.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / hard]** Kraken2 integration for taxonomic assignment, including UNITE database compatibility (see kraken2 issue #97).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — taxonomy)

- **[Low / hard]** FunVIP + Sativa + tronko for phylogeny-guided taxonomic assignment and post-assignment verification; also `phylotax`.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — taxonomy)

- **[Low / hard]** Metabuli integration for taxonomic classification.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

## Deferred / Considered-rejected

- **[Low / —]** `microbiomeMarker` integration — rejected: constraints on internal `tax_table` format and use of outdated ANCOMBC1. Prefer native `ancombc2_pq()` + `aldex_pq()`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2492–2527)

- **[Low / —]** `ggpicrust2` pathway_daa wrappers — rejected: too many non-CRAN dependencies.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2534–2570)

- **[Low / hard]** `ANCOMBC::secom_linear` wrapper — not working as of writing; may revisit if client need arises.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2411–2486)

- **[Low / —]** DAR package (consensus differential abundance) — rejected: numerous bugs, overly strict column-name requirements, no progress bar, sessions interfere with parallel R processes.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, abandonné)

- **[Low / —]** ensembleTax — deferred: low citation count, niche use case; may serve as inspiration for a lightweight consensus helper.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, abandonné)

- **[Low / —]** CBEA (competitive balance for microbiome analysis) — rejected: unmaintained, low citations, bioRxiv-only article.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, abandonné)

- **[Low / —]** `vdiffr` for visual regression testing of figures — deferred; not critical relative to current test-coverage priorities.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, abandonné)

- **[Low / —]** `edge::odp()` + `fFDR::fqvalue` — rejected: very few citations, redundant with existing analyses.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, abandonné)

- **[Low / —]** songbird (multinomial regression for differential ranking) — deferred; low R adoption, Python-first tool.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / —]** iqtree — deferred; existing `build_phytree_pq()` covers most use cases.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, abandonné)

- **[Low / easy]** Non-`fill` variant of `plot_tax_pq()` for count-based (non-stacked proportion) bar charts.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, abandonné)

---

# tidypq

- **[High / moderate]** Integrate `contamination.R`: add `classify_neg_control_taxa()` using known-contaminant genus blocklist (65 genera from micRoclean + GRIMER `contaminants.yml` / `human-related.yml` lists). Cite `micRoclean` as an alternative for low-biomass samples (not integrated directly).
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`, tidypq section; `ROADMAP.md`:10–37)

---

# dbpq

- **[High / hard]** Add ACVPMBD (https://github.com/Andreas-Bio/ACVPMBD, https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdf/10.1002/edn3.70125) idea for plant-specific databases.

- **[High / moderate]** Add download functions for GTDB (bacteria + archaea), MIDORI2, and KSGP databases. See Box 1 in ISME Commun. 2025 for archaea-specific workflow documentation.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:43–45)

- **[Medium / moderate]** New vignette: measuring and visualising reference database usage via OpenAlex + `openalexR` (direct citations + database-name search).
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:47)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Integrate selected `refdb` package functions: `igraph_from_taxo()`, `refdb_filter_seq_primer()`, `valid_taxo_rank()`, `refdb_sample_tax()`, `refdb_export_*()`.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

---

# comparpq

- **[Medium / moderate]** Finalize distance-to-true-community metric in the in-silico benchmarking workflow.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/benchmar_assign_taxo/analysis/benchmark.qmd`:402)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Loop to create multiple communities at the last timestep of miaSim Hubbell model (multiple independent simulations for robustness).
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/benchmar_assign_taxo/In_silico_simulation.qmd`:69)

- **[Low / easy]** Optionally add "fake" taxa to benchmark for TRUE-negative / trade-off analysis. Not urgent.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/benchmar_assign_taxo/R/functions.R`:103)

---

# taxinfo

## Dataset bundling

- **[High / moderate]** Bundle FungalTraits database as `inst/extdata`. **License check required before inclusion.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/ARCHIVES/mitis_taxonomic_project.qmd`:1471)

- **[High / moderate]** Bundle TAXREFv18 as `inst/extdata`. **License check required before inclusion.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/ARCHIVES/mitis_taxonomic_project.qmd`:1490)

- **[High / moderate]** Bundle BDC_18 taxref status (TAXREF × BDC crosswalk) as `inst/extdata`. **License check required before inclusion.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/ARCHIVES/mitis_taxonomic_project.qmd`:1521)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Add Dinerstein et al. 2017 ecoregion shapefile (CC-BY 4.0) to `inst/extdata` for use in range / bioregion functions.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:122; https://ecoregions.appspot.com/)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Integrate BACTOTRAITS dataset (Genus-level and Species-level CSVs) via `add_tax_info_pq()`. Note complex format: each trait has multiple binary/proportion columns (`motility_yes`, `motility_no`) that should be merged into a single column with TRUE / FALSE / probability values. Allow passthrough of raw columns as an option.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:124; https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-026-06652-2)

- **[Medium / easy]** Integrate Bioshift database (https://bioshifts.github.io/BioShiftR/reference/get_shifts.html)

## Functionality

- **[Medium / moderate]** Integrate `delimtools` for species-boundary cluster analysis.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:126; https://legallab.github.io/delimtools/)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Bayesian Surprise: add altitude information; consider Lidar data as additional spatial predictor.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:128–130)

- **[Low / easy]** Photo transformations via `magick`: oilpaint, reduce noise, resize, grayscale, format conversion for taxon images.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/ARCHIVES/mitis_taxonomic_project.qmd`:3408–3418)

- **[Medium / moderate]** BacDive API integration via `bacdive` R client for bacterial phenotype, cultivation, and isolation data.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Low / hard]** picrust2 functional annotation — predict metagenome functions from marker gene data; integrate as a post-processing step.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; #taxinfo-tagged)

- **[High / moderate ]** improve use of ecoregion with less points from gbif needed

- **[High / hard ]** facilitate the use of occ_download rather than occ_search

---

# greenAlgoR

- **[Low / moderate]** Additional visualization options for carbon-footprint reports.
  (source: `pqverse_pkg/greenAlgoR/README.md`)

- **[Low / moderate]** Autometric plots + `targets` pipeline network visualisation via `ggnetwork` — node size/color from CPU/memory used; link weight from runtime.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

---

# pqverse (meta-package)

- **[Medium / moderate]** OpenAlex bibliometric analysis of pqverse/phyloseq usage: resolve parquet-save bug, normalize country names, build citation graph, compare pre- vs. post-phyloseq era literature, scale up beyond `n_publi = 1000`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/open_alex_metabarcoding/Scripts/`)

- **[Low / easy]** `dupree`-based code deduplication scan as a regular refactoring step — find copy-pasted logic candidates across the package.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, régulièrement)

---

# Proposed new packages

## netaipq — Networks and ML for phyloseq

The next new package to develop in the pqverse. Wraps network inference,
bipartite visualisation, and machine-learning methods for phyloseq objects.

- **[High / moderate]** Bipartite-network visualisation — samples and taxa as nodes, edges from the OTU table, inspired by Taudiere et al. 2018 Fig. 2 and vda-lab/snowflake. ggplot2 only (no htmlwidgets). Supports sample-based and taxa-based metrics as aesthetics.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md` previous version; https://gitlab.com/vda-lab/snowflake)

- **[High / hard]** DAGITTY causal DAG integration — build and test causal models with `dagitty::localTests()` using phyloseq data.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:218–270)

- **[High / hard]** Co-occurrence networks via `cooccur` (Veech 2013 probabilistic model). Note: bug when abundance data is passed — needs fix.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:281–354)

- **[Medium / hard]** `magma_pq()` — rMAGMA microbial association network inference. Full prototype available.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:430–494)

- **[Medium / hard]** NetCoMi wrapper — taxon and sample-level networks, Aitchison distance-based.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:497–659)

- **[Medium / hard]** `CompoCor::SparCEV` compositional correlation with environmental variable.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:357–380)

- **[Medium / hard]** `ggClusterNet::network.2` / `network.i` wrappers.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:384–427)

- **[Medium / hard]** `microeco::trans_network` wrapper.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:662–696)

- **[Medium / hard]** `netassoc::make_netassoc_network` + `SpiecEasi` wrappers.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:702–730)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Random Forest wrappers: Boruta variable importance (`Boruta::Boruta`), plain `randomForest`, and `rfPermute` (Alexis Carteron variant) — all operating on `psmelt_samples_pq` output.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:748–891)

- **[Medium / hard]** MiRKAT microbiome-outcome association using UniFrac / GUniFrac kernels.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:894–934)

- **[Medium / moderate]** SHAP value plots after random forest for variable importance interpretation (`iml`, `fastshap`, or `DALEX`).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** meconetcomp + MetaNet — comparison of co-occurrence networks across groups or studies.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — network)

- **[Medium / hard]** EndDED for environmental filtering of ecological interaction networks (disentangle direct vs. environment-driven associations).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — network)

- **[Medium / hard]** qcmi integration for quantitative co-occurrence networks.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — network)

- **[Low / hard]** JSDMs / gllvm / sjSDM / MrIML for model-based co-occurrence inference (Bayesian framework, accounts for environmental confounders).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

## Ecosystem siblings (not pqverse packages, but co-developed here)

- **enasubmit** — R package for ENA sequence data submission. Already scaffolded (`checklist.R`, `submit.R`, `taxonomy.R`, vignette). Located at `arround_MiscMetabar/enasubmit/`.

- **bioinfo.starter** — `targets`-based metabarcoding pipeline template. Located at `arround_MiscMetabar/bioinfo.starter/`. Remaining work: fill `_targets.R` primer/refseq placeholders, complete `02_alpha_diversity.qmd` / `03_beta_diversity.qmd` factor-of-interest params, populate `NEWS.md`, ship Docker image.

- **docker_starter** and **starter_folder** rest on the pqverse package

---

# Cross-cutting concerns

- **Hill diversity convention** — all new Hill-based functions must use `divent::div_hill()` with `estimator = "UnveilJ"` as default (migration completed in MiscMetabar 0.15.1 and comparpq 0.2.1; deprecate `scales`/`hill_scales` params in favour of `q`).

- **Windows platform** — 13+ MiscMetabar functions are documented as non-working on Windows (`build_phytree_pq`, `cutadapt_remove_primers`, `krona`, `venn_pq`, etc.). Consider improving Docker workflow documentation as the primary mitigation.

- **Pending external contacts:**
  - **Mahendra Mariadassou** — permission to port ternary/diamond plot implementation into MiscMetabar.
  - **Éric Marcon** — discuss `divent`-based Hill accumulation and singleton handling approach.

- **Dataset licensing** — before bundling FungalTraits, TAXREFv18, or BDC_18 into any `inst/extdata`, verify that the respective licenses permit redistribution inside an R package.

---

# CRAN Submission Roadmap

> Goal: submit every pqverse package to CRAN, in dependency-first order.

## Submission order

The order is dictated by the dependency graph. Each package must be on CRAN before downstream dependents can submit.

| # | Package | Version | Bioconptron in Depends/Imports? | Non-CRAN Suggests? | Blockers | Readiness |
|---|---------|---------|------------------------------|-------------------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | greenAlgoR | 0.1.2 | No | No | Minor | High |
| 2 | tidypq | 0.1.1 | phyloseq (Depends) | Biostrings, dada2 | phyloseq risk | Medium-High |
| 3 | taxinfo | 0.1.2 | phyloseq (Imports) | gbif.range | LazyData, gbif.range guard | Medium |
| 4 | comparpq | 0.1.3 | phyloseq (Depends), Biostrings (Imports) | Multiple Bioc | Quarto vignette, Biostrings in Imports | Medium-Low |
| 5 | dbpq | 0.0.0.9000 | Biostrings (Imports) | dada2 | Version bump, Biostrings → Suggests | Low |
| 6 | MiscMetabar | 0.16.1.9000 | phyloseq, dada2 (Depends) | None critical | Already on CRAN; .html vignette cleanup | On CRAN |
| 7 | pqverse | 0.1.0 | None direct | None | ALL Imports not on CRAN yet | Very Low |

> MiscMetabar is already on CRAN and is listed last because it needs only maintenance fixes. pqverse must wait for every other package to clear CRAN first.

Let me present the strongest **Medium/easy**, pure-R, demo-friendly MiscMetabar candidates for you to pick from.

questions.question: Which MiscMetabar ROADMAP features should I build this batch? Pick 3-4 (all are Medium/easy, pure-R, demo-friendly with data_fungi_mini).

options.label: Volcano plot for DA
options.description: plot_volcano_pq() — log2FC vs -log10(padj) from DESeq2/ANCOMBC/edgeR output, directional colouring (Up/Down/NotDA). Pure ggplot2.
options.preview:
plot_volcano_pq(
  deseq2_or_ancombc_result,
  fc = 'log2FoldChange',
  padj = 'padj',
  alpha = 0.05, lfc_threshold = 1
)  # ggplot: Up/Down/NotDA

options.label: plot_overview_pq()
options.description: Single-call function producing basic alpha + beta diversity figures from a phyloseq object and a grouping factor. Wraps existing helpers + patchwork.
options.preview:
plot_overview_pq(
  data_fungi_mini,
  fact = 'Time'
)  # alpha boxplot + ordination + rank-abund, patchwork grid

options.label: envfit overlay in ordination
options.description: Add vegan::envfit() environmental-vector overlay option to biplot_pq()/pcoa-style ordination plots.
options.preview:
biplot_pq(
  data_fungi_mini,
  ...,
  envfit_fact = c('Height','Time')
)  # arrows for significant env vectors

options.label: GBIF MDT CSV export
options.description: write_pq()-style exporter aligning CSV output to the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit template format.
options.preview:
phyloseq_to_MDT_csv(
  data_fungi_mini,
  path = 'mdt/'
)  # OTU table + taxa + samples in MDT template layout

content.content: 1 ################################################################################ 2 #' Export a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF MDT submission 3 #' 4 #' @description 5 #' 6 #' 7 #' 8 #' 9 #' Write the OTU table, the sample data, the taxonomy table and (if present) the 10 #' reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a single multi-sheet `.xlsx` 11 #' file, formatted for submission to the GBIF 12 #' [Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT)](https://mdt.gbif.org/). 13 #' 14 #' Each sheet gets a leading `id` column holding the row identifiers (taxa or 15 #' samples names). The reference sequences, when available, are appended as a 16 #' `DNA_sequence` column of the taxonomy sheet (Darwin Core DNA-derived-data 17 #' extension term). 18 #' 19 #' When `check_dwc = TRUE` (the default), a lightweight Darwin Core compliance 20 #' check warns about recommended sample-level terms that are missing from 21 #' `sample_data` (`decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`, `eventDate`). This is 22 #' a non-blocking helper, not a full validation of the GBIF MDT template. 23 #' 24 #' @param physeq (required) a \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object. 25 #' @param filename (character, default `"Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx"`) Path of the 26 #' output `.xlsx` file. 27 #' @param check_dwc (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, warn about recommended 28 #' Darwin Core sample-level terms missing from `sample_data`. 29 #' 30 #' @return Invisibly returns the path to the written file (`filename`). 31 #' @export 32 #' @author Adrien Taudière 33 #' @details 34 #' This function requires the \pkg{writexl} package. See the GBIF 35 #' [Metabarcoding Data Toolkit](https://mdt.gbif.org/) for the expected 36 #' input format. 37 #' 38 #' @examples 39 #' \donttest{ 40 #' if (requireNamespace("writexl")) { 41 #' out <- file.path(tempdir(), "data_fungi_mini_MDT.xlsx") 42 #' phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out) 43 #' file.exists(out) 44 #' unlink(out) 45 #' } 46 #' } 47 phyloseq_to_MDT_excel <- function( 48 physeq, 49 filename = "Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx", 50 check_dwc = TRUE 51 ) { 52 if (!requireNamespace("writexl", quietly = TRUE)) { 53 cli::cli_abort( 54 "Package {.pkg writexl} is required for {.fn phyloseq_to_MDT_excel}. Please install it." 55 ) 56 } 57 verify_pq(physeq) 58 59 # OTU table: samples in rows, taxa in columns 60 if (taxa_are_rows(physeq)) { 61 otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(t(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix"))) 62 } else { 63 otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix")) 64 } 65 otu_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(otu_table_df), otu_table_df) 66 67 # Sample data 68 sample_sheet <- NULL 69 if (!is.null(physeq@sam_data)) { 70 sample_data_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@sam_data, "data.frame")) 71 sample_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(sample_data_df), sample_data_df) 72 73 if (check_dwc) { 74 recommended <- c("decimalLatitude", "decimalLongitude", "eventDate") 75 missing_terms <- setdiff(recommended, colnames(sample_data_df)) 76 if (length(missing_terms) > 0) { 77 cli::cli_warn(c( 78 "!" = "Recommended Darwin Core sample terms missing from {.field sample_data}: {.val {missing_terms}}.", 79 "i" = "Add them before GBIF MDT submission if available." 80 )) 81 } 82 } 83 } 84 85 # Taxonomy table (+ refseq as DNA_sequence) 86 taxonomy_sheet <- NULL 87 if (!is.null(physeq@tax_table)) { 88 tax_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@tax_table, "matrix")) 89 if (!is.null(physeq@refseq)) { 90 tax_table_df[["DNA_sequence"]] <- as.character(physeq@refseq) 91 } 92 taxonomy_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(tax_table_df), tax_table_df) 93 } 94 95 sheets <- list(OTU_table = otu_sheet) 96 if (!is.null(sample_sheet)) { 97 sheets[["Samples"]] <- sample_sheet 98 } 99 if (!is.null(taxonomy_sheet)) { 100 sheets[["Taxonomy"]] <- taxonomy_sheet 101 } 102 103 writexl::write_xlsx(sheets, path = filename) 104 message("Excel file written to ", normalizePath(filename)) 105 invisible(filename) 106 } 107 ################################################################################ 108

The reference sequences, when available, are appended as a #' `DNA_sequence` column of the taxonomy sheet (Darwin Core DNA-derived-data #' extension term). #' #' When `check_dwc = TRUE` (the default), a lightweight Darwin Core compliance #' check warns about recommended sample-level terms that are missing from #' `sample_data` (`decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`, `eventDate`). This is #' a non-blocking helper, not a full validation of the GBIF MDT template. #' #' @param physeq (required) a \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object. #' @param filename (character, default `"Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx"`) Path of the #' output `.xlsx` file. #' @param check_dwc (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, warn about recommended #' Darwin Core sample-level terms missing from `sample_data`. #' #' @return Invisibly returns the path to the written file (`filename`). #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details #' This function requires the \pkg{writexl} package. See the GBIF #' [Metabarcoding Data Toolkit](https://mdt.gbif.org/) for the expected #' input format. #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("writexl")) { #' out <- file.path(tempdir(), "data_fungi_mini_MDT.xlsx") #' phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out) #' file.exists(out) #' unlink(out) #' } #' } phyloseq_to_MDT_excel <- function( physeq, filename = "Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx", check_dwc = TRUE ) { if (!requireNamespace("writexl", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg writexl} is required for {.fn phyloseq_to_MDT_excel}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) # OTU table: samples in rows, taxa in columns if (taxa_are_rows(physeq)) { otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(t(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix"))) } else { otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix")) } otu_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(otu_table_df), otu_table_df) # Sample data sample_sheet <- NULL if (!is.null(physeq@sam_data)) { sample_data_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@sam_data, "data.frame")) sample_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(sample_data_df), sample_data_df) if (check_dwc) { recommended <- c("decimalLatitude", "decimalLongitude", "eventDate") missing_terms <- setdiff(recommended, colnames(sample_data_df)) if (length(missing_terms) > 0) { cli::cli_warn(c( "!" = "Recommended Darwin Core sample terms missing from {.field sample_data}: {.val {missing_terms}}.", "i" = "Add them before GBIF MDT submission if available." )) } } } # Taxonomy table (+ refseq as DNA_sequence) taxonomy_sheet <- NULL if (!is.null(physeq@tax_table)) { tax_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@tax_table, "matrix")) if (!is.null(physeq@refseq)) { tax_table_df[["DNA_sequence"]] <- as.character(physeq@refseq) } taxonomy_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(tax_table_df), tax_table_df) } sheets <- list(OTU_table = otu_sheet) if (!is.null(sample_sheet)) { sheets[["Samples"]] <- sample_sheet } if (!is.null(taxonomy_sheet)) { sheets[["Taxonomy"]] <- taxonomy_sheet } writexl::write_xlsx(sheets, path = filename) message("Excel file written to ", normalizePath(filename)) invisible(filename) } ################################################################################

content.text: `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R` is the clear template for the CSV exporter. Now let me study the DA function outputs (for the volcano plot) and the alpha/beta helpers + `biplot_pq` signature (for `plot_overview_pq`).

150	#' @param pval (default: 0.05) the significance cutoff used for optimizing
151	#'   the independent filtering. If the adjusted p-value cutoff (FDR) will be a
152	#'   value other than 0.05, pval should be set to that value.
153	#' @param taxolev taxonomic level of interest
154	#' @param select_taxa Either the name of the taxa (in the form of [DESeq2::results()])
155	#'   or a logical vector (length of the results from [DESeq2::results()]) to select taxa
156	#'   to plot.
157	#' @param color_tax taxonomic level used for color or a
158	#'   color vector.
159	#' @param tax_depth Taxonomic depth to test for differential
160	#'   distribution among contrast. If Null the analysis is done at the OTU
161	#'   (i.e. Species) level. If not Null, data need to be a column name in
162	#'   the `tax_table` slot of the \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object.
163	#' @param verbose whether the function print some information during
164	#'   the computation
165	#' @param jitter_width width for the jitter positioning
166	#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to \code{\link[DESeq2]{DESeq}}
167	#'   or \code{\link[ggplot2]{ggplot}}
168	#'
169	#' @export
170	#' @details
171	#'   Please cite `DESeq2` package if you use chis function.
172	#' @examples
173	#' \donttest{
174	#'
175	#' data("GlobalPatterns", package = "phyloseq")
176	#' GP <- subset_taxa(GlobalPatterns, GlobalPatterns@tax_table[, 1] == "Archaea")
177	#' GP <- subset_samples(GP, SampleType %in% c("Soil", "Skin"))
178	#' if (requireNamespace("DESeq2")) {
179	#'   res <- DESeq2::DESeq(phyloseq_to_deseq2(GP, ~SampleType),
180	#'     test = "Wald", fitType = "local"
181	#'   )
182	#'   plot_deseq2_pq(res, c("SampleType", "Soil", "Skin"),
183	#'     tax_table = GP@tax_table, color_tax = "Kingdom"
184	#'   )
185	#'   plot_deseq2_pq(res, c("SampleType", "Soil", "Skin"),
186	#'     tax_table = GP@tax_table, color_tax = "Kingdom",
187	#'     pval = 0.7
188	#'   )
189	#'   plot_deseq2_pq(res, c("SampleType", "Soil", "Skin"),
190	#'     tax_table = GP@tax_table, color_tax = "Class",
191	#'     select_taxa = c("522457", "271582")
192	#'   )
193	#' }
194	#' }
195	#' @author Adrien Taudière
196	#'
197	#' @return A \code{\link[ggplot2]{ggplot}}2 plot representing DESeq2 results
198	#'
199	#' @seealso \code{\link[DESeq2]{DESeq}}
200	#' @seealso \code{\link[DESeq2]{results}}
201	#' @seealso \code{\link{plot_edgeR_pq}}
202	
203	plot_deseq2_pq <-
204	  function(
205	    data,
206	    contrast = NULL,
207	    tax_table = NULL,
208	    pval = 0.05,
209	    taxolev = "Genus",
210	    select_taxa = NULL,
211	    color_tax = "Phylum",
212	    tax_depth = NULL,
213	    verbose = TRUE,
214	    jitter_width = 0.1,
215	    ...
216	  ) {
217	    if (!inherits(data, "phyloseq")) {
218	      if (!inherits(data, "DESeqDataSet")) {
219	        stop("data must be an object of class 'phyloseq' or 'DESeqDataSet'")
220	      }
221	    } else {
222	      # Calculate new dataset given the Taxa depth if tax_depth is not null
223	      if (!is.null(tax_depth)) {
224	        data_tax <- data
225	        data_tax@otu_table <-
226	          otu_table(
227	            apply(data@otu_table, 2, function(x) {
228	              tapply(
229	                x,
230	                data@tax_table[, tax_depth],
231	                sum
232	              )
233	            }),
234	            taxa_are_rows = TRUE
235	          )
236	        data_tax@tax_table <-
237	          tax_table(apply(
238	            data@tax_table[
239	              ,
240	              1:match(
241	                tax_depth,
242	                colnames(data@tax_table)
243	              )
244	            ],
245	            2,
246	            function(x) {
247	              tapply(
248	                x,
249	                data@tax_table[, tax_depth],
250	                function(xx) {
251	                  xx[1]
252	                }
253	              )
254	            }
255	          ))
256	        data_tax@refseq <- NULL
257	        data <- data_tax
258	        if (is.na(match(taxolev, colnames(data@tax_table)))) {
259	          taxolev <- tax_depth
260	        }
261	      }
262	
263	      if (is.null(tax_table) && inherits(data, "phyloseq")) {
264	        tax_table <- data@tax_table
265	      }
266	
267	      if (verbose) {
268	        message("Conversion to Deseq2 format.")
269	      }
270	      data_deseq2 <-
271	        phyloseq_to_deseq2(data, stats::as.formula(paste("~", contrast[1])))
272	
273	      if (verbose) {
274	        message("Calculation of Deseq2 results.")
275	      }
276	      data <-
277	        DESeq2::DESeq(
278	          data_deseq2,
279	          test = "Wald",
280	          fitType = "parametric",
281	          quiet = !verbose,
282	          ...
283	        )
284	    }
285	
286	    # Calcul deseq2 results
287	    res <- DESeq2::results(data, contrast = contrast)
288	
289	    if (!is.null(select_taxa[1])) {
290	      res <- res[select_taxa, ]
291	    }
292	
293	    d <- res[which(res$padj < pval), ]
294	
295	    if (dim(d)[1] == 0) {
296	      message(
297	        "None taxa present significant distribution pattern through
298	              contrast."
299	      )
300	      return(
301	        "None taxa present significant distribution pattern through
302	             contrast."
303	      )
304	    }
305	    d <-
306	      cbind(
307	        methods::as(d, "data.frame"),
308	        methods::as(tax_table[rownames(d), ], "matrix")
309	      )
310	
311	    # Compute colors
312	    are_colors <- function(x) {
313	      sapply(x, function(xx) {
314	        tryCatch(
315	          is.matrix(grDevices::col2rgb(xx)),
316	          error = function(e) {
317	            FALSE
318	          }
319	        )
320	      })
321	    }
322	
323	    if (!sum(are_colors(color_tax)) > 0) {
324	      x <- tapply(d$log2FoldChange, d[, color_tax], function(x) {
325	        max(x)
326	      })
327	      x <- sort(x, TRUE)
328	      d$col_tax <-
329	        factor(as.character(d[, color_tax]), levels = names(x))
330	    } else {
331	      d$col_tax <- rep(color_tax, length = dim(d)[1])
332	    }
333	
334	    # Compute log2FoldChange values
335	    x <- tapply(d$log2FoldChange, d[, taxolev], function(x) {
336	      max(x)
337	    })
338	    x <- sort(x, TRUE)
339	    d$tax <- factor(as.character(d[, taxolev]), levels = names(x))
340	
341	    if (!sum(are_colors(color_tax)) > 0) {
342	      p <-
343	        ggplot(d, aes(x = tax, y = log2FoldChange, color = col_tax), ...) +
344	        geom_point(
345	          size = 6,
346	          position = position_jitter(width = jitter_width, height = 0)
347	        ) +
348	        theme(
349	          axis.text.x = element_text(
350	            angle = -90,
351	            hjust = 0,
352	            vjust = 0.5
353	          )
354	        ) +
355	        labs(
356	          title = paste(
357	            "Change in abundance for ",
358	            contrast[1],
359	            " (top:",
360	            contrast[2],
361	            " vs down:",
362	            contrast[3],
363	            ")",
364	            sep = ""
365	          )
366	        )
367	    } else {
368	      p <-
369	        ggplot(d, aes(x = tax, y = log2FoldChange), ...) +
370	        geom_point(
371	          size = 6,
372	          color = d$col_tax,
373	          position = position_jitter(width = jitter_width, height = 0)
374	        ) +
375	        theme(
376	          axis.text.x = element_text(
377	            angle = -90,
378	            hjust = 0,
379	            vjust = 0.5
380	          )
381	        ) +
382	        labs(
383	          title = paste(
384	            "Change in abundance for ",
385	            contrast[1],
386	            " (top:",
387	            contrast[2],
388	            " vs down:",
389	            contrast[3],
390	            ")",
391	            sep = ""
392	          )
393	        )
394	    }
395	
396	    return(p)
397	  }
