content.text: Max 4 options per question — let me split the panels across two questions.

questions.question: Core panels for the new plot_overview_pq() — which to include by default?

options.label: Read-depth / library size
options.description: Distribution of sequencing depth per sample (+ n samples / n taxa summary). Classic QC first-look.
options.preview:
depth per sample (sorted bar or boxplot by group)
+ text: N samples, N taxa, N seqs

options.label: Taxonomic composition
options.description: Stacked relative-abundance barplot at a chosen rank, per sample or merged per group. Wraps tax_bar_pq()/plot_tax_pq().
options.preview: stacked bars: rel. abundance by Phylum

options.label: Alpha diversity
options.description: Hill-number boxplots by group (q = 0,1,2). Wraps hill_pq().
options.preview: boxplots Hill q0/q1/q2 by group

options.label: Beta ordination
options.description: PCoA/NMDS ordination colored by group. Wraps plot_ordination_pq().
options.preview: PCoA scatter, points colored by group

questions.question: Additional panels (optional) — include any of these too?

options.label: Rarefaction / accumulation
options.description: Rarefaction or species-accumulation curve to judge sampling completeness. Wraps SRS_curve_pq()/hill_curves_pq().
options.preview: richness vs sampling effort curve(s)

options.label: Prevalence vs abundance
options.description: Per-taxon prevalence vs mean abundance — a filtering-decision aid.
options.preview: scatter: x = mean abundance (log), y = prevalence

options.label: None of these
options.description: Keep the core panels only; no rarefaction or prevalence panel.
options.preview: (core panels only)

questions.question: Should the grouping factor be required or optional?

options.label: Optional (recommended)
options.description: Works with no factor (pure dataset overview); if a factor is given, group/color panels by it. Most flexible for a 'first look'.
options.preview:
plot_overview_pq(physeq)
plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact='Height')

options.label: Required
options.description: Always needs a grouping factor (like the current version). Simpler, but no ungrouped overview.
options.preview: plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact='Height')

questions.question: What return type do you prefer?

options.label: Both via one_plot (recommended)
options.description: one_plot=TRUE returns an assembled patchwork; one_plot=FALSE returns a named list of the panels. Mirrors hill_pq().
options.preview:
one_plot=TRUE  -> patchwork
one_plot=FALSE -> named list

options.label: Assembled patchwork only
options.description: Always one combined figure; toggle panels with add_* args, layout via ncol/guides.
options.preview: single patchwork figure

options.label: Named list only
options.description: Return panels as a named list; caller assembles/customizes.
options.preview: list(depth=, taxa=, alpha=, beta=)

content.text:
Got it — so far you're leaning toward **taxonomic composition + alpha diversity + beta ordination + a Venn/UpSet panel** of shared taxa across groups (MiscMetabar already has `ggvenn_pq()` / `venn_pq()` / `upset_pq()` for that last one). I held off on the other three questions.

What would you like to clarify? A few things that might be behind your hesitation, but tell me what's actually on your mind:

- **The panel menu itself** — were the options too narrow/wrong, or is it more about *how* panels combine (e.g. you want a specific layout, or panels that interact)?
- **The Venn/UpSet panel** — Venn caps at ~4 groups; UpSet scales further. Did you mean shared *taxa* across factor levels, or something else (shared across samples)?
- **Scope** — should this be a fixed opinionated figure, or a configurable one where the user picks panels?
- **A reference** — do you have an existing figure/tool/paper in mind that this should look like? That'd anchor the whole design fast.

Tell me what you'd like to dig into and I'll reshape the questions (or just sketch the design) around it.

content: Goal: redesign `plot_overview_pq` into a one-call dataset overview. You picked taxonomic composition, alpha, beta, and a Venn/UpSet panel. Next: tell me what you want to clarify so I can finalize the panel design. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - **Original:** User invoked `/r-feature-batch` to implement 3-4 ROADMAP features for the MiscMetabar R package, one at a time on the `dev` branch, then consolidate all global-file edits once, write a single Quarto demo, and leave everything uncommitted for the developer. The skill explicitly forbids committing.
   - **Feature selection:** User chose 3 features: (1) Volcano plot for differential abundance → `plot_volcano_pq()`, (2) `plot_overview_pq()` single-call alpha+beta overview, (3) GBIF MDT CSV export → `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()`.
   - **Interrupt 1:** "show me the html demo file in a browser" (completed).
   - **Interrupt 2 (CURRENT, overriding):** "The plot_overview_pq is not what I want lets talk about how to implement this function and plan a new version." → User wants to REDESIGN `plot_overview_pq()` collaboratively.
   - **Most recent signal:** User rejected my reformulated AskUserQuestion, saying they want to clarify the questions first ("Start by asking them what they would like to clarify"). User pre-answered Q1 Core panels as: "Taxonomic composition, Alpha diversity, Beta ordination, venn diagramm (or upset plot)" — notably ADDING a venn/upset panel that was not among my offered options.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - R package development for phyloseq objects (pqverse ecosystem: MiscMetabar, tidypq, etc.)
   - phyloseq S4 object slots: otu_table, tax_table, sam_data, refseq
   - CRAN check-time constraints: data_fungi_mini has 137 samples; cap per-sample-loop examples to 5; `\donttest{}` runs on CRAN, `\dontrun{}` never runs; dada2/purrr/divent in Imports not Depends (need `library()` in test files)
   - divent in Imports → produces "CheckArguments because divent is not attached" warnings (documented, harmless)
   - Hill numbers (q=0,1,2) via hill_pq(); ordination via plot_ordination_pq() (PCoA recommended over NMDS for small data); patchwork for composition
   - Differential abundance: DESeq2 (log2FoldChange/padj), ANCOMBC (lfc_*/q_*), ALDEx2 (effect/wi.eBH)
   - GBIF MDT template: 4 sheets (OTU_table, Taxonomy, Samples, Study); OTU table = OTU IDs in ROWS, sample IDs in COLUMNS; tables keyed by `id` column; accepts TSV+XLSX
   - Conventions: base pipe `|>`, `function() {}` not `\()`, `{}` around all if/else, en-US spelling, `air format`, roxygen2, NEWS bullets alphabetical by function name, underscore test filenames `test_<slug>.R`, lifecycle-experimental badge in roxygen
   - RTK hook rewrites bash commands (rg→grep can break with directories)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`R/plot_volcano_pq.R`** (CREATED, 9 tests PASS) — Volcano plot, column-agnostic. Signature: `plot_volcano_pq(df, fc = "log2FoldChange", padj = "padj", alpha = 0.05, lfc_threshold = 1, label_col = NULL, label_n = 10, point_size = 2, point_alpha = 0.7, palette = c(Down = "#1d2949", NotDA = "grey70", Up = "#4B3E1E"))`. Handles padj==0 (caps -log10 at finite_max*1.05) and NA padj (→NotDA). Status factor levels c("Down","NotDA","Up"). Uses `is_signif` (renamed from `signif` to avoid base-symbol lint). Pure ggplot2.
   - **`R/plot_overview_pq.R`** (CREATED — USER WANTS REDESIGNED). Current signature: `plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, ...)`. Currently just composes `hill_pq(physeq, fact=fact, q=q, one_plot=FALSE, plot_with_tuckey=FALSE)` (returns named list of ggplots) + `plot_ordination_pq(physeq, method=dist_method, ordination_method=ordination_method, color=fact)` via `patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)`. Guards patchwork with requireNamespace+cli_abort. **This is the file to be rewritten per the new design.**
   - **`R/phyloseq_to_MDT_csv.R`** (CREATED, 8 tests PASS). Signature: `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv(physeq, path = ".", prefix = "", sep = ",", check_dwc = TRUE)`. Writes 3 files (OTU_table, Taxonomy, Samples) via internal `write_one()` helper (uses `out_file`, not `file`, to avoid lint). **Correct MDT orientation: taxa in rows, samples in columns, leading `id` column** (opposite of the buggy excel exporter). ext .csv for sep="," else .tsv. Returns invisible named char vector. DWC check warns on missing decimalLatitude/Longitude/eventDate.
   - **`R/phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R`** (READ ONLY, template) — Has the WRONG orientation bug: writes samples-in-rows. NOT modified (flagged for developer).
   - **Test files** (CREATED): `tests/testthat/test_plot_volcano_pq.R`, `test_plot_overview_pq.R`, `test_phyloseq_to_MDT_csv.R` (underscore convention, skip_on_cran, skip_if_not_installed guards).
   - **`NEWS.md`** (MODIFIED) — under `# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000`, added 3 bullets, re-sorted with "Rarefaction across the package..." (non-function) first, then alphabetical: clean_pq, fastp, is_blastn_installed, phyloseq_to_MDT_csv, phyloseq_to_MDT_excel, plot_overview_pq, plot_volcano_pq, rarefy_pq, reshape_ggplot.
   - **`_pkgdown.yml`** (MODIFIED) — plot_overview_pq after plot_ordination_pq (beta section); plot_volcano_pq after plot_mt (DA section); phyloseq_to_MDT_csv before phyloseq_to_MDT_excel (Import/export).
   - **`inst/WORDLIST`** (MODIFIED) — added TSV, XLSX, validator (LC_ALL=C re-sorted).
   - **`ROADMAP.md`** (MODIFIED, at pqverse root) — deleted 3 shipped bullets (volcano, plot_overview_pq, GBIF MDT CSV).
   - **`arround_MiscMetabar/overview_volcano_mdt_demo.qmd`** (CREATED) + **`overview_volcano_mdt_demo.html`** (RENDERED, 1.9MB, untracked artifact) — demo covering all 3 features; setup loads dev pkg via devtools::load_all, uses 6-sample subset (3 High + 3 Low).
   - **`.claude/settings.json`** (MODIFIED during session, NOT by me) — harness-written permission update: added Bash(cd/tail/head/ps/which/rtk rg) to allow, removed Read(./.entire/metadata/**) deny.
   - **`summary_plot_pq()`** in R/plot_functions.R:2179 (READ) — existing STRUCTURAL overview (shows slots otu_table/tax_table/sam_data/refseq with counts), NOT a diversity overview; does not collide with plot_overview_pq's diversity purpose.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **RTK hook rewrote `rg`→`grep`** causing "Is a directory" errors → switched to `grep -r`.
   - **Lint: reused base symbol `signif`** in plot_volcano_pq.R → renamed to `is_signif`.
   - **Lint: reused base symbol `file`** in phyloseq_to_MDT_csv.R write_one() → renamed to `out_file`.
   - **plot_overview_pq test with q=0 failed** (`dim(X) must have a positive length` — pre-existing hill_pq limitation: single-q drops to vector) → changed test to q=c(0,1). Documented q as a vector.
   - **MDT format: I almost guessed** → advisor said WebFetch the docs. WebFetch 403/404, so WebSearch confirmed correct orientation (taxa-in-rows).
   - **British spellings** (coloured/colouring/favoured) flagged by spell check → changed to American (Language=en-US, codebase uses color 235x vs colour 14x).
   - **`.claude/settings.json` mis-attribution:** I initially said it was "pre-existing"; advisor corrected me — it was modified DURING the session. Verified via git diff: it's a harness permission-allowlist write (not my deliverable, not mine to revert).
   - **AskUserQuestion >4 options rejected** → split 6 panel options into two 4-and-3-option questions.
   - **User REJECTED the reformulated AskUserQuestion**, wanting to clarify the questions first. User's instruction: "The user wants to clarify these questions... Start by asking them what they would like to clarify."

5. Problem Solving:
   - All 3 features implemented, individually validated (9+3+8=20 PASS), consolidated cleanly (no NAMESPACE/man carryover), spelling clean, full suite exit 0, demo renders end-to-end.
   - Identified and flagged (NOT fixed) the `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel` orientation bug — built CSV exporter to correct orientation, left excel for developer decision.
   - The feature batch was essentially complete when the user pivoted to wanting plot_overview_pq redesigned.

6. All user messages:
   - [/clear then /r-feature-batch skill invocation with no args]
   - (via AskUserQuestion answer) Selected features: "Volcano plot for DA, plot_overview_pq(), GBIF MDT CSV export"
   - "show me the html demo file in a browser"
   - "The plot_overview_pq is not what I want lets talk about how to implement this function and plan a new version."
   - (via AskUserQuestion rejection) Answered Core panels: "Taxonomic composition, Alpha diversity, Beta ordination, venn diagramm (or upset plot"; left Q2-Q4 unanswered; rejection note: "The user wants to clarify these questions. This means they may have additional information, context or questions for you. Take their response into account and then reformulate the questions if appropriate. Start by asking them what they would like to clarify."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **PRIMARY (active):** Redesign/plan a new version of `plot_overview_pq()`. User wants it to include (at minimum): Taxonomic composition, Alpha diversity, Beta ordination, AND a Venn diagram or Upset plot panel (note: venn/upset was user-added, not in my original options). Grouping-factor requirement, extra panels (rarefaction, prevalence), and return type are still undecided.
   - **Immediate sub-task per user instruction:** ASK the user what they'd like to clarify about the design questions before reformulating them.
   - (Batch leftover, not yet reported to user): final feature-batch report (3 features shipped, tests green, demo path, MDT orientation discrepancy flag, R CMD check NOT run → offer /r-check). This was about to be delivered when the user pivoted.

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary, I was redesigning `plot_overview_pq()` per user interrupt 2. I had explained the current implementation and nearby existing functions (summary_plot_pq, hill_pq, plot_ordination_pq, tax_bar_pq/plot_tax_pq, SRS_curve_pq, hill_curves_pq, venn_pq, ggvenn_pq, upset_pq), then attempted an AskUserQuestion with 4 questions (Core panels, Extra panels, Factor required/optional, Return type). I should acknowledge their Core-panels answer (Taxonomic composition, Alpha diversity, Beta ordination, + Venn/Upset diagram — noting venn/upset is a new addition I'll incorporate) and invite them to tell me what about the remaining questions (extra panels, factor required/optional, return type) or the panel set itself they want to adjust or discuss further, before I reformulate. file.content:
## R Development

### Available skills

**Validation pipeline** (read-only — never modify code without asking):

- `/r-test` — run the test suite, report coverage, propose new tests, fix failures, submit to Codecov.
- `/r-check` — style with `air format`, regenerate docs with roxygen2, run R CMD check, propose fixes for NOTEs/WARNINGs/ERRORs.
- `/r-build` — build README, pkgdown site, source tarball; lint the package.
- `/r-audit` — pqverse-specific external audit. Generates independent test data, runs every exported function with edge cases (NAs, empty samples, zero-count taxa, malformed trees), produces a TODO report. Targets MiscMetabar, tidypq, comparpq.

**Release pipeline** (Stages 1–9 — Stages 8 & 9 mutate git state):

- `/r-pkg-release` — orchestrator. Runs Stages 1–7 (validation: test → audit → check → CRAN extra checks → build → optional code review → optional lifecycle review), then optionally Stages 8–9 (mutating).
- `/r-pkg-bump-version` — Stage 8. Bumps `DESCRIPTION` from `X.Y.Z.9000` → release `X.Y.Z` and rewrites the first NEWS.md heading. Suggests major/minor/patch from NEWS keywords. No commit.
- `/r-pkg-merge-release` — Stage 9. Commits Stage 8's changes, merges `dev` → `master`, pushes, then bumps dev to `X.Y.Z.9000` for the next development cycle. Offers a dry-run.

**Feature development**:

- `/r-feature-batch` — implement 3-4 ROADMAP features one after another, then consolidate in a single pass with full hygiene (tests, alphabetical NEWS bullets, `_pkgdown.yml` entries, regenerated `man/`, spelling, scoped `air format`) and one runnable Quarto demo in `arround_MiscMetabar/` covering every new feature. Leaves the diff uncommitted for the developer.

**Analysis & figures**:

- `/r-stats` — guided statistical analysis: test selection, assumption checks, effect sizes, APA-compliant reporting (t-test, ANOVA, chi-square, correlation, regression, Bayesian).
- `/r-viz` — publication-ready scientific figures with ggplot2: multi-panel layouts, significance annotations, error bars, colorblind-safe palettes, journal-specific formatting.

### Plugin-provided skills worth knowing

Reference material loaded on demand via the Skill tool — useful when the task matches:

- `r-lib:r-package-development` — devtools/testthat/roxygen2 fundamentals.
- `r-lib:testing-r-packages` — testthat 3+ patterns (snapshots, fixtures, mocking).
- `r-lib:cran-extrachecks` — ad-hoc CRAN requirements (used by `/r-pkg-release` Stage 4).
- `r-lib:lifecycle` — experimental/deprecated/superseded badges (used by `/r-pkg-release` Stage 7).
- `r-lib:cli` — semantic console output (`cli_abort`, `cli_inform`, progress bars).
- `r-lib:r-cli-app` — building command-line apps with Rapp.
- `r-lib:mirai` — async/parallel/distributed computing.
- `posit-dev:critical-code-reviewer` — adversarial review (used by `/r-pkg-release` Stage 6).
- `posit-dev:describe-design` — architectural docs with Mermaid diagrams.
- `open-source:create-release-checklist` — generates a GitHub release-checklist issue.
- `open-source:release-post` — tidyverse/Shiny-style release blog posts.

### Coding conventions

- When working with R code, prefer base R or tidyverse/established package solutions (e.g., vegan::renyi) over manual reimplementations. Ask before writing custom implementations of things that likely exist in CRAN packages.

- When editing R package code: follow existing package conventions (e.g., silent-by-default behavior, parameter ordering, roxygen style). Read 2-3 existing functions before writing new ones to match patterns.

- Always run `air format .` after generating code (or invoke `/r-check`, which runs styling as part of the check pipeline).
- Use the base pipe operator (`|>`) not the magrittr pipe (`%>%`)
- Use `\() ...` for single-line anonymous functions. For all other cases, use `function() {...}`
- Always use `{}` around if else statement (e.g. `if(cond) df <- my_function(x)` is not allowed, prefer `if(cond) {df <- my_function(x)}`)


### CRAN check time — keeping under 10 minutes

R CMD check runs the package through multiple stages (package load, examples,
tests). Each `Depends` package is *attached* (search-path level) before every
stage; `Imports` packages are loaded lazily. Heavy packages (Bioconductor,
dada2, etc.) in `Depends` therefore inflate *every* check stage's wall time.

**Depends → Imports migration checklist:**
1. Move the package in `DESCRIPTION` from `Depends:` to `Imports:`.
2. Remove the blanket `@import pkg` from the package file (keep or add targeted
   `@importFrom pkg fn` for functions used without `::` in R code).
3. Prefix any remaining bare calls in `R/*.R` with `pkg::`.
4. Add `library(pkg)` at the top of **test files** that call that package's
   functions directly — they no longer get it for free via the search path.
5. Run `devtools::document()` to regenerate `NAMESPACE`.

**Example timing — two passes on CRAN:**
- `--no-run-donttest`: runs only plain examples (~157 s locally for MiscMetabar)
- `--run-donttest`: also runs `\donttest{}` blocks (~439 s)
- `\dontrun{}` blocks never run; use them to preserve extra examples in the
  help page without contributing to check time.

**Test file `skip_on_cran()` placement:**
- Must be the **first line** of the file (before any `data()` calls, outside
  any `test_that()`) to skip the entire file on CRAN.
- `skip_on_cran()` *inside* a `test_that()` block only skips that one test.

### Testing

- Tests for `R/{name}.R` go in `tests/testthat/test-{name}.R`.
- All new code in package should have an accompanying test.
- If there are existing tests, place new tests next to similar existing tests.
- Strive to keep your tests minimal with few comments.

### `NEWS.md`

- Every user-facing change should be given a bullet in `NEWS.md`. Do not add bullets for small documentation changes or internal refactorings.
- Each bullet should briefly describes the change to the end user, and mention the related issue in parentheses.
- A bullet can consist of multiple sentences but should not contain any new lines (i.e. don't wrap the bullet).
- If the change is related to a function, put the name of the function early in the bullet.
- Order bullets alphabetically by function name. Put all bullets that don't mention function names at the beginning.
- Frame bullets positively (i.e. what now happens, not what used to happen), and use the present tense.
- Functions, arguments, and file names should be wrapped in backticks. Function names should include parentheses : for example `my_new_function()`
- Each release should have a level 1 heading (#) containing the package name and version number. For smaller packages or patch releases this amount of organisation may be sufficient.
- Breaking changes should also appear in their own section at the top. Each bullet should include a description of the symptoms of the change, and what is needed to fix it. The bullet should also be repeated in the appropriate section.

### Documentation

- Every user-facing function should be exported and have roxygen2 documentation.
- Whenever you add a new (non-internal) documentation topic, also add the topic to `_pkgdown.yml`.

## Domain-Specific Notes

- When working with phyloseq objects and the user's R packages (pqverse, tidypq, MiscMetabar): always read the existing function signatures and helpers before implementing new functions. Pay attention to how taxa/sample operations are structured.

## Reporting

For informational/status messages, prefer message. Use print only for displaying objects, and mostly in debugging contexts. Use cat only for specific low-level output formatting (e.g., when defining print methods for new objects).

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project overview

This workspace ("pqverse") contains R packages and projects for metabarcoding analysis.

- `pqverse_pkg/` - All R packages of the pqverse (see table below).
- `arround_MiscMetabar/` - Work-in-progress scripts, analyses, and non-package projects (benchmarks, formations, etc.).

The central data structure across the pqverse is the `phyloseq` object from the phyloseq package.

### Packages

| Package | Description | Status |
|---------|-------------|--------|
| **MiscMetabar** | Miscellaneous functions for description, transformation, exploration, and reproducibility of metabarcoding analyses. Built on top of phyloseq, dada2, and targets. | Published on [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=MiscMetabar) and [JOSS](https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.06038) |
| **tidypq** | Tidyverse-style verbs for manipulating phyloseq objects at four scales: samples, taxa, occurrences, and tree. | In development |
| **dbpq** | Download, format, summarize, and modify FASTA reference databases for taxonomic assignment. | In development |
| **comparpq** | Tools to compare and analyse multiple phyloseq objects. | In development |
| **taxinfo** | Augment phyloseq objects with taxonomy-based information from sources such as GBIF, Wikipedia, and GloBI. | In development |
| **greenAlgoR** | Carbon footprint estimation for R computations, based on the Green Algorithms framework. Includes support for targets pipelines. | In development |
| **pqverse** | Meta-package that installs and loads all pqverse packages in a single `library()` call, following the tidyverse pattern. | In development |


## Roadmap

`ROADMAP.md` (workspace root) is the single source of truth for planned
features across all pqverse packages. Each item carries a `[Priority /
facility]` tag:

- **Priority:** Critical → High → Medium → Low
- **Facility:** easy (< 1 day) → moderate (a few days) → hard (architectural / external)

Prototype code for many items lives in
`arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd` (partitioned by target package).
When implementing an item, cross-reference the `(source: …)` pointer in
the bullet to find the prototype.


## Build and development commands

Follow the instruction in @/home/adrien/.claude/skills/r_development.md

## MiscMetabar — CRAN check-time constraints

MiscMetabar was archived on 2026-05-19 for exceeding CRAN's 10-minute check budget.
Keep the following in mind whenever touching examples, tests, or `DESCRIPTION`.

### `DESCRIPTION` — Depends vs Imports

**Current Depends:** `R (>= 4.1.0)`, `phyloseq`, `ggplot2 (>= 3.5.0)`, `dplyr`
**In Imports (not Depends):** `dada2`, `purrr`, `divent` (moved 2026-06)

Every package in `Depends` is *attached* (put on the search path) at each check
stage, adding wall time proportional to that package's load cost. `dada2` is
heavy (Bioconductor + ShortRead + Biostrings chain); keeping it in Imports saves
significant per-stage overhead.

Consequence: **test files that call dada2/purrr/divent functions directly must
add `library(dada2)` (or the relevant package) at the top of the file.** The
function won't be found otherwise. Files currently requiring this:
`test_blast.R`, `test_targets.R` (dada2), `test_visualization.R` (divent —
needed for divent's internal `CheckArguments` check, even if no divent function
is called directly in the test file).

Moving `phyloseq`, `ggplot2`, and `dplyr` from Depends → Imports is a
**breaking change** for users and is deferred to 0.17.0.

### `data_fungi_mini` has 137 samples

Any function that loops over samples (e.g. `profile_hill_pq`, `umap_pq`,
`psmelt_samples_pq`) will be slow on `data_fungi_mini`. Limit examples to 5
samples:

```r
prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini)
```

### Example wrapping strategy

- `\donttest{}` — runs in BOTH example passes on CRAN (`--run-donttest`); use
  for the primary example of each function.
- `\dontrun{}` — **never runs automatically**; shown in the help page but
  doesn't contribute to check time. Use for extra/variant examples that must
  be preserved for documentation but are not strictly needed for check.
- Never *remove* examples — wrap them in `\dontrun{}` instead.

### Test file `skip_on_cran()` placement

`skip_on_cran()` must be the **very first line** of the file (before any
`data()` loads, outside any `test_that()` block) to skip the entire file on
CRAN. A `skip_on_cran()` *inside* a `test_that()` block skips only that one
test. Files with file-level skips are listed in `cran-comments.md`.

<!-- rtk-instructions v2 -->
# RTK (Rust Token Killer) - Token-Optimized Commands

## Golden Rule

**Always prefix commands with `rtk`**. If RTK has a dedicated filter, it uses it. If not, it passes through unchanged. This means RTK is always safe to use.

**Important**: Even in command chains with `&&`, use `rtk`:
```bash
# ❌ Wrong
git add . && git commit -m "msg" && git push

# ✅ Correct
rtk git add . && rtk git commit -m "msg" && rtk git push
```

## RTK Commands by Workflow

### Build & Compile (80-90% savings)
```bash
rtk cargo build         # Cargo build output
rtk cargo check         # Cargo check output
rtk cargo clippy        # Clippy warnings grouped by file (80%)
rtk tsc                 # TypeScript errors grouped by file/code (83%)
rtk lint                # ESLint/Biome violations grouped (84%)
rtk prettier --check    # Files needing format only (70%)
rtk next build          # Next.js build with route metrics (87%)
```

### Test (60-99% savings)
```bash
rtk cargo test          # Cargo test failures only (90%)
rtk go test             # Go test failures only (90%)
rtk jest                # Jest failures only (99.5%)
rtk vitest              # Vitest failures only (99.5%)
rtk playwright test     # Playwright failures only (94%)
rtk pytest              # Python test failures only (90%)
rtk rake test           # Ruby test failures only (90%)
rtk rspec               # RSpec test failures only (60%)
rtk test <cmd>          # Generic test wrapper - failures only
```

### Git (59-80% savings)
```bash
rtk git status          # Compact status
rtk git log             # Compact log (works with all git flags)
rtk git diff            # Compact diff (80%)
rtk git show            # Compact show (80%)
rtk git add             # Ultra-compact confirmations (59%)
rtk git commit          # Ultra-compact confirmations (59%)
rtk git push            # Ultra-compact confirmations
rtk git pull            # Ultra-compact confirmations
rtk git branch          # Compact branch list
rtk git fetch           # Compact fetch
rtk git stash           # Compact stash
rtk git worktree        # Compact worktree
```

Note: Git passthrough works for ALL subcommands, even those not explicitly listed.

### GitHub (26-87% savings)
```bash
rtk gh pr view <num>    # Compact PR view (87%)
rtk gh pr checks        # Compact PR checks (79%)
rtk gh run list         # Compact workflow runs (82%)
rtk gh issue list       # Compact issue list (80%)
rtk gh api              # Compact API responses (26%)
```

### JavaScript/TypeScript Tooling (70-90% savings)
```bash
rtk pnpm list           # Compact dependency tree (70%)
rtk pnpm outdated       # Compact outdated packages (80%)
rtk pnpm install        # Compact install output (90%)
rtk npm run <script>    # Compact npm script output
rtk npx <cmd>           # Compact npx command output
rtk prisma              # Prisma without ASCII art (88%)
```

### Files & Search (60-75% savings)
```bash
rtk ls <path>           # Tree format, compact (65%)
rtk read <file>         # Code reading with filtering (60%)
rtk grep <pattern>      # Search grouped by file (75%). Format flags (-c, -l, -L, -o, -Z) run raw.
rtk find <pattern>      # Find grouped by directory (70%)
```

### Analysis & Debug (70-90% savings)
```bash
rtk err <cmd>           # Filter errors only from any command
rtk log <file>          # Deduplicated logs with counts
rtk json <file>         # JSON structure without values
rtk deps                # Dependency overview
rtk env                 # Environment variables compact
rtk summary <cmd>       # Smart summary of command output
rtk diff                # Ultra-compact diffs
```

### Infrastructure (85% savings)
```bash
rtk docker ps           # Compact container list
rtk docker images       # Compact image list
rtk docker logs <c>     # Deduplicated logs
rtk kubectl get         # Compact resource list
rtk kubectl logs        # Deduplicated pod logs
```

### Network (65-70% savings)
```bash
rtk curl <url>          # Compact HTTP responses (70%)
rtk wget <url>          # Compact download output (65%)
```

### Meta Commands
```bash
rtk gain                # View token savings statistics
rtk gain --history      # View command history with savings
rtk discover            # Analyze Claude Code sessions for missed RTK usage
rtk proxy <cmd>         # Run command without filtering (for debugging)
rtk init                # Add RTK instructions to CLAUDE.md
rtk init --global       # Add RTK to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
```

## Token Savings Overview

| Category | Commands | Typical Savings |
|----------|----------|-----------------|
| Tests | vitest, playwright, cargo test | 90-99% |
| Build | next, tsc, lint, prettier | 70-87% |
| Git | status, log, diff, add, commit | 59-80% |
| GitHub | gh pr, gh run, gh issue | 26-87% |
| Package Managers | pnpm, npm, npx | 70-90% |
| Files | ls, read, grep, find | 60-75% |
| Infrastructure | docker, kubectl | 85% |
| Network | curl, wget | 65-70% |

Overall average: **60-90% token reduction** on common development operations.
<!-- /rtk-instructions -->

# RTK - Rust Token Killer

**Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% savings on dev operations)

## Meta Commands (always use rtk directly)

```bash
rtk gain              # Show token savings analytics
rtk gain --history    # Show command usage history with savings
rtk discover          # Analyze Claude Code history for missed opportunities
rtk proxy <cmd>       # Execute raw command without filtering (for debugging)
```

## Installation Verification

```bash
rtk --version         # Should show: rtk X.Y.Z
rtk gain              # Should work (not "command not found")
which rtk             # Verify correct binary
```

⚠️ **Name collision**: If `rtk gain` fails, you may have reachingforthejack/rtk (Rust Type Kit) installed instead.

## Hook-Based Usage

All other commands are automatically rewritten by the Claude Code hook.
Example: `git status` → `rtk git status` (transparent, 0 tokens overhead)

Refer to CLAUDE.md for full command reference.

- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- r-audit: Run an external audit of a pqverse R package (MiscMetabar, tidypq, comparpq). Creates independent test data, runs all exported functions, and produces an R script and a TODO report of warnings, errors, and recommendations. Use this skill to stress-test a package with edge cases (NAs, empty samples, zero-count taxa, malformed trees).
- r-build: Build the pkgdown documentation website and the R package source tarball. Use this skill whenever the user wants to build their R package, rebuild the pkgdown site, regenerate documentation, build the README, lint the package.
- r-check: Style, document, and run R CMD check on the current R package, then propose fixes for any issues found. Use this skill whenever the user wants to check, validate, or verify their R package, run R CMD check, fix NOTEs/WARNINGs/ERRORs.
- r-feature-batch: Implement a batch of 3-4 ROADMAP features for a pqverse R package, one after another, then consolidate with full package hygiene (tests, NEWS bullets, _pkgdown.yml entries, regenerated man/, spelling, air format) and a single Quarto demo illustrating every new feature. Use this skill whenever the user wants to ship several roadmap features at once, batch-implement features, or do a feature sprint.
- r-pkg-bump-version: Bump an R package from a development version (X.Y.Z.9000) to a release version and finalize the NEWS.md heading. Use this skill whenever the user wants to bump the version of an R package for release, prepare DESCRIPTION + NEWS.md for CRAN, finalize the version before merging dev → master, or trigger Stage 8 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-merge-release: Merge an R package's dev branch into master, push, and bump dev to the next development cycle (X.Y.Z → X.Y.Z.9000). Use this skill after Stage 8 (version bump) when the user wants to finalize the release, merge dev into master, push to origin, start the next dev cycle, or trigger Stage 9 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-release: Full release pipeline for R packages: runs format, document, R CMD check (--as-cran), tests, optional coverage/audit/win-builder/rhub, CRAN extra checks, and builds the package. Supports --auto mode for autonomous runs that only stop for true ambiguity, with a resumable state file. file.content:
plot_ordination_pq <- function(
  physeq,
  method = "robust.aitchison",
  ordination_method = "NMDS",
  ...
) {
  verify_pq(physeq)
  physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq)
  dist_mat <- vegan::vegdist(unclass(physeq@otu_table), method = method)
  attr(dist_mat, "Labels") <- sample_names(physeq)
  p <- plot_ordination(
    physeq,
    ordination = ordinate(
      physeq,
      method = ordination_method,
      distance = dist_mat
    ),
    ...
  ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(ordination_method, "ordination"),
      subtitle = paste("Using ", method, "distance")
    )
  return(p)
}
################################################################################

################################################################################
# Default y-axis labels for common Hill orders (internal)
.hill_y_lab <- function(q) {
  labs <- c(
    "0" = "Richness (Hill q=0)",
    "1" = "Shannon diversity (Hill q=1)",
    "2" = "Simpson diversity (Hill q=2)"
  )
  lab <- labs[as.character(q)]
  if (is.na(lab)) paste0("Hill index (q=", q, ")") else unname(lab)
}

# Single-panel bar-plot engine used by hill_bar_pq() (internal)
.hill_bar_single <- function(
  data,
  x_name,
  y_name,
  fill_name,
  x_lab,
  y_lab,
  alpha,
  point_size,
  base_size,
  jitter_width,
  bar_width,
  add_letters,
  p_threshold,
  letter_size,
  letters_top_offset,
  y_lab_size,
  x_lab_size,
  show_n_samples,
  palette,

content.text:
I now have the grounding I need. Key constraints from the existing code:

- `hill_pq()` **coerces the modality to a factor** (`as.factor`) and runs Kruskal/Tukey — so it's built for discrete groups, not a numeric gradient. A numeric modality needs a different alpha plot (Hill ~ scatter + correlation).
- `venn_pq()`/`ggvenn_pq()` need ≥2 levels and are only legible up to ~4–5 sets; `upset_pq()` scales past that.
- `plot_ordination_pq()` colours by the modality (gradient if numeric, discrete if factor) — works either way.
- `umap_pq()` **returns a data.frame, not a plot** — we'd build the ggplot ourselves.

Let me get a second opinion on the design before I propose it to you.

You've done the right orientation and your read of the constraints is correct. Before you write the proposal, three things to fold in — one of them is a constraint the task doesn't state but will dominate the design.

**1. CRAN check-time is the unstated constraint that shapes the defaults.** This package was *archived* for blowing the 10-min budget, and your own CLAUDE.md names `umap_pq` as a per-sample-loop slow function on `data_fungi_mini` (137 samples). A function that fires hill + ordination + umap + venn/upset in one call is the single most expensive example in the package. So bake into the design now, not later:
- Heavy panels (umap especially) default **off**, opt-in via flags.
- The example uses a ≤5-sample subset and is wrapped `\donttest{}` (primary) with the umap variant in `\dontrun{}`.
- Don't let this become a function whose example you can't afford to run on CRAN.

This is the thing most likely to bite after the fact. Surface it to the user as a design driver, not an afterthought.

**2. The numeric-modality case is the real fork in your case matrix — pin it explicitly.** You already found that `hill_pq()` coerces to factor, so for a continuous variable it produces a nonsensical one-box-per-value plot. There is **no existing delegate** for "Hill ~ numeric gradient." So the case matrix isn't symmetric:
- numeric → needs a *new small helper* (Hill vs variable scatter + correlation/lm); ordination/umap colour as gradient; **no venn/upset**.
- factor → `hill_pq()` boxplot delegates cleanly; venn (≤4 levels) vs upset (>4) by your threshold.

That one new helper is the only genuinely new code; everything else is orchestration of existing functions. Frame the proposal that way — *thin orchestrator + one numeric-Hill helper* — so the user sees the surface area is small. The user's literal question "alpha div de hill (quelle fonction)" is answered by: `hill_pq()` for factor, custom scatter for numeric. Confirm that's what they want rather than forcing one function to do both.

**3. Don't co-design both functions in this pass.** The user said the diagnostic is "à compléter avec" — a complement, i.e. a follow-on. Settle `plot_overview_pq()` first. For the diagnostic, do a quick existence check *before* designing it: does MiscMetabar already have prevalence-vs-abundance / library-size building blocks (you've seen `accu_plot`, `ridges_pq`, `tax_bar_pq`, `summary_plot_pq`)? Reinventing a prevalence plot that already exists is the trap. And "controls?" is genuinely the user's call — identifying controls needs them to tell you the mechanism (a sam_data column? a name pattern?); flag it, don't guess.

**One convention nit:** for return type, mirror `hill_pq()`'s `one_plot` argument (list of panels vs assembled patchwork) rather than inventing a new pattern — it's the closest sibling and keeps the API consistent.

Net: your track is right. Present it as a case-matrix table, default the expensive panels off for CRAN reasons, call out the numeric helper as the one new piece, and hold the diagnostic for a second pass. Solid feedback. Here's my proposed design — a prose plan, with the one piece of genuinely new code flagged. I'm holding the diagnostic function for a second pass (you framed it as "à compléter avec", and identifying controls needs a decision from you).

---

## `plot_overview_pq()` — redesign

**Core idea:** a thin orchestrator that picks the right alpha + beta panels for **one** modality, branching on the modality's *type*. Almost everything delegates to existing functions; only one small helper is new.

### Case matrix

| Modality type | Alpha (Hill) | Set overlap | Beta ordination | UMAP (opt-in) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **numeric** | **new helper**: Hill ~ variable scatter + lm/correlation | — (skip; needs discrete sets) | `plot_ordination_pq()`, colour = gradient | gradient colour |
| **factor, 2–4 levels** | `hill_pq()` boxplot (Kruskal/Tukey) | `ggvenn_pq()` | `plot_ordination_pq()`, discrete colour | discrete colour |
| **factor, >4 levels** | `hill_pq()` boxplot | `upset_pq()` (venn illegible past ~4 sets) | `plot_ordination_pq()` | discrete colour |

The Venn↔UpSet switch is automatic on `nlevels`, with a `venn_max` threshold (default 4) the user can override.

### "alpha div de Hill — quelle fonction ?"

Two paths, because `hill_pq()` coerces its modality to a factor (one box per value → nonsense for a continuous variable):
- **factor** → `hill_pq()` as-is.
- **numeric** → a **new small internal helper** (`.hill_scatter` or similar): melts Hill q-values per sample, plots Hill vs the numeric variable, adds `geom_smooth(method="lm")` + correlation annotation. This is the *only* genuinely new code; everything else is orchestration.

### CRAN check-time (this drives the defaults)

The package was archived for the 10-min budget, and `umap_pq()` is explicitly flagged as slow on 137-sample data. So:
- **UMAP defaults OFF** (`add_umap = FALSE`); ordination and alpha default on.
- Example uses a ≤5-sample subset; primary example `\donttest{}`, the UMAP variant in `\dontrun{}`.

### Return type

Mirror `hill_pq()`'s existing `one_plot` argument: `one_plot = FALSE` returns a **named list of panels**; `one_plot = TRUE` assembles them with `patchwork`. Keeps the API consistent with its closest sibling.

### Draft signature

```r
plot_overview_pq(
  physeq,
  fact,                      # the single modality
  q = c(0, 1, 2),
  add_alpha = TRUE,
  add_ordination = TRUE,
  add_venn = TRUE,           # auto venn/upset; skipped if numeric
  add_umap = FALSE,          # off by default (CRAN cost)
  venn_max = 4,              # venn ≤ this many levels, else upset
  ordination_method = "PCoA",
  dist_method = "bray",
  one_plot = FALSE,
  ...
)
```

---

content.text:
All three settled. Two of your answers I'll honor with one concrete adjustment worth flagging:

- **Numeric alpha → scatter, no fit.** Clean.
- **UMAP on by default.** I'll set `add_umap = TRUE`. One consequence to handle: `umap::umap()` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`, so it errors on the ≤5-sample CRAN example, and it's the slow panel that got the package archived. So the function default stays `TRUE` (your runtime preference), but the **CRAN-run example** (`\donttest{}`) will call it with `add_umap = FALSE`, and a full-output variant with UMAP goes in `\dontrun{}`. That keeps the check budget safe without changing the default you picked.
- **Diagnostic function → second pass.** Held.

Let me ground the numeric-scatter helper: I need per-sample Hill values as a data frame. Let me check what's already available so I delegate rather than recompute.

Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] 34 #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`). 35 #' 36 #' @return A \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} object assembling the panels. 37 #' @export 38 #' @author Adrien Taudière 39 #' @details This function requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. 40 #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()] 41 #' 42 #' @examples 43 #' \donttest{ 44 #' if (requireNamespace("patchwork")) { 45 #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example) 46 #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) 47 #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] 48 #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] 49 #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) 50 #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) 51 #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height") 52 #' } 53 #' } 54 #' \dontrun{ 55 #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), 56 #' ordination_method = "NMDS", dist_method = "robust.aitchison") 57 #' } 58 plot_overview_pq <- function( 59 physeq, 60 fact, 61 q = c(0, 1, 2), 62 ordination_method = "PCoA", 63 dist_method = "bray", 64 add_alpha = TRUE, 65 add_ordination = TRUE, 66 ... 67 ) { 68 if (!requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { 69 cli::cli_abort( 70 "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required for {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." 71 ) 72 } 73 verify_pq(physeq) 74 75 if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { 76 cli::cli_abort( 77 "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." 78 ) 79 } 80 if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination) { 81 cli::cli_abort( 82 "At least one of {.arg add_alpha} or {.arg add_ordination} must be TRUE." 83 ) 84 } 85 86 plots <- list() 87 88 if (add_alpha) { 89 alpha_plots <- hill_pq( 90 physeq, 91 fact = fact, 92 q = q, 93 one_plot = FALSE, 94 plot_with_tuckey = FALSE 95 ) 96 plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) 97 } 98 99 if (add_ordination) { 100 plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( 101 physeq, 102 method = dist_method, 103 ordination_method = ordination_method, 104 color = fact 105 ) 106 } 107 108 patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...) 109 } 110 ################################################################################ 111

Used to color both the alpha boxplots and the #' ordination points. Must have at least two levels. #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders #' passed to [hill_pq()] (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, add the alpha-diversity #' (Hill numbers) boxplots. #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, add the #' beta-diversity ordination panel. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`). #' #' @return A \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} object assembling the panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details This function requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("patchwork")) { #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example) #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height") #' } #' } #' \dontrun{ #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), #' ordination_method = "NMDS", dist_method = "robust.aitchison") #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, ... ) { if (!requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required for {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha} or {.arg add_ordination} must be TRUE." ) } plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...) } ################################################################################

1398	    stop(
1399	      "The factor '",

1403	    )
1404	  }
1405

1408	    if (nsamples(physeq) - nsamples(new_physeq) > 0 && verbose) {
1409	      message(paste0(
1410	        nsamples(physeq) - nsamples(new_physeq),
1411	        " were discarded due to NA in variable fact"
1412	      ))
1413	    }
1414	    physeq <- new_physeq
1415	  }
1416	
1417	  physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq)
1418

1420	    if (as(rngseed, "logical")) {
1421	      set.seed(rngseed)

1423	        message(
1424	          "`set.seed(",

content.text: Both panels are ggplot/patchwork-compatible (`ggvenn_pq` → ggplot; `upset_pq` → `ComplexUpset` which is patchwork-based). Design is fully grounded. Let me write the redesigned function.

input.content: ################################################################################ # Hill numbers vs a numeric variable (internal helper for plot_overview_pq) # # Builds a per-sample Hill-number scatter plot against a continuous sample # variable, faceted by Hill order q. Mirrors the renyi orientation used in # hill_curves_pq(). No smoothing/fit is added (raw points only). .hill_scatter_pq <- function(physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), point_size = 2) { physeq <- taxa_as_rows(physeq) df_hill <- vegan::renyi(t(physeq)@otu_table, scales = q, hill = TRUE) if (is.null(dim(df_hill))) { df_hill <- data.frame(x = df_hill) colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) } else { df_hill <- as.data.frame(df_hill) colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) } df_hill$.sample <- rownames(df_hill) var_val <- as.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) names(var_val) <- sample_names(physeq) df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer( df_hill, cols = as.character(q), names_to = "q", values_to = "diversity" ) df_long[[fact]] <- var_val[df_long$.sample] df_long$q <- factor(df_long$q, levels = as.character(q)) ggplot(df_long, aes(x = .data[[fact]], y = .data[["diversity"]])) + geom_point(size = point_size) + facet_wrap( vars(.data[["q"]]), scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(function(x) { vapply(x, .hill_y_lab, character(1)) }) ) + labs(x = fact, y = "Hill diversity") } ################################################################################ ################################################################################ #' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Produce, in a single call, the main relevant graphical views of alpha- and #' beta-diversity for a phyloseq object as a function of **one** sample #' variable (`fact`). The set of panels adapts to the *type* of `fact`: #' #' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter #' plot against the variable (one facet per Hill order `q`); the ordination #' and UMAP points are coloured by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet panels #' are skipped (they require discrete groups). #' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity boxplots (via #' [hill_pq()]), a Venn diagram of shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an #' ordination and a UMAP coloured by the levels. #' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn #' diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays #' legible with many sets. #' #' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication #' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], #' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to fine-tune each panel. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of a sample variable present in the `sam_data` #' slot of `physeq`. Drives every panel. Numeric variables trigger the #' gradient/scatter behaviour; other variables are treated as a factor and #' must have at least two levels. #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders #' (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) Add the alpha-diversity panel #' (boxplots for a factor, scatter for a numeric variable). #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) Add the beta-diversity #' ordination panel. #' @param add_venn (logical, default TRUE) Add a Venn (or UpSet) panel of #' shared taxa across the levels of `fact`. Ignored when `fact` is numeric. #' @param add_umap (logical, default TRUE) Add a UMAP panel (via [umap_pq()]). #' This panel can be slow on datasets with many samples and may fail on very #' small ones (`umap` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`); set to FALSE to skip. #' @param venn_max (integer, default 4) Maximum number of levels for which a #' Venn diagram is drawn. Above this threshold an UpSet plot is used instead. #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). #' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, assemble the panels into a #' single figure with the \pkg{patchwork} package. If FALSE (default), return #' a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} objects (one entry per panel), letting the #' user arrange them freely. Mirrors the `one_plot` argument of [hill_pq()]. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`) when `one_plot = TRUE`. #' #' @return If `one_plot = FALSE` (default), a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} #' objects. If `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} #' object assembling the panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details The `one_plot = TRUE` path requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. #' Individual panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options #' used. #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], #' [umap_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example). #' # UMAP is disabled here (too few samples for the default n_neighbors). #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_umap = FALSE) #' } #' \dontrun{ #' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn). #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) #' #' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter + gradient-coloured ordination/UMAP, #' # Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, add_venn = TRUE, add_umap = TRUE, venn_max = 4, ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", one_plot = FALSE, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." ) } if (one_plot && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required when {.arg one_plot = TRUE} in {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) } is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { if (is_numeric_fact) { plots[["alpha"]] <- .hill_scatter_pq(physeq, fact = fact, q = q) } else { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } } if (add_venn) { if (is_numeric_fact) { cli::cli_inform( "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." ) } else { n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) if (n_lev <= venn_max) { plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } else { plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } } } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } if (add_umap) { df_umap <- umap_pq(physeq) plots[["umap"]] <- ggplot( df_umap, aes( x = .data[["x_umap"]], y = .data[["y_umap"]], color = .data[[fact]] ) ) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(title = "UMAP") } if (one_plot) { return(patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)) } plots } ################################################################################

Used to color both the alpha boxplots and the #' ordination points. Must have at least two levels. #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders #' passed to [hill_pq()] (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, add the alpha-diversity #' (Hill numbers) boxplots. #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, add the #' beta-diversity ordination panel. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`). #' #' @return A \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} object assembling the panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details This function requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("patchwork")) { #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example) #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height") #' } #' } #' \dontrun{ #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), #' ordination_method = "NMDS", dist_method = "robust.aitchison") #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, ... ) { if (!requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required for {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha} or {.arg add_ordination} must be TRUE." ) } plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...) } ################################################################################

file.content:
test_that("plot_overview_pq returns a patchwork combining alpha and beta", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("patchwork")
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  data(data_fungi_mini)
  sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
  hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
  lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
  ps <- clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
  p <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1))
  ))
  expect_s3_class(p, "patchwork")
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq can drop the ordination panel", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("patchwork")
  data(data_fungi_mini)
  sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
  hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
  lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
  ps <- clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
  p <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), add_ordination = FALSE)
  ))
  expect_s3_class(p, "patchwork")
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq aborts on an invalid factor", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("patchwork")
  data(data_fungi_mini)
  expect_error(
    plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "not_a_var"),
    "sam_data"
  )
})

input.content:
data(data_fungi_mini)

subset_pq <- function() {
  sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
  hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
  lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
  clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
}

test_that("plot_overview_pq returns a named list of ggplots by default", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_venn = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_type(res, "list")
  expect_true("ordination" %in% names(res))
  expect_true(all(vapply(res, ggplot2::is.ggplot, logical(1))))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq assembles a patchwork when one_plot = TRUE", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("patchwork")
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  p <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_venn = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE,
      one_plot = TRUE
    )
  ))
  expect_s3_class(p, "patchwork")
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq uses a Hill scatter and skips venn for numeric fact", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  expect_message(
    res <- suppressWarnings(
      plot_overview_pq(
        ps,
        fact = "Time",
        q = c(0, 1),
        add_umap = FALSE
      )
    ),
    "numeric"
  )
  expect_true("alpha" %in% names(res))
  expect_false(any(c("venn", "upset") %in% names(res)))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq draws a Venn for a factor within venn_max", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  skip_if_not_installed("ggVennDiagram")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_alpha = FALSE,
      add_ordination = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_true("venn" %in% names(res))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq switches to UpSet above venn_max", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  skip_if_not_installed("ComplexUpset")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      venn_max = 1,
      add_alpha = FALSE,
      add_ordination = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_true("upset" %in% names(res))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq aborts on an invalid factor", {
  skip_on_cran()
  data(data_fungi_mini)
  expect_error(
    plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "not_a_var"),
    "sam_data"
  )
})

content.content:
1	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
2	* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
3	* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.
4	* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
5	* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.
6	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
7	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
8	* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call diversity overview of a phyloseq object for a grouping factor, combining alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots and a beta-diversity ordination into one `patchwork` figure.
9	* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant.
10	* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
11	* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`.
12	
13	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
14	* Further check-time reductions for CRAN compliance:

file.content:
# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.
* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.
* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call diversity overview of a phyloseq object for a grouping factor, combining alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots and a beta-diversity ordination into one `patchwork` figure.
* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant.
* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`.

# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
* Further check-time reductions for CRAN compliance:

input.old_string: * `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call diversity overview of a phyloseq object for a grouping factor, combining alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots and a beta-diversity ordination into one `patchwork` figure.
input.new_string: * `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter for a numeric variable (gradient-coloured ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number boxplots plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.

toolUseResult.newString: * `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter for a numeric variable (gradient-coloured ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number boxplots plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.

lines: * `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.

* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.

* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.

-* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call diversity overview of a phyloseq object for a grouping factor, combining alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots and a beta-diversity ordination into one `patchwork` figure.

+* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter for a numeric variable (gradient-coloured ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number boxplots plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.

* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant.

* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`.

Mirrors the renyi orientation used in 6 # hill_curves_pq(). No smoothing/fit is added (raw points only). 7 .hill_scatter_pq <- function(physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), point_size = 2) { 8 physeq <- taxa_as_rows(physeq) 9 df_hill <- vegan::renyi(t(physeq)@otu_table, scales = q, hill = TRUE) 10 11 if (is.null(dim(df_hill))) { 12 df_hill <- data.frame(x = df_hill) 13 colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) 14 } else { 15 df_hill <- as.data.frame(df_hill) 16 colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) 17 } 18 df_hill$.sample <- rownames(df_hill) 19 20 var_val <- as.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) 21 names(var_val) <- sample_names(physeq) 22 23 df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer( 24 df_hill, 25 cols = as.character(q), 26 names_to = "q", 27 values_to = "diversity" 28 ) 29 df_long[[fact]] <- var_val[df_long$.sample] 30 df_long$q <- factor(df_long$q, levels = as.character(q)) 31 32 ggplot(df_long, aes(x = .data[[fact]], y = .data[["diversity"]])) + 33 geom_point(size = point_size) + 34 facet_wrap( 35 vars(.data[["q"]]), 36 scales = "free_y", 37 labeller = as_labeller(function(x) { 38 vapply(x, .hill_y_lab, character(1)) 39 }) 40 ) + 41 labs(x = fact, y = "Hill diversity") 42 } 43 ################################################################################ 44 45 ################################################################################ 46 #' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object 47 #' 48 #' @description 49 #' 50 #' 51 #' 52 #' 53 #' Produce, in a single call, the main relevant graphical views of alpha- and 54 #' beta-diversity for a phyloseq object as a function of **one** sample 55 #' variable (`fact`). The set of panels adapts to the *type* of `fact`: 56 #' 57 #' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter 58 #' plot against the variable (one facet per Hill order `q`); the ordination 59 #' and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet panels 60 #' are skipped (they require discrete groups). 61 #' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity boxplots (via 62 #' [hill_pq()]), a Venn diagram of shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an 63 #' ordination and a UMAP colored by the levels. 64 #' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn 65 #' diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays 66 #' legible with many sets. 67 #' 68 #' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication 69 #' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], 70 #' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to fine-tune each panel. 71 #' 72 #' @inheritParams clean_pq 73 #' @param fact (required) Name of a sample variable present in the `sam_data` 74 #' slot of `physeq`. Drives every panel. Numeric variables trigger the 75 #' gradient/scatter behaviour; other variables are treated as a factor and 76 #' must have at least two levels. 77 #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders 78 #' (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). 79 #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) Add the alpha-diversity panel 80 #' (boxplots for a factor, scatter for a numeric variable). 81 #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) Add the beta-diversity 82 #' ordination panel. 83 #' @param add_venn (logical, default TRUE) Add a Venn (or UpSet) panel of 84 #' shared taxa across the levels of `fact`. Ignored when `fact` is numeric. 85 #' @param add_umap (logical, default TRUE) Add a UMAP panel (via [umap_pq()]). 86 #' This panel can be slow on datasets with many samples and may fail on very 87 #' small ones (`umap` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`); set to FALSE to skip. 88 #' @param venn_max (integer, default 4) Maximum number of levels for which a 89 #' Venn diagram is drawn. Above this threshold an UpSet plot is used instead. 90 #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method 91 #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always 92 #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. 93 #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to 94 #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). 95 #' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, assemble the panels into a 96 #' single figure with the \pkg{patchwork} package. If FALSE (default), return 97 #' a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} objects (one entry per panel), letting the 98 #' user arrange them freely. Mirrors the `one_plot` argument of [hill_pq()]. 99 #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] 100 #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`) when `one_plot = TRUE`. 101 #' 102 #' @return If `one_plot = FALSE` (default), a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} 103 #' objects. If `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} 104 #' object assembling the panels. 105 #' @export 106 #' @author Adrien Taudière 107 #' @details The `one_plot = TRUE` path requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. 108 #' Individual panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), 109 #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options 110 #' used. 111 #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], 112 #' [umap_pq()] 113 #' 114 #' @examples 115 #' \donttest{ 116 #' #
