1	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
2	* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
3	* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.
4	* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
5	* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.
6	* `hill_bar_pq()` now sets the "Error bars / Tukey HSD" caption once for the whole figure (via `patchwork::plot_annotation()`) instead of repeating it on every `q` panel, fixing the overlapping captions seen with several values of `q`. Panels where Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons were not run (no global Kruskal-Wallis significance) are flagged with a `sig_symbol` (default `"†"`, new argument) appended to their subtitle and explained once in the shared caption.
7	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
8	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
9	* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter for a numeric variable (gradient-coloured ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number boxplots plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.
10	* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant.
11	* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
12	* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle, caption and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`.
13	
14	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
15	* Further check-time reductions for CRAN compliance:
16	  extra examples `\dontrun{}` (kept for documentation,
17	  not run during checks). 
18	* Added file-level `skip_on_cran()` to some heavy test files.
19	* Example speed-ups for some functions.
20	* Fix several bugs when using Windows paths by quoting system call arguments with `shQuote()`
21	
22	
23	# MiscMetabar 0.16.6 
24	* `verify_tax_table()` is now ~10× faster on full-size taxonomy tables.
25	* `divent_hill_matrix_pq()` no longer recomputes the per-sample
26	  positive-subset (`x <- x[x > 0]`) once per Hill order. The loop is
27	  now sample-outer / q-inner, so each row is sliced once. Numeric
28	  output is bitwise-identical. Speeds up every Hill-diversity
29	  computation in the package: `hill_pq()`, `hill_bar_pq()`,
30	  `hill_tuckey_pq()`, `profile_hill_pq()`, `psmelt_samples_pq()`,
31	  `plot_refseq_extremity_pq()`, and the `*_rarperm_pq` family.
32	* `circle_pq()` replaces a nested
33	  `pbapply(., 2, pbtapply(., group, sum))` over the OTU table with
34	  two `rowsum()` calls. On `data_fungi` (1420 taxa × 185 samples)
35	  the example dropped from ~18 s to ~1.8 s (≈ 10× faster). Output
36	  unchanged.
37	* `format2dada2(fasta_db = …)`, `hill_acc_pq(type = "sample")`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()` are also faster.
38	* New pkgdown article: `vignettes/articles/timing.Rmd` documents
39	  wall-clock cost of the main functions on `data_fungi` and
40	  `data_fungi_mini`, with a CSV refreshed by `inst/benchmark/function_timings.R`.
41	* Pkgdown articles use fewer permutations / simulations to keep the site build under a few minutes.
42	* Reduced `R CMD check` time to keep CRAN's 10-minute budget. Examples for
43	  `verify_tax_table()`, `adonis_pq()`, `plot_SCBD_pq()`, `multipatt_pq()`,
44	  `hill_pq()`, `plot_tsne_pq()`, `upset_test_pq()`, `summary_plot_pq()`,
45	  `ggvenn_pq()`, `plot_refseq_pq()`, `plot_seq_ratio_pq()`,
46	  `plot_refseq_extremity_pq()`, `glmutli_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`,
47	  `lefser_pq()`, `var_par_pq()`, `var_par_rarperm_pq()`,
48	  `taxa_only_in_one_level()`, `distri_1_taxa()`, `accu_plot_balanced_modality()`,
49	  `multi_biplot_pq()`, `tax_bar_pq()`, `plot_var_part_pq()`, `track_wkflow()`,
50	  `reorder_taxa_pq()` and `transform_pq()` now use `data_fungi_mini`
51	  (137 × 45) instead of the full `data_fungi` (185 × 1420), keeping behaviour
52	  identical but much faster.
53	* `hill_acc_pq()`, `iNEXT_pq()`, `format2dada2()` and `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`
54	  examples moved from `\donttest{}` to `\dontrun{}`. These functions are
55	  inherently CPU-bound (sample-based accumulation, fasta reformatting,
56	  permutation × rarefaction × q-loop) and were the largest individual
57	  contributors to the 10-min CRAN budget. Their behaviour is documented in
58	  the corresponding vignettes.
59	* `verify_pq()` example switched from `data_fungi` to `data_fungi_mini`
60	  (82 s → < 5 s).
61	* Tests: `plot_LCBD_pq()` / `LCBD_pq()` smoke tests in
62	  `tests/testthat/test_figures_beta_div.R` lowered `nperm` from 100 to 9
63	  (they only assert return class, not numeric stability).
64	* Function defaults (`nperm`, `n_permutations`) are unchanged.
65	
66	# MiscMetabar 0.16.5 
67	* `funguild_assign()` and `rotl_pq()` examples now use `\dontrun{}` instead of
68	  `\donttest{}`. Both examples call external APIs (`www.stbates.org` and the
69	  Open Tree of Life respectively) that are not always reachable during CRAN's
70	  `--run-donttest` check, causing spurious ERRORs.
71	* `verify_tax_table()`'s introductory example was moved inside the existing
72	  `\donttest{}` block. The call against the full `data_fungi` dataset took
73	  ~70 s, which triggered the CRAN "examples > 5 s" NOTE on every check.
74	* `XVector` removed from `DESCRIPTION` `Imports`. It was declared but never
75	  imported in `NAMESPACE` or used directly; `Biostrings` already loads it
76	  transitively. CRAN flagged this as "Namespace in Imports field not imported
77	  from".
78	* Bibliography: corrected the DOI for Taberlet et al. (2012) "Environmental
79	  DNA" in `paper/bibliography.bib`, `paper/paper.bib`, and the two
80	  `vignettes/*.bib` files (was the journal ISSN landing
81	  `10.1002/(issn)2637-4943`, now the paper DOI
82	  `10.1111/j.1365-294X.2012.05542.x`). `README.md` and the pkgdown site
83	  regenerate accordingly.
84	
85	# MiscMetabar 0.16.3
86	
87	* `verify_tax_table()` now recognises **non-breaking space (U+00A0)** and other
88	  Unicode separators (em space, ideographic space, ...) as border / internal
89	  whitespace. Previously the detection regex `^\s|\s$` (TRE) and the stripping

101	# 2. `plot_overview_pq()` — one-call alpha + beta overview
102	
103	## What it does
104	
105	`plot_overview_pq()` assembles, in a single call, a quick diversity overview
106	for a grouping factor: alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots (via `hill_pq()`)
107	next to a beta-diversity ordination (via `plot_ordination_pq()`), combined
108	with **patchwork**.
109

111	#| fig-width: 11
112	#| fig-height: 7

114	```
115	
116	The ordination defaults to `PCoA` (which always converges, unlike `NMDS` on
117	small/sparse data) on a Bray-Curtis distance; both are tunable. The
118	ordination panel can be dropped to keep only the alpha boxplots:

122	#| fig-height: 4

file.content:
---

# 2. `plot_overview_pq()` — one-call alpha + beta overview

## What it does

`plot_overview_pq()` assembles, in a single call, a quick diversity overview
for a grouping factor: alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots (via `hill_pq()`)
next to a beta-diversity ordination (via `plot_ordination_pq()`), combined
with **patchwork**.

```{r overview-default}
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height")
```

The ordination defaults to `PCoA` (which always converges, unlike `NMDS` on
small/sparse data) on a Bray-Curtis distance; both are tunable. The
ordination panel can be dropped to keep only the alpha boxplots:

```{r overview-alpha-only}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 4
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), add_ordination = FALSE)
```

Because the result is a `patchwork` object, the usual layout controls flow
through `...` (e.g. `ncol`, `guides`).

content.content:
44	
45	All per-sample examples use a 6-sample subset of the bundled
46	`data_fungi_mini` dataset (`dfm`).
47	
48	---
49	
50	# 1. `plot_volcano_pq()` — volcano plot for differential abundance
51	
52	## What it does
53	
54	A volcano plot puts the (log2) **fold change** on the x-axis and the
55	**statistical significance** (\(-\log_{10}\) of the adjusted p-value) on the
56	y-axis, so the most interesting taxa — large effect *and* strong support —
57	sit in the top corners. `plot_volcano_pq()` is column-name agnostic: point
58	`fc =` and `padj =` at the relevant columns of any differential-abundance
59	result table (`DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()`, `aldex_pq()`, …).
60	
61	```{r volcano-synthetic}
62	set.seed(42)
63	res <- data.frame(
64	  log2FoldChange = rnorm(300, sd = 2),
65	  padj = runif(300)^3,
66	  Genus = sample(paste0("Genus_", 1:25), 300, replace = TRUE)
67	)
68	plot_volcano_pq(res)
69	```
70	
71	Taxa are classified `Up` / `Down` / `NotDA` from the `alpha` (significance)
72	and `lfc_threshold` (effect-size) cut-offs, shown as dashed guides. We can
73	tighten the effect-size threshold and label the top significant taxa:
74	
75	```{r volcano-labelled}
76	plot_volcano_pq(
77	  res,
78	  lfc_threshold = 2,
79	  label_col = "Genus",
80	  label_n = 6
81	)
82	```
83	
84	Edge cases are handled: `padj == 0` is capped just above the largest finite
85	\(-\log_{10}\) value, and a missing `padj` is treated as `NotDA`.
86	
87	```{r volcano-edge}
88	res_edge <- data.frame(
89	  log2FoldChange = c(4, -4, 0.1, 2),
90	  padj = c(0, 1e-12, NA, 0.001)
91	)
92	plot_volcano_pq(res_edge)
93	```
94	
95	For a real analysis the input is simply the result object, e.g.
96	`plot_volcano_pq(DESeq2::results(dds))` — see `?plot_volcano_pq` for the
97	`DESeq2` / `ANCOMBC` / `ALDEx2` column mappings.
98

file.content:
All per-sample examples use a 6-sample subset of the bundled
`data_fungi_mini` dataset (`dfm`).

---

# 1. `plot_volcano_pq()` — volcano plot for differential abundance

## What it does

A volcano plot puts the (log2) **fold change** on the x-axis and the
**statistical significance** (\(-\log_{10}\) of the adjusted p-value) on the
y-axis, so the most interesting taxa — large effect *and* strong support —
sit in the top corners. `plot_volcano_pq()` is column-name agnostic: point
`fc =` and `padj =` at the relevant columns of any differential-abundance
result table (`DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()`, `aldex_pq()`, …).

```{r volcano-synthetic}
set.seed(42)
res <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = rnorm(300, sd = 2),
  padj = runif(300)^3,
  Genus = sample(paste0("Genus_", 1:25), 300, replace = TRUE)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res)
```

Taxa are classified `Up` / `Down` / `NotDA` from the `alpha` (significance)
and `lfc_threshold` (effect-size) cut-offs, shown as dashed guides. We can
tighten the effect-size threshold and label the top significant taxa:

```{r volcano-labelled}
plot_volcano_pq(
  res,
  lfc_threshold = 2,
  label_col = "Genus",
  label_n = 6
)
```

Edge cases are handled: `padj == 0` is capped just above the largest finite
\(-\log_{10}\) value, and a missing `padj` is treated as `NotDA`.

```{r volcano-edge}
res_edge <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = c(4, -4, 0.1, 2),
  padj = c(0, 1e-12, NA, 0.001)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res_edge)
```

For a real analysis the input is simply the result object, e.g.
`plot_volcano_pq(DESeq2::results(dds))` — see `?plot_volcano_pq` for the
`DESeq2` / `ANCOMBC` / `ALDEx2` column mappings.

content.content:
1	---
2	title: "MiscMetabar — Overview, volcano & MDT-CSV (dev 0.16.8.9000)"
3	author: "Adrien Taudière"
4	date: today
5	format:
6	  html:
7	    toc: true
8	    toc-depth: 3
9	    code-fold: show
10	    df-print: paged
11	    embed-resources: true
12	execute:
13	  warning: false
14	  message: false
15	editor_options:
16	  chunk_output_type: console
17	---
18	
19	```{r setup}
20	#| include: false
21	# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
22	# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
23	devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
24	library(ggplot2)
25	data(data_fungi_mini)
26	
27	# Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two `Height` levels, so every chunk runs
28	# fast (data_fungi_mini has 137 samples).
29	sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
30	hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
31	lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
32	dfm <- clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
33	```
34	
35	This document illustrates three features added to **MiscMetabar** during the
36	`0.16.8.9000` development cycle, all drawn from the *pqverse* `ROADMAP.md`

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
library(ggplot2)
data(data_fungi_mini)

# Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two `Height` levels, so every chunk runs
# fast (data_fungi_mini has 137 samples).
sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
dfm <- clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
```

This document illustrates three features added to **MiscMetabar** during the
`0.16.8.9000` development cycle, all drawn from the *pqverse* `ROADMAP.md`
(`MiscMetabar` section):

| Theme | Function | Roadmap tag |
|---|---|---|
| Visualisation | `plot_volcano_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Visualisation | `plot_overview_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Data I/O | `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` | Medium / easy |

content.text: The demo's 6-sample `dfm` would break the new `add_umap = TRUE` default (UMAP needs ≥16 samples). Rewriting the overview section to disable UMAP for the small subset and showcase the new adaptive behavior (factor→Venn, numeric→Hill scatter).

input.old_string:
# 2. `plot_overview_pq()` — one-call alpha + beta overview

## What it does

`plot_overview_pq()` assembles, in a single call, a quick diversity overview
for a grouping factor: alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots (via `hill_pq()`)
next to a beta-diversity ordination (via `plot_ordination_pq()`), combined
with **patchwork**.

```{r overview-default}
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height")
```

The ordination defaults to `PCoA` (which always converges, unlike `NMDS` on
small/sparse data) on a Bray-Curtis distance; both are tunable. The
ordination panel can be dropped to keep only the alpha boxplots:

```{r overview-alpha-only}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 4
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), add_ordination = FALSE)
```

Because the result is a `patchwork` object, the usual layout controls flow
through `...` (e.g. `ncol`, `guides`).
input.new_string:
# 2. `plot_overview_pq()` — one-call diversity overview, adapted to the variable

## What it does

`plot_overview_pq()` assembles, in a single call, the main alpha- and
beta-diversity views for **one** sample variable. Crucially, the panel set
adapts to the *type* of that variable:

- **Factor** → Hill-number **boxplots** (`hill_pq()`) + a **Venn** diagram of
  shared taxa (`ggvenn_pq()`, switched to an **UpSet** plot above `venn_max`
  levels) + an ordination + a UMAP, all keyed on the levels.
- **Numeric** → a Hill-number **scatter** against the variable (one facet per
  `q`) + gradient-colored ordination and UMAP. Venn/UpSet are skipped (they
  need discrete groups).

By default the panels come back as a **named list** of ggplots; pass
`one_plot = TRUE` to assemble them with **patchwork**.

A factor variable (`Height`, two levels here → Venn). UMAP is disabled because
this 6-sample subset has fewer samples than `umap`'s default `n_neighbors`:

```{r overview-factor}
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 8
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1),
  add_umap = FALSE, one_plot = TRUE)
```

The ordination defaults to `PCoA` (which always converges, unlike `NMDS` on
small/sparse data) on a Bray-Curtis distance; both are tunable via
`ordination_method` and `dist_method`.

A numeric variable (`Time`) switches the alpha panel to a Hill scatter and
drops the Venn panel automatically:

```{r overview-numeric}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Time", q = c(0, 1),
  add_umap = FALSE, one_plot = TRUE)
```

Any subset of panels can be requested with the `add_*` flags, and when
`one_plot = TRUE` the usual patchwork layout controls flow through `...`
(e.g. `ncol`, `guides`). With UMAP enabled on a larger dataset the call is
simply `plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact = "Height")`.

---
title: "MiscMetabar — Overview, volcano & MDT-CSV (dev 0.16.8.9000)"
author: "Adrien Taudière"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: show
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
library(ggplot2)
data(data_fungi_mini)

# Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two `Height` levels, so every chunk runs
# fast (data_fungi_mini has 137 samples).
sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
dfm <- clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
```

This document illustrates three features added to **MiscMetabar** during the
`0.16.8.9000` development cycle, all drawn from the *pqverse* `ROADMAP.md`
(`MiscMetabar` section):

| Theme | Function | Roadmap tag |
|---|---|---|
| Visualisation | `plot_volcano_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Visualisation | `plot_overview_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Data I/O | `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` | Medium / easy |

All per-sample examples use a 6-sample subset of the bundled
`data_fungi_mini` dataset (`dfm`).

---

# 1. `plot_volcano_pq()` — volcano plot for differential abundance

## What it does

A volcano plot puts the (log2) **fold change** on the x-axis and the
**statistical significance** (\(-\log_{10}\) of the adjusted p-value) on the
y-axis, so the most interesting taxa — large effect *and* strong support —
sit in the top corners. `plot_volcano_pq()` is column-name agnostic: point
`fc =` and `padj =` at the relevant columns of any differential-abundance
result table (`DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()`, `aldex_pq()`, …).

```{r volcano-synthetic}
set.seed(42)
res <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = rnorm(300, sd = 2),
  padj = runif(300)^3,
  Genus = sample(paste0("Genus_", 1:25), 300, replace = TRUE)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res)
```

Taxa are classified `Up` / `Down` / `NotDA` from the `alpha` (significance)
and `lfc_threshold` (effect-size) cut-offs, shown as dashed guides. We can
tighten the effect-size threshold and label the top significant taxa:

```{r volcano-labelled}
plot_volcano_pq(
  res,
  lfc_threshold = 2,
  label_col = "Genus",
  label_n = 6
)
```

Edge cases are handled: `padj == 0` is capped just above the largest finite
\(-\log_{10}\) value, and a missing `padj` is treated as `NotDA`.

```{r volcano-edge}
res_edge <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = c(4, -4, 0.1, 2),
  padj = c(0, 1e-12, NA, 0.001)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res_edge)
```

For a real analysis the input is simply the result object, e.g.
`plot_volcano_pq(DESeq2::results(dds))` — see `?plot_volcano_pq` for the
`DESeq2` / `ANCOMBC` / `ALDEx2` column mappings.

---

# 2. `plot_overview_pq()` — one-call alpha + beta overview

## What it does

`plot_overview_pq()` assembles, in a single call, a quick diversity overview
for a grouping factor: alpha-diversity Hill-number boxplots (via `hill_pq()`)
next to a beta-diversity ordination (via `plot_ordination_pq()`), combined
with **patchwork**.

```{r overview-default}
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height")
```

The ordination defaults to `PCoA` (which always converges, unlike `NMDS` on
small/sparse data) on a Bray-Curtis distance; both are tunable. The
ordination panel can be dropped to keep only the alpha boxplots:

```{r overview-alpha-only}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 4
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), add_ordination = FALSE)
```

Because the result is a `patchwork` object, the usual layout controls flow
through `...` (e.g. `ncol`, `guides`).

---

# 3. `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` — GBIF MDT template export (CSV/TSV)

## What it does

`phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` writes a phyloseq object to the plain-text
(CSV/TSV) version of the GBIF
[Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT)](https://mdt.gbif.org/) template — the
counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`. It produces **one file per table**
(OTU table, taxonomy, samples), each keyed by a leading `id` column, with the
OTU table in MDT orientation: **OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns**.

```{r mdt-csv}
out_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_demo")
files <- phyloseq_to_MDT_csv(dfm, path = out_dir, check_dwc = FALSE)
files
```

The OTU table has one row per taxon and one column per sample (plus `id`):

```{r mdt-csv-peek}
otu <- utils::read.csv(files[["OTU_table"]], check.names = FALSE)
otu[1:5, 1:4]
c(n_rows = nrow(otu), n_taxa = ntaxa(dfm),
  n_cols_minus_id = ncol(otu) - 1L, n_samples = nsamples(dfm))
```

Pass `sep = "\t"` for tab-separated `.tsv` files (the format favored by the
MDT validator), and keep the default `check_dwc = TRUE` to be warned about
recommended Darwin Core sample terms (`decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`,
`eventDate`) missing from `sample_data`.

```{r mdt-cleanup}
#| include: false
unlink(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
```

---

# Session info

```{r session}
sessionInfo()
```

attachment.snippet:
1	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
2	* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
3	* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.
4	* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
5	* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.
6	* `hill_bar_pq()` now sets the "Error bars / Tukey HSD" caption once for the whole figure (via `patchwork::plot_annotation()`) instead of repeating it on every `q` panel, fixing the overlapping captions seen with several values of `q`. Panels where Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons were not run (no global Kruskal-Wallis significance) are flagged with a `sig_symbol` (default `"∅"`, the empty set, new argument) appended to their subtitle and explained once in the shared caption.
7	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
8	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
9	* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number boxplots plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.
10	* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant.
11	* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
12	* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle, caption and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`.
13	
14	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
15	* Further check-time reductions for CRAN compliance:
16	  extra examples `\dontrun{}` (kept for documentation,
17	  not run during checks). 
18	* Added file-level `skip_on_cran()` to some heavy test files.
19	* Example speed-ups for some functions.
20	* Fix several bugs when using Windows paths by quoting system call arguments with `shQuote()`
21	
22	
23	# MiscMetabar 0.16.6 
24	* `verify_tax_table()` is now ~10× faster on full-size taxonomy tables.
25	* `divent_hill_matrix_pq()` no longer recomputes the per-sample
26	  positive-subset (`x <- x[x > 0]`) once per Hill order. The loop is
27	  now sample-outer / q-inner, so each row is sliced once. Numeric
28	  output is bitwise-identical. Speeds up every Hill-diversity
29	  computation in the package: `hill_pq()`, `hill_bar_pq()`,
30	  `hill_tuckey_pq()`, `profile_hill_pq()`, `psmelt_samples_pq()`,
31	  `plot_refseq_extremity_pq()`, and the `*_rarperm_pq` family.
32	* `circle_pq()` replaces a nested
33	  `pbapply(., 2, pbtapply(., group, sum))` over the OTU table with
34	  two `rowsum()` calls. On `data_fungi` (1420 taxa × 185 samples)
35	  the example dropped from ~18 s to ~1.8 s (≈ 10× faster). Output
36	  unchanged.
37	* `format2dada2(fasta_db = …)`, `hill_acc_pq(type = "sample")`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()` are also faster.
38	* New pkgdown article: `vignettes/articles/timing.Rmd` documents
39	  wall-clock cost of the main functions on `data_fungi` and
40	  `data_fungi_mini`, with a CSV refreshed by `inst/benchmark/function_timings.R`.
41	* Pkgdown articles use fewer permutations / simulations to keep the site build under a few minutes.
42	* Reduced `R CMD check` time to keep CRAN's 10-minute budget. Examples for
43	  `verify_tax_table()`, `adonis_pq()`, `plot_SCBD_pq()`, `multipatt_pq()`,
44	  `hill_pq()`, `plot_tsne_pq()`, `upset_test_pq()`, `summary_plot_pq()`,
45	  `ggvenn_pq()`, `plot_refseq_pq()`, `plot_seq_ratio_pq()`,
46	  `plot_refseq_extremity_pq()`, `glmutli_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`,
47	  `lefser_pq()`, `var_par_pq()`, `var_par_rarperm_pq()`,
48	  `taxa_only_in_one_level()`, `distri_1_taxa()`, `accu_plot_balanced_modality()`,
49	  `multi_biplot_pq()`, `tax_bar_pq()`, `plot_var_part_pq()`, `track_wkflow()`,
50	  `reorder_taxa_pq()` and `transform_pq()` now use `data_fungi_mini`
51	  (137 × 45) instead of the full `data_fungi` (185 × 1420), keeping behaviour
52	  identical but much faster.
53	* `hill_acc_pq()`, `iNEXT_pq()`, `format2dada2()` and `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`
54	  examples moved from `\donttest{}` to `\dontrun{}`. These functions are
55	  inherently CPU-bound (sample-based accumulation, fasta reformatting,
56	  permutation × rarefaction × q-loop) and were the largest individual
57	  contributors to the 10-min CRAN budget. Their behaviour is documented in
58	  the corresponding vignettes.
59	* `verify_pq()` example switched from `data_fungi` to `data_fungi_mini`
60	  (82 s → < 5 s).
61	* Tests: `plot_LCBD_pq()` / `LCBD_pq()` smoke tests in
62	  `tests/testthat/test_figures_beta_div.R` lowered `nperm` from 100 to 9
63	  (they only assert return class, not numeric stability).
64	* Function defaults (`nperm`, `n_permutations`) are unchanged.
65	
66	# MiscMetabar 0.16.5 
67	* `funguild_assign()` and `rotl_pq()` examples now use `\dontrun{}` instead of
68	  `\donttest{}`. Both examples call external APIs (`www.stbates.org` and the
69	  Open Tree of Life respectively) that are not always reachable during CRAN's
70	  `--run-donttest` check, causing spurious ERRORs.
71	* `verify_tax_table()`'s introductory example was moved inside the existing
72	  `\donttest{}` block. The call against the full `data_fungi` dataset took
73	  ~70 s, which triggered the CRAN "examples > 5 s" NOTE on every check.
74	* `XVector` removed from `DESCRIPTION` `Imports`. It was declared but never
75	  imported in `NAMESPACE` or used directly; `Biostrings` already loads it
76	  transitively. CRAN flagged this as "Namespace in Imports field not imported
77	  from".
78	* Bibliography: corrected the DOI for Taberlet et al. (2012) "Environmental
79	  DNA" in `paper/bibliography.bib`, `paper/paper.bib`, and the two
80	  `vignettes/*.bib` files (was the journal ISSN landing
81	  `10.1002/(issn)2637-4943`, now the paper DOI
82	  `10.1111/j.1365-294X.2012.05542.x`). `README.md` and the pkgdown site
83	  regenerate accordingly.
84	
85	# MiscMetabar 0.16.3
86	
87	* `verify_tax_table()` now recognises **non-breaking space (U+00A0)** and other
88	  Unicode separators (em space, ideographic space, ...) as border / internal
89	  whitespace. Previously the detection regex `^\s|\s$` (TRE) and the stripping

input.command:
NOT_CRAN=true Rscript -e '
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all(quiet=TRUE))
data(data_fungi_mini)
sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]

title: "MiscMetabar — Overview, volcano & MDT-CSV (dev 0.16.8.9000)"
author: "Adrien Taudière"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: show
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
library(ggplot2)
data(data_fungi_mini)

# Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two `Height` levels, so every chunk runs
# fast (data_fungi_mini has 137 samples).
sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
dfm <- clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
```

This document illustrates three features added to **MiscMetabar** during the
`0.16.8.9000` development cycle, all drawn from the *pqverse* `ROADMAP.md`
(`MiscMetabar` section):

| Theme | Function | Roadmap tag |
|---|---|---|
| Visualisation | `plot_volcano_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Visualisation | `plot_overview_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Data I/O | `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` | Medium / easy |

All per-sample examples use a 6-sample subset of the bundled
`data_fungi_mini` dataset (`dfm`).

---

# 1. `plot_volcano_pq()` — volcano plot for differential abundance

## What it does

A volcano plot puts the (log2) **fold change** on the x-axis and the
**statistical significance** (\(-\log_{10}\) of the adjusted p-value) on the
y-axis, so the most interesting taxa — large effect *and* strong support —
sit in the top corners. `plot_volcano_pq()` is column-name agnostic: point
`fc =` and `padj =` at the relevant columns of any differential-abundance
result table (`DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()`, `aldex_pq()`, …).

```{r volcano-synthetic}
set.seed(42)
res <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = rnorm(300, sd = 2),
  padj = runif(300)^3,
  Genus = sample(paste0("Genus_", 1:25), 300, replace = TRUE)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res)
```

Taxa are classified `Up` / `Down` / `NotDA` from the `alpha` (significance)
and `lfc_threshold` (effect-size) cut-offs, shown as dashed guides. We can
tighten the effect-size threshold and label the top significant taxa:

```{r volcano-labelled}
plot_volcano_pq(
  res,
  lfc_threshold = 2,
  label_col = "Genus",
  label_n = 6
)
```

Edge cases are handled: `padj == 0` is capped just above the largest finite
\(-\log_{10}\) value, and a missing `padj` is treated as `NotDA`.

```{r volcano-edge}
res_edge <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = c(4, -4, 0.1, 2),
  padj = c(0, 1e-12, NA, 0.001)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res_edge)
```

For a real analysis the input is simply the result object, e.g.
`plot_volcano_pq(DESeq2::results(dds))` — see `?plot_volcano_pq` for the
`DESeq2` / `ANCOMBC` / `ALDEx2` column mappings.

---

# 2. `plot_overview_pq()` — one-call diversity overview, adapted to the variable

## What it does

`plot_overview_pq()` assembles, in a single call, the main alpha- and
beta-diversity views for **one** sample variable. Crucially, the panel set
adapts to the *type* of that variable:

- **Factor** → Hill-number **boxplots** (`hill_pq()`) + a **Venn** diagram of
  shared taxa (`ggvenn_pq()`, switched to an **UpSet** plot above `venn_max`
  levels) + an ordination + a UMAP, all keyed on the levels.
- **Numeric** → a Hill-number **scatter** against the variable (one facet per
  `q`) + gradient-colored ordination and UMAP. Venn/UpSet are skipped (they
  need discrete groups).

By default the panels come back as a **named list** of ggplots; pass
`one_plot = TRUE` to assemble them with **patchwork**.

A factor variable (`Height`, two levels here → Venn). UMAP is disabled because
this 6-sample subset has fewer samples than `umap`'s default `n_neighbors`:

```{r overview-factor}
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 8
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1),
  add_umap = FALSE, one_plot = TRUE)
```

The ordination defaults to `PCoA` (which always converges, unlike `NMDS` on
small/sparse data) on a Bray-Curtis distance; both are tunable via
`ordination_method` and `dist_method`.

A numeric variable (`Time`) switches the alpha panel to a Hill scatter and
drops the Venn panel automatically:

```{r overview-numeric}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Time", q = c(0, 1),
  add_umap = FALSE, one_plot = TRUE)
```

Any subset of panels can be requested with the `add_*` flags, and when
`one_plot = TRUE` the usual patchwork layout controls flow through `...`
(e.g. `ncol`, `guides`). With UMAP enabled on a larger dataset the call is
simply `plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact = "Height")`.

---

# 3. `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` — GBIF MDT template export (CSV/TSV)

## What it does

`phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` writes a phyloseq object to the plain-text
(CSV/TSV) version of the GBIF
[Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT)](https://mdt.gbif.org/) template — the
counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`. It produces **one file per table**
(OTU table, taxonomy, samples), each keyed by a leading `id` column, with the
OTU table in MDT orientation: **OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns**.

```{r mdt-csv}
out_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_demo")
files <- phyloseq_to_MDT_csv(dfm, path = out_dir, check_dwc = FALSE)
files
```

The OTU table has one row per taxon and one column per sample (plus `id`):

```{r mdt-csv-peek}
otu <- utils::read.csv(files[["OTU_table"]], check.names = FALSE)
otu[1:5, 1:4]
c(n_rows = nrow(otu), n_taxa = ntaxa(dfm),
  n_cols_minus_id = ncol(otu) - 1L, n_samples = nsamples(dfm))
```

Pass `sep = "\t"` for tab-separated `.tsv` files (the format favored by the
MDT validator), and keep the default `check_dwc = TRUE` to be warned about
recommended Darwin Core sample terms (`decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`,
`eventDate`) missing from `sample_data`.

```{r mdt-cleanup}
#| include: false
unlink(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
```

---

# Session info

```{r session}
sessionInfo()
```

content.text: Rendered successfully (the remaining lints are trivial line-length hints in the WIP demo, not the package). Deliverables are written. Let me get a final review before declaring done.

You're at the review stage and your testing is solid — but notice one thing about your own test/example suite: **every single test and example sets `add_umap = FALSE`.** The one behavior you made the default (`add_umap = TRUE`) is the one path your checks never exercise in its failure mode. That's the classic blind spot — your verification differs from real default usage.

**The finding that matters (decide before you call it done):**

With `add_umap = TRUE` as the default, the bare call `plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact)` hard-fails in two common cases:
- `umap` not installed → `umap::umap` errors (no `requireNamespace` guard, unlike the patchwork one you *did* write).
- `nsamples < n_neighbors` (default 15) → umap errors. Lots of real metabarcoding datasets, and `data_fungi_mini` subsets, are under 16 samples.

So your default path aborts the *entire* overview because one optional panel can't render. That contradicts the package's silent/graceful convention and your own patchwork-guard pattern. The discriminating question: **what should `plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact)` do on a 12-sample dataset without `umap` installed — abort, or return the alpha/venn/ordination panels and skip umap with a message?** I'd guard it (skip-with-`cli_inform` when umap is unavailable or `nsamples <= n_neighbors`), which preserves the user's "on by default" intent without making the default brittle. 

Does it block? It doesn't block the feature *working* when conditions are right, but it makes the default fragile in exactly the conditions this package runs in — I'd treat it as fix-before-done, not ship-as-is. Quick verify either way: run `plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height")` with the real default (no `add_umap` arg) on your 6-sample `dfm` and watch what a user actually gets.

**Minor (optional):** Your NEWS bullets are now mis-ordered — the developer's concurrent `hill_bar_pq()` bullet sits *after* `is_blastn_installed` (line 6 vs 5). Reordering two adjacent bullets isn't reverting their content, it's the alphabetical hygiene the skill mandates. Your instinct not to touch their *content* is right; nudging order is fine. Skip if you'd rather not.

**Everything else checks out** — the renyi orientation mirrors `hill_curves_pq` correctly, the venn↔upset switch and numeric-skip logic are tested, and the full-render of the demo (EXIT:0) genuinely exercised the venn-in-patchwork and numeric-ordination-gradient paths that your unit tests alone didn't build. Don't re-verify those.

**One framing note:** the user opened with "let's *talk* about how to implement and *plan*." You planned, got concrete answers, and built the whole thing — defensible, since they answered all three design questions. But present this back as "here's the implementation per your answers, review it," not "done" — and explicitly resurface the deferred second function (the quality/diagnostic `*_pq`), since that was half their vision. Leave it uncommitted (you have).

The advisor caught a real fragility: with `add_umap = TRUE` as default, the bare call aborts the whole overview when `umap` is missing or `nsamples < n_neighbors` — common in this domain. Let me verify the failure, then guard it.

toolUseResult.originalFile: ################################################################################ # Hill numbers vs a numeric variable (internal helper for plot_overview_pq) # # Builds a per-sample Hill-number scatter plot against a continuous sample # variable, faceted by Hill order q. Mirrors the renyi orientation used in # hill_curves_pq(). No smoothing/fit is added (raw points only). .hill_scatter_pq <- function(physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), point_size = 2) { physeq <- taxa_as_rows(physeq) df_hill <- vegan::renyi(t(physeq)@otu_table, scales = q, hill = TRUE) if (is.null(dim(df_hill))) { df_hill <- data.frame(x = df_hill) colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) } else { df_hill <- as.data.frame(df_hill) colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) } df_hill$.sample <- rownames(df_hill) var_val <- as.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) names(var_val) <- sample_names(physeq) df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer( df_hill, cols = as.character(q), names_to = "q", values_to = "diversity" ) df_long[[fact]] <- var_val[df_long$.sample] df_long$q <- factor(df_long$q, levels = as.character(q)) ggplot(df_long, aes(x = .data[[fact]], y = .data[["diversity"]])) + geom_point(size = point_size) + facet_wrap( vars(.data[["q"]]), scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(function(x) { vapply(x, .hill_y_lab, character(1)) }) ) + labs(x = fact, y = "Hill diversity") } ################################################################################ ################################################################################ #' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Produce, in a single call, the main relevant graphical views of alpha- and #' beta-diversity for a phyloseq object as a function of **one** sample #' variable (`fact`). The set of panels adapts to the *type* of `fact`: #' #' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter #' plot against the variable (one facet per Hill order `q`); the ordination #' and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet panels #' are skipped (they require discrete groups). #' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity boxplots (via #' [hill_pq()]), a Venn diagram of shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an #' ordination and a UMAP colored by the levels. #' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn #' diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays #' legible with many sets. #' #' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication #' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], #' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to fine-tune each panel. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of a sample variable present in the `sam_data` #' slot of `physeq`. Drives every panel. Numeric variables trigger the #' gradient/scatter behavior; other variables are treated as a factor and #' must have at least two levels. #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders #' (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) Add the alpha-diversity panel #' (boxplots for a factor, scatter for a numeric variable). #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) Add the beta-diversity #' ordination panel. #' @param add_venn (logical, default TRUE) Add a Venn (or UpSet) panel of #' shared taxa across the levels of `fact`. Ignored when `fact` is numeric. #' @param add_umap (logical, default TRUE) Add a UMAP panel (via [umap_pq()]). #' This panel can be slow on datasets with many samples and may fail on very #' small ones (`umap` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`); set to FALSE to skip. #' @param venn_max (integer, default 4) Maximum number of levels for which a #' Venn diagram is drawn. Above this threshold an UpSet plot is used instead. #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). #' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, assemble the panels into a #' single figure with the \pkg{patchwork} package. If FALSE (default), return #' a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} objects (one entry per panel), letting the #' user arrange them freely. Mirrors the `one_plot` argument of [hill_pq()]. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`) when `one_plot = TRUE`. #' #' @return If `one_plot = FALSE` (default), a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} #' objects. If `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} #' object assembling the panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details The `one_plot = TRUE` path requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. #' Individual panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options #' used. #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], #' [umap_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example). #' # UMAP is disabled here (too few samples for the default n_neighbors). #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_umap = FALSE) #' } #' \dontrun{ #' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn). #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) #' #' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter + gradient-colored ordination/UMAP, #' # Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, add_venn = TRUE, add_umap = TRUE, venn_max = 4, ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", one_plot = FALSE, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." ) } if (one_plot && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required when {.arg one_plot = TRUE} in {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) } is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { if (is_numeric_fact) { plots[["alpha"]] <- .hill_scatter_pq(physeq, fact = fact, q = q) } else { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } } if (add_venn) { if (is_numeric_fact) { cli::cli_inform( "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." ) } else { n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) if (n_lev <= venn_max) { plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } else { plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } } } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } if (add_umap) { df_umap <- umap_pq(physeq) plots[["umap"]] <- ggplot( df_umap, aes( x = .data[["x_umap"]], y = .data[["y_umap"]], color = .data[[fact]] ) ) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(title = "UMAP") } if (one_plot) { return(patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)) } plots } ################################################################################

toolUseResult.oldString: test_that("plot_overview_pq aborts on an invalid factor", {
toolUseResult.originalFile:
data(data_fungi_mini)

subset_pq <- function() {
  sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
  hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
  lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
  clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
}

test_that("plot_overview_pq returns a named list of ggplots by default", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_venn = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_type(res, "list")
  expect_true("ordination" %in% names(res))
  expect_true(all(vapply(res, ggplot2::is.ggplot, logical(1))))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq assembles a patchwork when one_plot = TRUE", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("patchwork")
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  p <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_venn = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE,
      one_plot = TRUE
    )
  ))
  expect_s3_class(p, "patchwork")
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq uses a Hill scatter and skips venn for numeric fact", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  expect_message(
    res <- suppressWarnings(
      plot_overview_pq(
        ps,
        fact = "Time",
        q = c(0, 1),
        add_umap = FALSE
      )
    ),
    "numeric"
  )
  expect_true("alpha" %in% names(res))
  expect_false(any(c("venn", "upset") %in% names(res)))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq draws a Venn for a factor within venn_max", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  skip_if_not_installed("ggVennDiagram")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_alpha = FALSE,
      add_ordination = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_true("venn" %in% names(res))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq switches to UpSet above venn_max", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  skip_if_not_installed("ComplexUpset")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      venn_max = 1,
      add_alpha = FALSE,
      add_ordination = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_true("upset" %in% names(res))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq aborts on an invalid factor", {
  skip_on_cran()
  data(data_fungi_mini)
  expect_error(
    plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "not_a_var"),
    "sam_data"
  )
})

lines: * Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.

* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.

* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
-* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.

* `hill_bar_pq()` now sets the "Error bars / Tukey HSD" caption once for the whole figure (via `patchwork::plot_annotation()`) instead of repeating it on every `q` panel, fixing the overlapping captions seen with several values of `q`. Panels where Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons were not run (no global Kruskal-Wallis significance) are flagged with a `sig_symbol` (default `"∅"`, the empty set, new argument) appended to their subtitle and explained once in the shared caption.

* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.

* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.

Panels where Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons were not run (no global Kruskal-Wallis significance) are flagged with a `sig_symbol` (default `"∅"`, the empty set, new argument) appended to their subtitle and explained once in the shared caption.
6	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
7	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
8	* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number boxplots plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.

content.text:
Done — `plot_overview_pq()` is redesigned per your answers and left uncommitted for review. Here's what it does now.

## `plot_overview_pq()` — implementation summary

**Adapts the panel set to the modality type**, exactly as you sketched:

| `fact` type | Alpha | Set overlap | Beta | UMAP |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **numeric** | Hill **scatter** vs the variable, faceted by `q`, raw points (no fit) | skipped (with a message) | ordination, gradient colour | gradient colour |
| **factor, ≤ `venn_max` (4)** | `hill_pq()` boxplots | `ggvenn_pq()` Venn | ordination | discrete colour |
| **factor, > `venn_max`** | `hill_pq()` boxplots | `upset_pq()` UpSet | ordination | discrete colour |

- **`alpha div de Hill — quelle fonction`**: `hill_pq()` for factors; a new internal helper `.hill_scatter_pq()` (using `vegan::renyi(..., hill=TRUE)`, same orientation as `hill_curves_pq()`) for the numeric case — that's the only genuinely new computation.
- **Return type** mirrors `hill_pq()`'s `one_plot`: named list of ggplots by default, or a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.
- **UMAP on by default** (your choice) — but **guarded**: it skips with a message when `umap` isn't installed or there are ≤15 samples (the `n_neighbors = 15` floor), so the bare call `plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact)` never aborts the whole overview just because one panel can't render. The advisor flagged that the default would otherwise hard-fail on small datasets — verified and fixed.

## Validation
- **13 tests pass** (`test_plot_overview_pq.R`): list/patchwork return, numeric→scatter+venn-skip, venn↔upset switch, graceful UMAP skip, invalid-factor abort.
- Demo `arround_MiscMetabar/overview_volcano_mdt_demo.qmd` updated (factor and numeric examples) and **re-rendered end-to-end** (exit 0).
- Docs regenerated, `air format` clean, spelling clean (`UpSet` added to WORDLIST), NEWS bullet rewritten and re-alphabetised.
- **R CMD check not run** — that's the heavy step; run `/r-check` when you want the full CRAN pass.

## Still open — the second function
You wanted this *complemented by* a separate **quality/diagnostic** function (low-frequency / low-abundance taxa, low seq- or taxa-counts per sample, controls). I held it for a second pass as you framed it. Two things I'll need from you before designing it: (1) is there existing prevalence/library-size building blocks I should reuse, and (2) **how are controls identified** — a `sam_data` column, or a name pattern? Say the word and we'll plan that one next.

hill_bar_pq <- function(
  physeq,
  x,
  q = c(0, 2),
  fill,
  x_lab = NULL,
  y_labs = NULL,
  ncol = NULL,
  alpha = 0.6,
  point_size = 3,
  base_size = 13,
  jitter_width = 0.15,
  bar_width = 0.7,
  add_letters = TRUE,
  p_threshold = 0.05,
  letter_size = 5,
  letters_top_offset = 0.05,
  y_lab_size = NULL,
  x_lab_size = NULL,
  show_n_samples = TRUE,
  palette = c(
    "#E69F00",
    "#56B4E9",
    "#009E73",
    "#F0E442",
    "#0072B2",
    "#D55E00",
    "#CC79A7",
    "#000000"
  ),
  error_fun = function(x) {
    m <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    se <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(x)))
    c(lower = m - se, upper = m + se)
  },
  error_fun_lab = "mean \u00b1 SE",
  error_bar_alpha = 0.35,
  point_alpha = 0.5,
  letters_below_bar = FALSE,
  sig_symbol = "\u2205",
  ...
) {
  verify_pq(physeq)

  data <- psmelt_samples_pq(physeq, q = q, ...)

  x_var <- rlang::ensym(x)
  fill_var <- if (missing(fill)) x_var else rlang::ensym(fill)
  x_name <- rlang::as_string(x_var)
  fill_name <- rlang::as_string(fill_var)
  x_lab <- if (is.null(x_lab)) x_name else x_lab

  ys <- paste0("Hill_", q)

  plot_args <- list(
    data = data,
    x_name = x_name,
    fill_name = fill_name,
    x_lab = x_lab,
    alpha = alpha,

################################################################################
# Hill numbers vs a numeric variable (internal helper for plot_overview_pq)
#
# Builds a per-sample Hill-number scatter plot against a continuous sample
# variable, faceted by Hill order q. Mirrors the renyi orientation used in
# hill_curves_pq(). No smoothing/fit is added (raw points only).
.hill_scatter_pq <- function(physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), point_size = 2) {
  physeq <- taxa_as_rows(physeq)
  df_hill <- vegan::renyi(t(physeq)@otu_table, scales = q, hill = TRUE)

  if (is.null(dim(df_hill))) {
    df_hill <- data.frame(x = df_hill)
    colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q)
  } else {
    df_hill <- as.data.frame(df_hill)
    colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q)
  }
  df_hill$.sample <- rownames(df_hill)

  var_val <- as.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])
  names(var_val) <- sample_names(physeq)

  df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(
    df_hill,
    cols = as.character(q),
    names_to = "q",
    values_to = "diversity"
  )
  df_long[[fact]] <- var_val[df_long$.sample]
  df_long$q <- factor(df_long$q, levels = as.character(q))

  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = .data[[fact]], y = .data[["diversity"]])) +
    geom_point(size = point_size) +
    facet_wrap(
      vars(.data[["q"]]),
      scales = "free_y",
      labeller = as_labeller(function(x) {
        vapply(x, .hill_y_lab, character(1))
      })
    ) +
    labs(x = fact, y = "Hill diversity")
}
################################################################################

################################################################################
#' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object
#'
#' @description
#'
#' <a href="https://adrientaudiere.github.io/MiscMetabar/articles/Rules.html#lifecycle">

No smoothing/fit is added (raw points only). .hill_scatter_pq <- function(physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), point_size = 2) { physeq <- taxa_as_rows(physeq) df_hill <- vegan::renyi(t(physeq)@otu_table, scales = q, hill = TRUE) if (is.null(dim(df_hill))) { df_hill <- data.frame(x = df_hill) colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) } else { df_hill <- as.data.frame(df_hill) colnames(df_hill) <- as.character(q) } df_hill$.sample <- rownames(df_hill) var_val <- as.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) names(var_val) <- sample_names(physeq) df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer( df_hill, cols = as.character(q), names_to = "q", values_to = "diversity" ) df_long[[fact]] <- var_val[df_long$.sample] df_long$q <- factor(df_long$q, levels = as.character(q)) ggplot(df_long, aes(x = .data[[fact]], y = .data[["diversity"]])) + geom_point(size = point_size) + facet_wrap( vars(.data[["q"]]), scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(function(x) { vapply(x, .hill_y_lab, character(1)) }) ) + labs(x = fact, y = "Hill diversity") } ################################################################################ ################################################################################ #' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Produce, in a single call, the main relevant graphical views of alpha- and #' beta-diversity for a phyloseq object as a function of **one** sample #' variable (`fact`). The set of panels adapts to the *type* of `fact`: #' #' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter #' plot against the variable (one facet per Hill order `q`); the ordination #' and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet panels #' are skipped (they require discrete groups). #' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity boxplots (via #' [hill_pq()]), a Venn diagram of shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an #' ordination and a UMAP colored by the levels. #' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn #' diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays #' legible with many sets. #' #' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication #' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], #' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to fine-tune each panel. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of a sample variable present in the `sam_data` #' slot of `physeq`. Drives every panel. Numeric variables trigger the #' gradient/scatter behavior; other variables are treated as a factor and #' must have at least two levels. #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders #' (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) Add the alpha-diversity panel #' (boxplots for a factor, scatter for a numeric variable). #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) Add the beta-diversity #' ordination panel. #' @param add_venn (logical, default TRUE) Add a Venn (or UpSet) panel of #' shared taxa across the levels of `fact`. Ignored when `fact` is numeric. #' @param add_umap (logical, default TRUE) Add a UMAP panel (via [umap_pq()]). #' This panel can be slow on datasets with many samples. It is skipped (with #' a message) when the \pkg{umap} package is not installed or when there are #' 15 samples or fewer (`umap` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`, which requires #' more samples than that); set to FALSE to skip it explicitly. #' @param venn_max (integer, default 4) Maximum number of levels for which a #' Venn diagram is drawn. Above this threshold an UpSet plot is used instead. #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). #' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, assemble the panels into a #' single figure with the \pkg{patchwork} package. If FALSE (default), return #' a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} objects (one entry per panel), letting the #' user arrange them freely. Mirrors the `one_plot` argument of [hill_pq()]. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`) when `one_plot = TRUE`. #' #' @return If `one_plot = FALSE` (default), a named list of \pkg{ggplot2} #' objects. If `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} #' object assembling the panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details The `one_plot = TRUE` path requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. #' Individual panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options #' used. #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], #' [umap_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example). #' # UMAP is disabled here (too few samples for the default n_neighbors). #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_umap = FALSE) #' } #' \dontrun{ #' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn). #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) #' #' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter + gradient-colored ordination/UMAP, #' # Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, add_venn = TRUE, add_umap = TRUE, venn_max = 4, ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", one_plot = FALSE, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." ) } if (one_plot && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required when {.arg one_plot = TRUE} in {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) } is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { if (is_numeric_fact) { plots[["alpha"]] <- .hill_scatter_pq(physeq, fact = fact, q = q) } else { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } } if (add_venn) { if (is_numeric_fact) { cli::cli_inform( "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." ) } else { n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) if (n_lev <= venn_max) { plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } else { plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } } } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } if (add_umap) { # umap::umap defaults to n_neighbors = 15, which requires > 15 samples. if (!requireNamespace("umap", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: package {.pkg umap} is not installed." ) } else if (nsamples(physeq) <= 15) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: {nsamples(physeq)} samples are too few for the default {.code n_neighbors = 15} of {.fn umap::umap}." ) } else { df_umap <- umap_pq(physeq) plots[["umap"]] <- ggplot( df_umap, aes( x = .data[["x_umap"]], y = .data[["y_umap"]], color = .data[[fact]] ) ) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(title = "UMAP") } } if (one_plot) { return(patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)) } plots } ################################################################################

-# Builds a per-sample Hill-number scatter plot against a continuous sample
-# variable, faceted by Hill order q. Mirrors the renyi orientation used in
-# hill_curves_pq(). No smoothing/fit is added (raw points only).
-  df_hill <- vegan::renyi(t(physeq)@otu_table, scales = q, hill = TRUE)

input.old_string:
#' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter
#'   plot against the variable (one facet per Hill order `q`); the ordination
#'   and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet panels
#'   are skipped (they require discrete groups).
#' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity boxplots (via
#'   [hill_pq()]), a Venn diagram of shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an
#'   ordination and a UMAP colored by the levels.
#' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn
#'   diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays
#'   legible with many sets.
#'
#' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication
#' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()],
#' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to fine-tune each panel.
input.new_string:
#' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter
#'   plot against the variable (via [ggscatt_pq()], one panel per Hill order
#'   `q`, with the correlation statistics from \pkg{ggstatsplot}); the
#'   ordination and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet
#'   panels are skipped (they require discrete groups).
#' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity bar plots with
#'   error bars and Tukey letters (via [hill_bar_pq()]), a Venn diagram of
#'   shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an ordination and a UMAP colored by the
#'   levels.
#' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn
#'   diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays
#'   legible with many sets.
#'
#' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication
#' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()],
#' [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to
#' fine-tune each panel.

If `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} #' object assembling the panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details The `one_plot = TRUE` path requires the \pkg{patchwork} package. #' Individual panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options #' used. #' @seealso [hill_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], #' [umap_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example). #' # UMAP is disabled here (too few samples for the default n_neighbors). #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_umap = FALSE) #' } #' \dontrun{ #' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn). #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) #' #' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter + gradient-colored ordination/UMAP, #' # Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, add_venn = TRUE, add_umap = TRUE, venn_max = 4, ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", one_plot = FALSE, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." ) } if (one_plot && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required when {.arg one_plot = TRUE} in {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) } is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { if (is_numeric_fact) { plots[["alpha"]] <- .hill_scatter_pq(physeq, fact = fact, q = q) } else { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } } if (add_venn) { if (is_numeric_fact) { cli::cli_inform( "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." ) } else { n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) if (n_lev <= venn_max) { plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } else { plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } } } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } if (add_umap) { # umap::umap defaults to n_neighbors = 15, which requires > 15 samples. if (!requireNamespace("umap", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: package {.pkg umap} is not installed." ) } else if (nsamples(physeq) <= 15) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: {nsamples(physeq)} samples are too few for the default {.code n_neighbors = 15} of {.fn umap::umap}." ) } else { df_umap <- umap_pq(physeq) plots[["umap"]] <- ggplot( df_umap, aes( x = .data[["x_umap"]], y = .data[["y_umap"]], color = .data[[fact]] ) ) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(title = "UMAP") } } if (one_plot) { return(patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)) } plots } ################################################################################

Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, add_venn = TRUE, add_umap = TRUE, venn_max = 4, ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", one_plot = FALSE, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." ) } if (one_plot && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required when {.arg one_plot = TRUE} in {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) } is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { if (is_numeric_fact) { plots[["alpha"]] <- .hill_scatter_pq(physeq, fact = fact, q = q) } else { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } } if (add_venn) { if (is_numeric_fact) { cli::cli_inform( "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." ) } else { n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) if (n_lev <= venn_max) { plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } else { plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } } } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } if (add_umap) { # umap::umap defaults to n_neighbors = 15, which requires > 15 samples. if (!requireNamespace("umap", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: package {.pkg umap} is not installed." ) } else if (nsamples(physeq) <= 15) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: {nsamples(physeq)} samples are too few for the default {.code n_neighbors = 15} of {.fn umap::umap}." ) } else { df_umap <- umap_pq(physeq) plots[["umap"]] <- ggplot( df_umap, aes( x = .data[["x_umap"]], y = .data[["y_umap"]], color = .data[[fact]] ) ) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(title = "UMAP") } } if (one_plot) { return(patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)) } plots } ################################################################################
