toolUseResult.originalFile: ################################################################################ #' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Produce, in a single call, the main relevant graphical views of alpha- and #' beta-diversity for a phyloseq object as a function of **one** sample #' variable (`fact`). The set of panels adapts to the *type* of `fact`: #' #' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter #' plot against the variable (via [ggscatt_pq()], one panel per Hill order #' `q`, with the correlation statistics from \pkg{ggstatsplot}); the #' ordination and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet #' panels are skipped (they require discrete groups). #' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity bar plots with #' error bars and Tukey letters (via [hill_bar_pq()]), a Venn diagram of #' shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an ordination and a UMAP colored by the #' levels. #' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn #' diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays #' legible with many sets. #' #' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication #' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()], #' [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to #' fine-tune each panel. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of a sample variable present in the `sam_data` #' slot of `physeq`. Drives every panel. Numeric variables trigger the #' gradient/scatter behavior; other variables are treated as a factor and #' must have at least two levels. #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders #' (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) Add the alpha-diversity panel: a #' Hill-number bar plot ([hill_bar_pq()]) for a factor, or a Hill-number #' scatter ([ggscatt_pq()]) for a numeric variable. #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) Add the beta-diversity #' ordination panel. #' @param add_venn (logical, default TRUE) Add a Venn (or UpSet) panel of #' shared taxa across the levels of `fact`. Ignored when `fact` is numeric. #' @param add_umap (logical, default TRUE) Add a UMAP panel (via [umap_pq()]). #' This panel can be slow on datasets with many samples. It is skipped (with #' a message) when the \pkg{umap} package is not installed or when there are #' 15 samples or fewer (`umap` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`, which requires #' more samples than that); set to FALSE to skip it explicitly. #' @param venn_max (integer, default 4) Maximum number of levels for which a #' Venn diagram is drawn. Above this threshold an UpSet plot is used instead. #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). #' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, assemble the panels into a #' single figure with the \pkg{patchwork} package. If FALSE (default), return #' a named list (one entry per panel), letting the user arrange them freely. #' Mirrors the `one_plot` argument of [hill_pq()]. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`) when `one_plot = TRUE`. #' #' @return If `one_plot = FALSE` (default), a named list of plot objects (the #' `alpha` entry is itself a multi-panel \pkg{patchwork} figure). If #' `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} object #' assembling all panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details The alpha-diversity panel relies on \pkg{patchwork} (always) and, #' for a numeric `fact`, on \pkg{ggstatsplot} (via [ggscatt_pq()]). Other #' panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options #' used. #' @seealso [hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], #' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example). #' # UMAP is disabled here (too few samples for the default n_neighbors). #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_umap = FALSE) #' } #' \dontrun{ #' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn). #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) #' #' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter + gradient-colored ordination/UMAP, #' # Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, add_venn = TRUE, add_umap = TRUE, venn_max = 4, ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", one_plot = FALSE, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." ) } if (one_plot && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required when {.arg one_plot = TRUE} in {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) } is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { if (is_numeric_fact) { plots[["alpha"]] <- .hill_scatter_pq(physeq, fact = fact, q = q) } else { alpha_plots <- hill_pq( physeq, fact = fact, q = q, one_plot = FALSE, plot_with_tuckey = FALSE ) plots <- c(plots, alpha_plots) } } if (add_venn) { if (is_numeric_fact) { cli::cli_inform( "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." ) } else { n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) if (n_lev <= venn_max) { plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } else { plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } } } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } if (add_umap) { # umap::umap defaults to n_neighbors = 15, which requires > 15 samples. if (!requireNamespace("umap", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: package {.pkg umap} is not installed." ) } else if (nsamples(physeq) <= 15) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: {nsamples(physeq)} samples are too few for the default {.code n_neighbors = 15} of {.fn umap::umap}." ) } else { df_umap <- umap_pq(physeq) plots[["umap"]] <- ggplot( df_umap, aes( x = .data[["x_umap"]], y = .data[["y_umap"]], color = .data[[fact]] ) ) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(title = "UMAP") } } if (one_plot) { return(patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)) } plots } ################################################################################

#'   \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options
#'   used.
#' @seealso [hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()],
#'   [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]
#'
#' @examples
#' \donttest{
#' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example).
#' # UMAP is disabled here (too few samples for the default n_neighbors).
#' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
#' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
#' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
#' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini)
#' ps <- clean_pq(ps)
#' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_umap = FALSE)
#' }
#' \dontrun{
#' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn).
#' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE)
#'
#' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter + gradient-colored ordination/UMAP,
#' # Venn/UpSet automatically skipped.
#' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE)
#' }
plot_overview_pq <- function(
  physeq,
  fact,
  q = c(0, 1, 2),
  add_alpha = TRUE,
  add_ordination = TRUE,
  add_venn = TRUE,
  add_umap = TRUE,
  venn_max = 4,
  ordination_method = "PCoA",
  dist_method = "bray",
  one_plot = FALSE,
  ...

#' @examples
#' \donttest{
#' if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {
#'   # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example).
#'   # UMAP and Venn are disabled here (too few samples / extra dependency).
#'   sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
#'   hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
#'   lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
#'   ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini)
#'   ps <- clean_pq(ps)
#'   plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_venn = FALSE, add_umap = FALSE)
#' }
#' }
#' \dontrun{
#' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn).
#' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE)
#'
#' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter (ggscatt_pq) + gradient-colored
#' # ordination/UMAP, Venn/UpSet automatically skipped.
#' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE)
#' }
toolUseResult.originalFile: ################################################################################ #' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Produce, in a single call, the main relevant graphical views of alpha- and #' beta-diversity for a phyloseq object as a function of **one** sample #' variable (`fact`). The set of panels adapts to the *type* of `fact`: #' #' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter #' plot against the variable (via [ggscatt_pq()], one panel per Hill order #' `q`, with the correlation statistics from \pkg{ggstatsplot}); the #' ordination and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet #' panels are skipped (they require discrete groups). #' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity bar plots with #' error bars and Tukey letters (via [hill_bar_pq()]), a Venn diagram of #' shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an ordination and a UMAP colored by the #' levels. #' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn #' diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays #' legible with many sets. #' #' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication #' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()], #' [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to #' fine-tune each panel. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of a sample variable present in the `sam_data` #' slot of `physeq`. Drives every panel. Numeric variables trigger the #' gradient/scatter behavior; other variables are treated as a factor and #' must have at least two levels. #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders #' (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) Add the alpha-diversity panel: a #' Hill-number bar plot ([hill_bar_pq()]) for a factor, or a Hill-number #' scatter ([ggscatt_pq()]) for a numeric variable. #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) Add the beta-diversity #' ordination panel. #' @param add_venn (logical, default TRUE) Add a Venn (or UpSet) panel of #' shared taxa across the levels of `fact`. Ignored when `fact` is numeric. #' @param add_umap (logical, default TRUE) Add a UMAP panel (via [umap_pq()]). #' This panel can be slow on datasets with many samples. It is skipped (with #' a message) when the \pkg{umap} package is not installed or when there are #' 15 samples or fewer (`umap` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`, which requires #' more samples than that); set to FALSE to skip it explicitly. #' @param venn_max (integer, default 4) Maximum number of levels for which a #' Venn diagram is drawn. Above this threshold an UpSet plot is used instead. #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). #' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, assemble the panels into a #' single figure with the \pkg{patchwork} package. If FALSE (default), return #' a named list (one entry per panel), letting the user arrange them freely. #' Mirrors the `one_plot` argument of [hill_pq()]. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`) when `one_plot = TRUE`. #' #' @return If `one_plot = FALSE` (default), a named list of plot objects (the #' `alpha` entry is itself a multi-panel \pkg{patchwork} figure). If #' `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} object #' assembling all panels. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details The alpha-diversity panel relies on \pkg{patchwork} (always) and, #' for a numeric `fact`, on \pkg{ggstatsplot} (via [ggscatt_pq()]). Other #' panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options #' used. #' @seealso [hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], #' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()] #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example). #' # UMAP is disabled here (too few samples for the default n_neighbors). #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_umap = FALSE) #' } #' \dontrun{ #' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn). #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) #' #' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter + gradient-colored ordination/UMAP, #' # Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) #' } plot_overview_pq <- function( physeq, fact, q = c(0, 1, 2), add_alpha = TRUE, add_ordination = TRUE, add_venn = TRUE, add_umap = TRUE, venn_max = 4, ordination_method = "PCoA", dist_method = "bray", one_plot = FALSE, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { cli::cli_abort( "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." ) } if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { cli::cli_abort( "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." ) } # patchwork is needed to assemble the panels (one_plot = TRUE) and by the # alpha-diversity wrappers (hill_bar_pq / ggscatt_pq) for multi-panel output. if ((one_plot || add_alpha) && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required by {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." ) } # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) } is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) plots <- list() if (add_alpha) { if (is_numeric_fact) { plots[["alpha"]] <- ggscatt_pq( physeq, num_modality = fact, q = q, one_plot = TRUE, verbose = FALSE ) } else { plots[["alpha"]] <- rlang::inject( hill_bar_pq(physeq, x = !!rlang::sym(fact), q = q) ) } } if (add_venn) { if (is_numeric_fact) { cli::cli_inform( "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." ) } else { n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) if (n_lev <= venn_max) { plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } else { plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) } } } if (add_ordination) { plots[["ordination"]] <- plot_ordination_pq( physeq, method = dist_method, ordination_method = ordination_method, color = fact ) } if (add_umap) { # umap::umap defaults to n_neighbors = 15, which requires > 15 samples. if (!requireNamespace("umap", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: package {.pkg umap} is not installed." ) } else if (nsamples(physeq) <= 15) { cli::cli_inform( "The UMAP panel is skipped: {nsamples(physeq)} samples are too few for the default {.code n_neighbors = 15} of {.fn umap::umap}." ) } else { df_umap <- umap_pq(physeq) plots[["umap"]] <- ggplot( df_umap, aes( x = .data[["x_umap"]], y = .data[["y_umap"]], color = .data[[fact]] ) ) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(title = "UMAP") } } if (one_plot) { return(patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ...)) } plots } ################################################################################

file.content:
data(data_fungi_mini)

subset_pq <- function() {
  sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
  hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
  lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
  clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
}

test_that("plot_overview_pq returns a named list of ggplots by default", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_venn = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_type(res, "list")
  expect_true("ordination" %in% names(res))
  expect_true(all(vapply(res, ggplot2::is.ggplot, logical(1))))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq assembles a patchwork when one_plot = TRUE", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("patchwork")
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  p <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_venn = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE,
      one_plot = TRUE
    )
  ))
  expect_s3_class(p, "patchwork")
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq uses a Hill scatter and skips venn for numeric fact", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  expect_message(
    res <- suppressWarnings(
      plot_overview_pq(
        ps,
        fact = "Time",
        q = c(0, 1),
        add_umap = FALSE
      )
    ),
    "numeric"
  )
  expect_true("alpha" %in% names(res))
  expect_false(any(c("venn", "upset") %in% names(res)))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq draws a Venn for a factor within venn_max", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  skip_if_not_installed("ggVennDiagram")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      add_alpha = FALSE,
      add_ordination = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_true("venn" %in% names(res))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq switches to UpSet above venn_max", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  skip_if_not_installed("ComplexUpset")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  res <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    plot_overview_pq(
      ps,
      fact = "Height",
      q = c(0, 1),
      venn_max = 1,
      add_alpha = FALSE,
      add_ordination = FALSE,
      add_umap = FALSE
    )
  ))
  expect_true("upset" %in% names(res))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq skips UMAP gracefully on too-few samples", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  ps <- subset_pq()
  expect_message(
    res <- suppressWarnings(
      plot_overview_pq(
        ps,
        fact = "Height",
        q = c(0, 1),
        add_venn = FALSE
      )
    ),
    "UMAP panel is skipped"
  )
  expect_false("umap" %in% names(res))
  expect_true("ordination" %in% names(res))
})

test_that("plot_overview_pq aborts on an invalid factor", {
  skip_on_cran()
  data(data_fungi_mini)
  expect_error(
    plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "not_a_var"),
    "sam_data"
  )
})

subset_pq <- function() {

test_that("plot_overview_pq uses a Hill scatter and skips venn for numeric fact", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")

attachment.snippet: 1 ################################################################################ 2 #' Overview plot of alpha and beta diversity for a phyloseq object 3 #' 4 #' @description 5 #' 6 #' 7 #' 8 #' 9 #' Produce, in a single call, the main relevant graphical views of alpha- and 10 #' beta-diversity for a phyloseq object as a function of **one** sample 11 #' variable (`fact`). The set of panels adapts to the *type* of `fact`: 12 #' 13 #' - **Numeric variable** — alpha-diversity is shown as a Hill-numbers scatter 14 #' plot against the variable (via [ggscatt_pq()], one panel per Hill order 15 #' `q`, with the correlation statistics from \pkg{ggstatsplot}); the 16 #' ordination and UMAP points are colored by a continuous gradient. Venn/UpSet 17 #' panels are skipped (they require discrete groups). 18 #' - **Factor with 2 to `venn_max` levels** — alpha-diversity bar plots with 19 #' error bars and Tukey letters (via [hill_bar_pq()]), a Venn diagram of 20 #' shared taxa (via [ggvenn_pq()]), an ordination and a UMAP colored by the 21 #' levels. 22 #' - **Factor with more than `venn_max` levels** — same as above but the Venn 23 #' diagram is replaced by an UpSet plot (via [upset_pq()]), which stays 24 #' legible with many sets. 25 #' 26 #' This is a convenience wrapper meant for quick exploration; for publication 27 #' figures, call the dedicated functions ([hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()], 28 #' [plot_ordination_pq()], [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()]) directly to 29 #' fine-tune each panel. 30 #' 31 #' @inheritParams clean_pq 32 #' @param fact (required) Name of a sample variable present in the `sam_data` 33 #' slot of `physeq`. Drives every panel. Numeric variables trigger the 34 #' gradient/scatter behavior; other variables are treated as a factor and 35 #' must have at least two levels. 36 #' @param q (vector of integer, default `c(0, 1, 2)`) The Hill numbers orders 37 #' (q = 0 richness, q = 1 Shannon, q = 2 Simpson). 38 #' @param add_alpha (logical, default TRUE) Add the alpha-diversity panel: a 39 #' Hill-number bar plot ([hill_bar_pq()]) for a factor, or a Hill-number 40 #' scatter ([ggscatt_pq()]) for a numeric variable. 41 #' @param add_ordination (logical, default TRUE) Add the beta-diversity 42 #' ordination panel. 43 #' @param add_venn (logical, default TRUE) Add a Venn (or UpSet) panel of 44 #' shared taxa across the levels of `fact`. Ignored when `fact` is numeric. 45 #' @param add_umap (logical, default TRUE) Add a UMAP panel (via [umap_pq()]). 46 #' This panel can be slow on datasets with many samples. It is skipped (with 47 #' a message) when the \pkg{umap} package is not installed or when there are 48 #' 15 samples or fewer (`umap` defaults to `n_neighbors = 15`, which requires 49 #' more samples than that); set to FALSE to skip it explicitly. 50 #' @param venn_max (integer, default 4) Maximum number of levels for which a 51 #' Venn diagram is drawn. Above this threshold an UpSet plot is used instead. 52 #' @param ordination_method (character, default `"PCoA"`) Ordination method 53 #' passed to [plot_ordination_pq()]. `"PCoA"` is recommended as it always 54 #' converges; `"NMDS"` may fail on small or sparse datasets. 55 #' @param dist_method (character, default `"bray"`) Distance method passed to 56 #' [plot_ordination_pq()] (and ultimately to [vegan::vegdist()]). 57 #' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, assemble the panels into a 58 #' single figure with the \pkg{patchwork} package. If FALSE (default), return 59 #' a named list (one entry per panel), letting the user arrange them freely. 60 #' Mirrors the `one_plot` argument of [hill_pq()]. 61 #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [patchwork::wrap_plots()] 62 #' (e.g. `ncol`, `nrow`, `guides`) when `one_plot = TRUE`. 63 #' 64 #' @return If `one_plot = FALSE` (default), a named list of plot objects (the 65 #' `alpha` entry is itself a multi-panel \pkg{patchwork} figure). If 66 #' `one_plot = TRUE`, a single \code{\link[patchwork]{patchwork}} object 67 #' assembling all panels. 68 #' @export 69 #' @author Adrien Taudière 70 #' @details The alpha-diversity panel relies on \pkg{patchwork} (always) and, 71 #' for a numeric `fact`, on \pkg{ggstatsplot} (via [ggscatt_pq()]). Other 72 #' panels may additionally require \pkg{ggVennDiagram} (Venn), 73 #' \pkg{ComplexUpset} (UpSet) or \pkg{umap} (UMAP) depending on the options 74 #' used. 75 #' @seealso [hill_bar_pq()], [ggscatt_pq()], [plot_ordination_pq()], 76 #' [ggvenn_pq()], [upset_pq()], [umap_pq()] 77 #' 78 #' @examples 79 #' \donttest{ 80 #' if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) { 81 #' # Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two Height levels (fast example). 82 #' # UMAP and Venn are disabled here (too few samples / extra dependency). 83 #' sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini) 84 #' hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]] 85 #' lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]] 86 #' ps <- prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini) 87 #' ps <- clean_pq(ps) 88 #' plot_overview_pq(ps, fact = "Height", add_venn = FALSE, add_umap = FALSE) 89 #' } 90 #' } 91 #' \dontrun{ 92 #' # Full overview with all panels (factor with 2 levels -> Venn). 93 #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) 94 #' 95 #' # Numeric variable -> Hill scatter (ggscatt_pq) + gradient-colored 96 #' # ordination/UMAP, Venn/UpSet automatically skipped. 97 #' plot_overview_pq(data_fungi_mini, fact = "Time", add_umap = FALSE) 98 #' } 99 plot_overview_pq <- function( 100 physeq, 101 fact, 102 q = c(0, 1, 2), 103 add_alpha = TRUE, 104 add_ordination = TRUE, 105 add_venn = TRUE, 106 add_umap = TRUE, 107 venn_max = 4, 108 ordination_method = "PCoA", 109 dist_method = "bray", 110 one_plot = FALSE, 111 ... 112 ) { 113 verify_pq(physeq) 114 115 if (is.null(fact) || !fact %in% sample_variables(physeq)) { 116 cli::cli_abort( 117 "{.arg fact} must be a variable of the {.field sam_data} slot of {.arg physeq}." 118 ) 119 } 120 if (!add_alpha && !add_ordination && !add_venn && !add_umap) { 121 cli::cli_abort( 122 "At least one of {.arg add_alpha}, {.arg add_ordination}, {.arg add_venn} or {.arg add_umap} must be TRUE." 123 ) 124 } 125 # patchwork is needed to assemble the panels (one_plot = TRUE) and by the 126 # alpha-diversity wrappers (hill_bar_pq / ggscatt_pq) for multi-panel output. 127 if ( 128 (one_plot || add_alpha) && !requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE) 129 ) { 130 cli::cli_abort( 131 "Package {.pkg patchwork} is required by {.fn plot_overview_pq}. Please install it." 132 ) 133 } 134 135 # Drop samples with a missing modality so every panel sees the same samples. 136 if (anyNA(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { 137 physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) 138 } 139 140 is_numeric_fact <- is.numeric(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) 141 plots <- list() 142 143 if (add_alpha) { 144 if (is_numeric_fact) { 145 plots[["alpha"]] <- ggscatt_pq( 146 physeq, 147 num_modality = fact, 148 q = q, 149 one_plot = TRUE, 150 verbose = FALSE 151 ) 152 } else { 153 plots[["alpha"]] <- rlang::inject( 154 hill_bar_pq(physeq, x = !!rlang::sym(fact), q = q) 155 ) 156 } 157 } 158 159 if (add_venn) { 160 if (is_numeric_fact) { 161 cli::cli_inform( 162 "{.arg fact} is numeric: the Venn/UpSet panel is skipped (it requires discrete groups)." 163 ) 164 } else { 165 n_lev <- nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) 166 if (n_lev <= venn_max) { 167 plots[["venn"]] <- ggvenn_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) 168 } else { 169 plots[["upset"]] <- upset_pq(physeq, fact = fact, verbose = FALSE) 170 } 171 } 172 } 173 ...   need discrete groups).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
title: "MiscMetabar — Overview, volcano & MDT-CSV (dev 0.16.8.9000)"
author: "Adrien Taudière"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: show
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
library(ggplot2)
data(data_fungi_mini)

# Balanced 6-sample subset spanning two `Height` levels, so every chunk runs
# fast (data_fungi_mini has 137 samples).
sn <- sample_names(data_fungi_mini)
hi <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "High")[1:3]]
lo <- sn[which(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height == "Low")[1:3]]
dfm <- clean_pq(prune_samples(c(hi, lo), data_fungi_mini))
```

This document illustrates three features added to **MiscMetabar** during the
`0.16.8.9000` development cycle, all drawn from the *pqverse* `ROADMAP.md`
(`MiscMetabar` section):

| Theme | Function | Roadmap tag |
|---|---|---|
| Visualisation | `plot_volcano_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Visualisation | `plot_overview_pq()` | Medium / easy |
| Data I/O | `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` | Medium / easy |

All per-sample examples use a 6-sample subset of the bundled
`data_fungi_mini` dataset (`dfm`).

---

# 1. `plot_volcano_pq()` — volcano plot for differential abundance

## What it does

A volcano plot puts the (log2) **fold change** on the x-axis and the
**statistical significance** (\(-\log_{10}\) of the adjusted p-value) on the
y-axis, so the most interesting taxa — large effect *and* strong support —
sit in the top corners. `plot_volcano_pq()` is column-name agnostic: point
`fc =` and `padj =` at the relevant columns of any differential-abundance
result table (`DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()`, `aldex_pq()`, …).

```{r volcano-synthetic}
set.seed(42)
res <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = rnorm(300, sd = 2),
  padj = runif(300)^3,
  Genus = sample(paste0("Genus_", 1:25), 300, replace = TRUE)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res)
```

Taxa are classified `Up` / `Down` / `NotDA` from the `alpha` (significance)
and `lfc_threshold` (effect-size) cut-offs, shown as dashed guides. We can
tighten the effect-size threshold and label the top significant taxa:

```{r volcano-labelled}
plot_volcano_pq(
  res,
  lfc_threshold = 2,
  label_col = "Genus",
  label_n = 6
)
```

Edge cases are handled: `padj == 0` is capped just above the largest finite
\(-\log_{10}\) value, and a missing `padj` is treated as `NotDA`.

```{r volcano-edge}
res_edge <- data.frame(
  log2FoldChange = c(4, -4, 0.1, 2),
  padj = c(0, 1e-12, NA, 0.001)
)
plot_volcano_pq(res_edge)
```

For a real analysis the input is simply the result object, e.g.
`plot_volcano_pq(DESeq2::results(dds))` — see `?plot_volcano_pq` for the
`DESeq2` / `ANCOMBC` / `ALDEx2` column mappings.

---

# 2. `plot_overview_pq()` — one-call diversity overview, adapted to the variable

## What it does

`plot_overview_pq()` assembles, in a single call, the main alpha- and
beta-diversity views for **one** sample variable. Crucially, the panel set
adapts to the *type* of that variable:

- **Factor** → Hill-number **boxplots** (`hill_pq()`) + a **Venn** diagram of
  shared taxa (`ggvenn_pq()`, switched to an **UpSet** plot above `venn_max`
  levels) + an ordination + a UMAP, all keyed on the levels.
- **Numeric** → a Hill-number **scatter** against the variable (one facet per
  `q`) + gradient-colored ordination and UMAP. Venn/UpSet are skipped (they
  need discrete groups).

By default the panels come back as a **named list** of ggplots; pass
`one_plot = TRUE` to assemble them with **patchwork**.

A factor variable (`Height`, two levels here → Venn). UMAP is disabled because
this 6-sample subset has fewer samples than `umap`'s default `n_neighbors`:

```{r overview-factor}
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 8
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Height", q = c(0, 1), add_umap = FALSE, one_plot = TRUE)
```

The ordination defaults to `PCoA` (which always converges, unlike `NMDS` on
small/sparse data) on a Bray-Curtis distance; both are tunable via
`ordination_method` and `dist_method`.

A numeric variable (`Time`) switches the alpha panel to a Hill scatter and
drops the Venn panel automatically:

```{r overview-numeric}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
plot_overview_pq(dfm, fact = "Time", q = c(0, 1), add_umap = FALSE, one_plot = TRUE)
```

Any subset of panels can be requested with the `add_*` flags, and when
`one_plot = TRUE` the usual patchwork layout controls flow through `...`
(e.g. `ncol`, `guides`). With UMAP enabled on a larger dataset the call is
simply `plot_overview_pq(physeq, fact = "Height")`.

---

# 3. `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` — GBIF MDT template export (CSV/TSV)

## What it does

`phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` writes a phyloseq object to the plain-text
(CSV/TSV) version of the GBIF
[Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT)](https://mdt.gbif.org/) template — the
counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`. It produces **one file per table**
(OTU table, taxonomy, samples), each keyed by a leading `id` column, with the
OTU table in MDT orientation: **OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns**.

```{r mdt-csv}
out_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_demo")
files <- phyloseq_to_MDT_csv(dfm, path = out_dir, check_dwc = FALSE)
files
```

The OTU table has one row per taxon and one column per sample (plus `id`):

```{r mdt-csv-peek}
otu <- utils::read.csv(files[["OTU_table"]], check.names = FALSE)
otu[1:5, 1:4]
c(n_rows = nrow(otu), n_taxa = ntaxa(dfm),
  n_cols_minus_id = ncol(otu) - 1L, n_samples = nsamples(dfm))
```

Pass `sep = "\t"` for tab-separated `.tsv` files (the format favored by the
MDT validator), and keep the default `check_dwc = TRUE` to be warned about
recommended Darwin Core sample terms (`decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`,
`eventDate`) missing from `sample_data`.

```{r mdt-cleanup}
#| include: false
unlink(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
```

---

# Session info

```{r session}
sessionInfo()
```

lines: * `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.

* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.

* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.

-* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number boxplots plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. +* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter via `ggscatt_pq()` for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number bar plots via `hill_bar_pq()` plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.

* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant.

* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle, caption and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`.

attachment.snippet:
1	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
2	* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
3	* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.
4	* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
5	* `hill_bar_pq()` now sets the "Error bars / Tukey HSD" caption once for the whole figure (via `patchwork::plot_annotation()`) instead of repeating it on every `q` panel, fixing the overlapping captions seen with several values of `q`. Panels where Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons were not run (no global Kruskal-Wallis significance) are flagged with a `sig_symbol` (default `"∅"`, the empty set, new argument) appended to their subtitle and explained once in the shared caption.
6	* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.
7	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
8	* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
9	* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter via `ggscatt_pq()` for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number bar plots via `hill_bar_pq()` plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.
10	* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant.
11	* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
12	* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle, caption and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`. It gains dedicated `width_caption` and `fontsize_caption` arguments to control the caption wrapping width and font size.
13	* `tax_bar_pq()` gains a `ribbon_hide_zero` argument (default `TRUE`) that, when `add_ribbon = TRUE`, suppresses the ribbon of a taxon between two adjacent bars whenever its value is zero in either connected bar, avoiding ribbons that collapse to a flat line.
14	
15	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
16	* Further check-time reductions for CRAN compliance:
17	  extra examples `\dontrun{}` (kept for documentation,
18	  not run during checks). 
19	* Added file-level `skip_on_cran()` to some heavy test files.
20	* Example speed-ups for some functions.
21	* Fix several bugs when using Windows paths by quoting system call arguments with `shQuote()`
22	
23	
24	# MiscMetabar 0.16.6 
25	* `verify_tax_table()` is now ~10× faster on full-size taxonomy tables.
26	* `divent_hill_matrix_pq()` no longer recomputes the per-sample
27	  positive-subset (`x <- x[x > 0]`) once per Hill order. The loop is
28	  now sample-outer / q-inner, so each row is sliced once. Numeric
29	  output is bitwise-identical. Speeds up every Hill-diversity
30	  computation in the package: `hill_pq()`, `hill_bar_pq()`,
31	  `hill_tuckey_pq()`, `profile_hill_pq()`, `psmelt_samples_pq()`,
32	  `plot_refseq_extremity_pq()`, and the `*_rarperm_pq` family.
33	* `circle_pq()` replaces a nested
34	  `pbapply(., 2, pbtapply(., group, sum))` over the OTU table with
35	  two `rowsum()` calls. On `data_fungi` (1420 taxa × 185 samples)
36	  the example dropped from ~18 s to ~1.8 s (≈ 10× faster). Output
37	  unchanged.
38	* `format2dada2(fasta_db = …)`, `hill_acc_pq(type = "sample")`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()` are also faster.
39	* New pkgdown article: `vignettes/articles/timing.Rmd` documents
40	  wall-clock cost of the main functions on `data_fungi` and
41	  `data_fungi_mini`, with a CSV refreshed by `inst/benchmark/function_timings.R`.
42	* Pkgdown articles use fewer permutations / simulations to keep the site build under a few minutes.
43	* Reduced `R CMD check` time to keep CRAN's 10-minute budget. Examples for
44	  `verify_tax_table()`, `adonis_pq()`, `plot_SCBD_pq()`, `multipatt_pq()`,
45	  `hill_pq()`, `plot_tsne_pq()`, `upset_test_pq()`, `summary_plot_pq()`,
46	  `ggvenn_pq()`, `plot_refseq_pq()`, `plot_seq_ratio_pq()`,
47	  `plot_refseq_extremity_pq()`, `glmutli_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`,
48	  `lefser_pq()`, `var_par_pq()`, `var_par_rarperm_pq()`,
49	  `taxa_only_in_one_level()`, `distri_1_taxa()`, `accu_plot_balanced_modality()`,
50	  `multi_biplot_pq()`, `tax_bar_pq()`, `plot_var_part_pq()`, `track_wkflow()`,
51	  `reorder_taxa_pq()` and `transform_pq()` now use `data_fungi_mini`
52	  (137 × 45) instead of the full `data_fungi` (185 × 1420), keeping behaviour
53	  identical but much faster.
54	* `hill_acc_pq()`, `iNEXT_pq()`, `format2dada2()` and `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`
55	  examples moved from `\donttest{}` to `\dontrun{}`. These functions are
56	  inherently CPU-bound (sample-based accumulation, fasta reformatting,
57	  permutation × rarefaction × q-loop) and were the largest individual
58	  contributors to the 10-min CRAN budget. Their behaviour is documented in
59	  the corresponding vignettes.
60	* `verify_pq()` example switched from `data_fungi` to `data_fungi_mini`
61	  (82 s → < 5 s).
62	* Tests: `plot_LCBD_pq()` / `LCBD_pq()` smoke tests in
63	  `tests/testthat/test_figures_beta_div.R` lowered `nperm` from 100 to 9
64	  (they only assert return class, not numeric stability).
65	* Function defaults (`nperm`, `n_permutations`) are unchanged.
66	
67	# MiscMetabar 0.16.5 
68	* `funguild_assign()` and `rotl_pq()` examples now use `\dontrun{}` instead of
69	  `\donttest{}`. Both examples call external APIs (`www.stbates.org` and the
70	  Open Tree of Life respectively) that are not always reachable during CRAN's
71	  `--run-donttest` check, causing spurious ERRORs.
72	* `verify_tax_table()`'s introductory example was moved inside the existing
73	  `\donttest{}` block. The call against the full `data_fungi` dataset took
74	  ~70 s, which triggered the CRAN "examples > 5 s" NOTE on every check.
75	* `XVector` removed from `DESCRIPTION` `Imports`. It was declared but never
76	  imported in `NAMESPACE` or used directly; `Biostrings` already loads it
77	  transitively. CRAN flagged this as "Namespace in Imports field not imported

lastPrompt: in plot_overview I prefer for alpha diversity the use of function hill_bar_pq for factor and MiscMetabar::ggscatt_pq() for numeric modality.

content.text:
Done. Swapped the alpha-diversity delegates as you asked:

- **Factor** → `hill_bar_pq(physeq, x = <fact>, q = q)` — Hill-number **bar plots** with error bars and Tukey letters (injected via `rlang::inject(... !!rlang::sym(fact) ...)` since `hill_bar_pq()` captures `x` with `ensym`).
- **Numeric** → `ggscatt_pq(physeq, num_modality = fact, q = q)` — Hill scatter with `ggstatsplot` correlation stats.
- Removed the now-redundant `.hill_scatter_pq()` helper.

Consequences handled:
- The `alpha` entry is now a **single composite panel** (both wrappers assemble their per-`q` panels with patchwork), so the named-list output has one `alpha` entry rather than `plot_Hill_*` entries.
- **patchwork** is now required whenever `add_alpha = TRUE` (guard updated); **ggstatsplot** is required for the numeric branch (documented in `@details`, tests guard with `skip_if_not_installed`).
- UMAP graceful-skip and the numeric→Venn-skip logic are unchanged.

Verified:
- **13 tests pass** (factor `hill_bar_pq`, numeric `ggscatt_pq`, venn↔upset, umap skip, invalid factor).
- `one_plot = TRUE` correctly nests both wrappers' patchworks inside `wrap_plots` (built end-to-end).
- Docs regenerated, `air format` clean, spelling clean, NEWS bullet + demo prose updated, demo **re-rendered** (exit 0).

R CMD check still not run — `/r-check` when you want the full CRAN pass. The quality/diagnostic companion function remains parked for the next pass whenever you want to design it (still need to know how controls are identified).
